business_rules.conditions: Applies to Properties securing Freddie Mac Mortgages under Chapter 8
business_rules.consequences: If violated, the Property/Mortgage may be noncompliant and subject to rejection/servicing action
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8, Section 8.2(a) Structure type and habitability (06/29/18)
source_reference.source_text: The Borrower must not participate in home sharing activities, which are defined as short -term rentals (typically less than one month) that are marketed through a peer -to-peer online marketplace or a home sharing platform, nor enter into leases, including master leases, of residential, corporate or commercial units that the Borrower knows or should have known are intended to be used by the tenants for full or part -time home sharing activities. For clarity, nonexclusive examples of home sharing platforms include Airbnb, VRBO, and booking.com.

examples: A borrower allowing 2-week rentals advertised on Airbnb violates the home sharing prohibition.
A borrower signing a master lease to a corporate tenant known to sublet units on VRBO violates the prohibition.
A borrower operating only 12-month leases with no short-term marketing on peer-to-peer platforms complies.
200-unit garden-style apartment community in Texas
Mixed-use property with residential units above retail space
Affordable housing property with regulatory restrictions

business_rules.rule_name: Late charge waiver barred for ARM or SBL Hybrid ARM 43.7
business_rules.description: A Servicer may not waive collection of any late charge payable under the Note for an ARM or an SBL Hybrid ARM.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Borrower requests late charge waiver on ARM/SBL Hybrid ARM
business_rules.consequences: If ARM/SBL Hybrid ARM, waiver is not permitted; late charge must be collected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must document the waiver and retain evidence of the waiver in the Mortgage File. The Servicer may not waive collection of any late charge payable under the Note for an ARM, or an SBL Hybrid ARM.

examples: Borrower on ARM requests waiver due to administrative error → Servicer must deny; waiver prohibited.
Borrower on SBL Hybrid ARM is 7 days late → late charge cannot be waived even if this is first request.
Servicer issues waiver on ARM anyway → noncompliance; must reverse and collect late charge.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing corrective action and potential reimbursement to Freddie Mac

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Servicer cannot modify loan terms and REMIC actions require counsel opinion
business_rules.description: The Servicer must not modify, waive, or release any term of any Note, Security Instrument, or other Loan Document; accept any prepayment; or consent to postponement of Borrower performance except as authorized by the Purchase and Servicing Documents. For Mortgages included in a REMIC securitization, the Servicer must not knowingly or intentionally take (or fail to take) any action that adversely affects REMIC status or results in REMIC tax under IRC 860F(a) or 860G(d); unless waived by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must deliver an acceptable counsel opinion at no expense to Freddie Mac confirming no adverse REMIC impact and no REMIC tax.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to all Servicers; additional requirements when Mortgage is in a REMIC
business_rules.consequences: Unauthorized action is prohibited and may trigger servicing remedies and legal/tax exposure
business_rules.exceptions: Actions authorized by Purchase and Servicing Documents or waived by Freddie Mac

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 43 – Specific Servicing Responsibilities; 43.14(a) Limits on Servicer
source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must not modify, waive or release any term of any Note, Security Instrument or other Loan Document, accept any prepayment, or consent to any postponement of performance by any Borrower of any obligation under a Note, Security Instrument or other Loan Document except as authorized by the Purchase and Servicing Documents. If a Mortgage is included in a REMIC Securitization, the Servicer will not knowingly or intentionally take any action ... that (i) adversely affects or impairs the status of the REMIC trust as a REMIC or (ii) results in the REMIC trust incur ring a tax under Section 860F (a) or Section 860G(d) ... unless waived by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must deliver an opinion of counsel

examples: Servicer agrees to waive a late fee without delegated authority; this is a prohibited waiver unless authorized by the Purchase and Servicing Documents.
Mortgage is in a REMIC and servicer proposes a modification; servicer must obtain (unless waived) counsel opinion confirming no REMIC status impairment and no 860F/860G tax.
Borrower requests postponed performance on a covenant; servicer cannot consent unless the servicing documents expressly authorize it or Freddie Mac grants approval.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Strong prohibition language; includes specific Internal Revenue Code sections and waiver concept; limited numeric content.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing transfer, indemnification, or other remedies under servicing docs

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: No Late Charge If Payment Received Within 10 Days
business_rules.description: For Mortgages (other than TAH bond credit enhancement Mortgages) originated prior to July 8, 1992, the Servicer may not impose any late charge for any monthly installment received within 10 days after the payment is due. If the grace period ends on a weekend or holiday, the grace period is extended to the next Business Day.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to pre-1992-07-08 mortgages when imposing late charges
business_rules.consequences: If complied, late charges are enforceable; if violated, late fee must not be charged/collected
business_rules.exceptions: Grace period extends to next Business Day if ending on weekend/holiday

source_reference.source_text: • May not impose any late charge for any monthly installment received within 10 days after the payment is due (If the grace period ends on a weekend or holiday, it is extended to the next Business Day.)

examples: Pre-1992 loan payment is received 8 days after due date; Servicer must not impose a late charge.
Due date is Friday; the 10th day falls on a Sunday, so grace extends to Monday—payment received Monday is within the grace period and no late charge is allowed.
Payment received 12 days after due date (no weekend extension applies); Servicer may impose a late charge if allowed by Note and other limits.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: No Collection by Reserves Deductions or Principal Add-On
business_rules.description: For all Mortgages, regardless of origination date or any contrary Loan Document provision, the Servicer may not collect due and unpaid late charges or default interest by (i) charging the Borrower's Reserves, (ii) deducting from a regular payment of the monthly installment, (iii) deducting from a payment made to partially or fully cure a Delinquency, or (iv) adding to the outstanding principal balance of the Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when attempting to collect unpaid late charges or default interest
business_rules.consequences: If prohibited methods used, transaction must be reversed/corrected and collections re-applied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated for these prohibited methods

source_reference.source_text: For all Mortgages, regardless of the date of origination or any provision to the contrary in the Loan Documents, the Servicer may not use any of the following methods to collect due and unpaid late charges or default interest: • Charging the Borrower's Reserves • Deducting from a regular payment of the monthly installment • Deducting from a payment made to partially or fully cure a Delinquency • Adding to the outstanding principal balance of the Mortgage

examples: Servicer uses the Tax & Insurance Reserve to pay an unpaid late charge; this is prohibited and must be corrected.
Borrower makes a delinquency cure payment; Servicer withholds $500 from the cure to satisfy old late fees—prohibited deduction method.
Servicer capitalizes default interest by adding it to principal balance; prohibited and must be reversed.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing corrective action / reapplication of funds

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Securitized mortgages cannot add subordinate financing except supplemental product
business_rules.description: For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing except Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product. If prohibited subordinate financing is requested or present, consent will be denied and Freddie Mac may reject the request and/or require remediation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when borrower requests subordinate financing on a securitized mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If securitized: request prohibited unless supplemental product; otherwise request denied
business_rules.exceptions: Subordinate financing allowed only if it is a supplemental mortgage under the product

source_reference.source_text: For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing other than Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product.

43.29 Subordinate Financing for non -SBL Mortgages (04/18/24) a. Conditions for Freddie Mac approval of Subordinate Financing for a non -SBL Mortgage (06/30/16) For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing other than Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product.

examples: Securitized Mortgage + proposed new subordinate lien → not permitted unless it is a Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage.
If the subordinate financing is not the Supplemental Mortgage Product, Freddie Mac will deny the request.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules prohibit Subordinate Financing on Mortgages purchased for Securitization except Subordinate Financing under the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product. The prohibition scope and exception are identical; consequences align (request prohibited/denied; Freddie will not permit subordinate financing).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 selected as primary: slightly clearer operational framing (borrower request for subordinate financing on a securitized mortgage) while retaining identical prohibition.

business_rules.rule_name: Subordinate Financing Fees: 50% Remit and $500 Counsel Fee
business_rules.description: When submitting a subordinate financing approval request package, the Servicer must handle fees as follows: (i) for the nonrefundable review/processing fee, the Servicer may retain 50% and must remit the remaining 50% to Freddie Mac when submitting the application; and (ii) the Servicer must charge the Borrower a nonrefundable counsel fee of $500 (which may exceed $500 for complex transactions). The Servicer may not charge the Borrower any additional fees for processing a request for Subordinate Financing.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Borrower requests Freddie Mac approval of subordinate financing
business_rules.consequences: If violated, fee submission may be rejected and noncompliance fees may be assessed
business_rules.exceptions: $500 counsel fee may exceed $500 for complex transactions

source_reference.source_text: 1. A nonrefundable review/processing fee in the amount specified in the Loan Documents, or, if no amount is specified, in the amount set forth in Exhibit 10 (The Servicer may retain 50 percent of the review/processing fee and must remit the remaining 50 percent to Freddie Mac when submitting the application for the Subordinate Financing. The Servicer must remit the fee by wire transfer ... The Servicer may not charge the Borrower any additional fees for processing a request for Subordinate Financing.) 2. The Servicer's charge to the Borrower of a nonrefundable counsel fee of $500 (The counsel fee is for the legal fees, expenses and costs of Freddie Mac's legal counsel, including its in -house counsel, and may exceed $500 for complex transactions.

examples: A $2,000 processing fee is collected: Servicer may retain $1,000 and must remit $1,000 to Freddie Mac with the application.
Servicer charges the Borrower $500 counsel fee and no other processing fee beyond the review/processing fee; compliant.
Servicer charges an extra $250 ‘administrative fee’ to the Borrower for the request; prohibited as an additional processing fee.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 defines a key consent gate for Freddie Mac review. BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_028_006 then prescribes how the Servicer must package and remit fees during submission in that same review pathway.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Failure to meet the gate but proceeding to fee submission creates borrower harm and process waste; meeting the gate but violating fee handling risks package rejection and fee-related compliance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Borrower and manager cannot select units for inspection
business_rules.description: On the day of inspection, unit selection authority depends on Freddie Mac presence: if Freddie Mac is on-site and participating, Freddie Mac identifies an appropriate sample of units to inspect from the selected units; if Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller selects units to be inspected. Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.
business_rules.conditions: Applies on inspection day when selecting units to be inspected
business_rules.consequences: If followed, selection independence maintained; if violated, inspection selection is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 8.15(b)(3) Unit selection on day of inspection
source_reference.source_text: 3. On the day of the inspection, if Freddie Mac is on-site and participating in the inspection, Freddie Mac will identify an appropriate sample of units to inspect from the selected units, ensuring that the required number of units are inspected... If Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller will select units to be inspected. Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.

examples: Freddie Mac attends the inspection: Freddie Mac chooses the inspected units from the preselected list; Borrower does not influence which units are inspected.
Freddie Mac is not present: Seller chooses inspected units; property manager suggests 'only renovated units'—Seller must reject the suggestion to comply.
Borrower provides a preferred unit list and insists those be inspected: violates the 'may not select or recommend' prohibition.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition and clear conditional selection authority.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004’s control ('Freddie Mac identifies from the selected units' / 'Seller selects') presupposes that an eligible pool of selected units exists, and that tenant notification/list delivery occurred as required.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the pre-inspection selection/notification/list is not performed, the on-day selection process cannot be compliant; inspections may be rescheduled, sampling validity questioned, and underwriting delayed.

business_rules.rule_name: All required changes must be made by modification exhibit and body text must not be altered except rider redlines
business_rules.description: The Seller must make all authorized and required changes on the appropriate modification exhibit and must mark the applicable box on the document to indicate the exhibit is attached. The Seller may not make any changes to the Loan Documents by altering the text of the body of the document. The only stated exception is that modifications may be made directly to the text of the body of Riders to Loan Documents if clearly identified by showing stricken-through (deleted) text and underlined (new) text.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer makes any changes to Freddie Mac loan documents
business_rules.consequences: If followed, changes are properly documented; if violated, documents may be rejected and must be re-executed/cured before purchase
business_rules.exceptions: Riders may be edited with visible strikethrough/underline redlines

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Completion of documents (10/01/25), Method for making changes
source_reference.source_text: The Seller must make all authorized and required changes on the appropriate modification exhibit to the document, and must mark the applicable box on the document to indicate such an exhibit is attached. The Seller may not make any changes to the Loan Documents by altering the text of the body of the document, except that modifications may be made directly to the text of the body of Riders to Loan Documents so long as such modifications are clearly identified by intentionally showing stricken-through (deleted) text and underlined (new) text.

examples: Seller adds a required program change by completing a modification exhibit and checks the exhibit-attachment box; acceptable.
Seller edits the Loan Agreement body paragraph directly (no exhibit); prohibited and the document must be corrected and re-executed.
Seller modifies a Rider by striking deleted text and underlining new text; permitted if clearly identified.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition with a specific exception for riders; non-numeric rule.

examples: Closing package assembled with executed note and recorded security instrument
Closing package flagged delivery-ready after final title and insurance evidence received
Closing package returned for correction due to missing signature/acknowledgment

dependencies.rationale: A closing package can be defective either by incomplete/incorrect blanks (005_003) or by impermissible text alterations outside approved modification exhibits (005_004). These controls are complementary quality gates that together ensure documents are in Freddie-acceptable form.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If blanks are not completed correctly or modifications are made improperly, documents may be rejected, requiring cure, re-execution, and potentially delaying funding/purchase.

business_rules.rule_name: No purchase unless warehouse assignment effective by funding date
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the Seller has assigned the Mortgage to the warehouse lender unless the warehouse lender assigns the Mortgage to the Seller effective on or before the Freddie Mac Funding Date. If the assignment to the warehouse lender was recorded, the Seller must record the assignment back to the Seller after the Freddie Mac Funding Date; if not recorded, return of the unrecorded assignment satisfies the requirement.
business_rules.conditions: Pledged Mortgage delivery where Seller previously assigned Mortgage to warehouse lender
business_rules.consequences: If unmet, Freddie Mac will not purchase; if met, purchase may proceed subject to other requirements
business_rules.exceptions: If initial warehouse assignment unrecorded, return of assignment satisfies section

source_reference.source_text:
Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the Seller has assigned the Mortgage to the
warehouse lender unless the warehouse lender assigns the Mortgage to the Seller effective on or
before the Freddie Mac Fu nding Date of the pledged Mortgage.

• If the assignment of the pledged Mortgage to the warehouse lender was recorded in the public
records of any jurisdiction, the Seller must record the assignment to the Seller in the public
records of the appropriate juri sdiction after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.

• If the assignment of the pledged Mortgage to the warehouse lender was not recorded, the
return of that unrecorded assignment to the Seller will satisfy the requirements of this section.

examples: Warehouse lender’s assignment back to Seller is effective one day before Funding Date: eligible for purchase; Seller records the assignment after Funding Date if the prior assignment was recorded.
Warehouse lender’s assignment back is effective two days after Funding Date: Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage.
Warehouse assignment was never recorded; lender returns the unrecorded assignment to Seller: requirement is satisfied without recording.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

business_rules.rule_name: No purchase unless UCC termination filed after funding date
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the warehouse lender has filed a financing statement relating to the pledged Mortgage unless the warehouse lender executes a termination statement. The Seller must file the termination statement in the appropriate public records after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.
business_rules.conditions: Pledged Mortgage where warehouse lender filed a financing statement in public records
business_rules.consequences: If termination not executed/filed, Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text:
Freddie Mac will not pu rchase a pledged Mortgage if the warehouse lender has filed a financing
statement relating to the pledged Mortgage in the public records of any jurisdiction unless the
warehouse lender executes a termination statement.  The Seller must file the termination
statement in the public records of the appropriate jurisdiction after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.

examples: Warehouse lender filed a UCC-1 against the pledged Mortgage; lender signs termination and Seller files it after Funding Date: purchase may proceed.
Seller cannot obtain lender-signed termination statement: Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage.
Termination statement signed but not filed in the appropriate jurisdiction: treated as noncompliant and purchase is blocked.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: The prohibition rule requires a termination statement if the warehouse lender filed a financing statement. The UCC search rule requires searching every filing office under Revised Article 9 and explaining financing statements found to counsel prior to origination. In practice, you cannot reliably comply with the financing-statement termination prohibition without the upstream UCC search control to detect and document the filing.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCC searches are not performed comprehensively, a warehouse financing statement may remain undiscovered and un-terminated, leading Freddie to refuse purchase of the pledged Mortgage and forcing last-minute cures/recording actions after Funding Date.

business_rules.rule_name: Cross-Collateralization Prohibited Unless Other Mortgage Purchased Simultaneously
business_rules.description: A mortgage must not be cross-collateralized and cross-defaulted with any other mortgage that is not being simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac for the purpose of meeting the non-Independent Property requirements. If the other mortgage is not simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac, the cross-collateralization/cross-default structure cannot be used to satisfy the subsection requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when structuring cross-collateralization to meet Section 8.6 requirements
business_rules.consequences: If violated, mortgage structure is impermissible and purchase eligibility may fail
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 8 – e. Additional requirements; prohibition sentence (04/13/23)
source_reference.source_text: The Property and the property on which the Essential Facilities, Recreational Facilities, and/or direct access are located must each meet all of the following requirements: • The Property and the property... must be owned and controlled by substantially the same Key Borrower Principals • The Property and the property... must together meet the requirements for an Independent Property... A Mortgage cannot be cross -collateralized and cross -defaulted with any other mortgage not being simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac in order to meet the requirements set forth in this subsection .

examples: Seller proposes cross-collateralizing a Freddie Mac delivery with an existing bank loan on the adjacent parcel; since the bank loan is not being simultaneously purchased, this is prohibited.
Two mortgages are delivered and purchased by Freddie Mac at the same time and are cross-collateralized/cross-defaulted; structure can be used to meet the subsection requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit prohibition; no numeric thresholds.

examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

business_rules.rule_name: No Purchase If Borrower Principal or Manager Is on FHFA SCP List
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage and will not allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership (under Chapters 41 or 41SBL), or approve any other servicing-related transaction, if any Borrower, Borrower Principal, or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) List, subject to conditions and/or exclusions in the Named Party’s final suspension order.
business_rules.conditions: When any Borrower, principal, or property manager matches FHFA SCP Named Party
business_rules.consequences: If no match: transaction may proceed; if match: purchase/approval is denied
business_rules.exceptions: Subject to conditions/exclusions in final suspension order

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage nor allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership under Chapters 41 or 41SBL, or approve any other Servicing -related transaction, in which any Borrower, Borrower Principal or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP List, subject to any conditions and/or exclusions set forth in each Named Party’s final suspension order.

examples: Borrower principal appears on FHFA SCP List: Freddie Mac denies purchase of the delivered Mortgage.
Property management company is a Named Party: Freddie Mac will not approve a Transfer of Ownership request.
Named Party order includes an exclusion permitting limited activity: decision follows the conditions/exclusions in that final order.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 prohibits purchase/servicing transactions if any borrower/principal/property manager is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP list (subject to final order conditions). BR_RETAINS_RECORDS_UNDER_DOCUMENTATION_021_006 requires retaining evidence of screening against restricted party lists including SCP (with screenshots/copies). In practice, evidence retention is the control that validates the organization complied with the screening obligation that supports the prohibition decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_RETAINS_RECORDS_UNDER_DOCUMENTATION_021_006 fails (no evidence), the Servicer may still have made correct decisions, but cannot demonstrate compliance. This increases findings risk, corrective action likelihood, and weakens defensibility if a prohibited counterparty is later discovered.

dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 requires using the most current Exclusionary List and makes a Mortgage ineligible if a listed party played certain roles in origination/transfer/real estate transaction (subject to an exception). BR_035_002 prohibits purchase/Transfer of Ownership/servicing approvals if Borrower/principal/property manager is a Named Party on FHFA SCP (subject to final suspension order conditions/exclusions). These are complementary negative screens that collectively define counterparty eligibility constraints; both are needed for a complete compliance determination.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_018_009 is followed but BR_035_002 is not (or vice versa): (1) Freddie Mac may later deny purchase/approval or require unwind actions, (2) Seller/Servicer may incur repurchase/indemnification exposure or operational losses, and (3) reputational and compliance findings risk increases due to inadequate restricted-party screening.

dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 covers multiple lists (OFAC SDN, OFAC Consolidated, FHFA Suspended Counterparty List subject to conditions, Freddie Mac Exclusionary List) for borrower/principal/guarantor. BR_035_002 specifically addresses FHFA SCP Named Party status and adds property management company as a covered party. Operationally, these rules should be navigated together during party screening to avoid gaps (e.g., management company SCP exposure not covered by BR_007_003).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 screening is performed without BR_035_002: lender may miss SCP exposure tied to the property management company, resulting in late-stage purchase denial and rework to replace management.

business_rules.rule_name: CGL Must Be Occurrence Based and No Assault Exclusions
business_rules.description: Standard commercial general liability (CGL) insurance must be provided on an Occurrence-based policy form. The liability policy must be written with no exclusions for claims related to assault and battery, molestation, sexual abuse, animal attacks, or firearms. If the Borrower changes from a Claims Made policy form to an Occurrence-based policy form, a Supplemental Extended Reporting Period (Tail) endorsement must be obtained to prevent a gap in coverage.
business_rules.conditions: When maintaining CGL coverage for a Freddie Mac-financed property
business_rules.consequences: If occurrence-based with no prohibited exclusions, compliant; if exclusions exist or form is wrong, noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Tail endorsement requirement applies only when changing from Claims Made to Occurrence-based

source_reference.source_text: Standard commercial general liability (CGL) insurance is required to insure against liability resulting from bodily injury, property damage, personal injury, advertising injury and contractual liability. The coverage must be provided on an Occurrence-based Policy Form. ... The liability policy must be written with no exclusions for claims related to assault and battery, molestation, sexual abuse, animal attacks, or firearms. If the Borrower changes from a Claims Made Policy Form to an Occurrence-based Policy Form, a Supplemental Extended Reporting Period (also known as a Tail) endorsement must be obtained to prevent a gap in coverage.

examples: CGL policy is occurrence-based and includes assault and battery coverage without exclusions; requirement satisfied
CGL policy excludes firearm-related claims; violates the no-exclusion requirement and must be corrected or replaced
Borrower moves from claims-made to occurrence-based but does not purchase a Tail endorsement; creates a coverage gap and fails requirement
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery condition / servicing compliance action

examples: Hazard policy covers the property with coverage amount meeting minimum requirements
Renewal policy replaces expiring policy without coverage lapse
Policy endorsement updates loss payee language after servicing transfer

business_rules.rule_name: Seller must deliver records within Fannie Mae deadline
business_rules.description: When Fannie Mae issues a written request to examine mortgage records, the seller/servicer must deliver all requested records to Fannie Mae (or its designee) within the timeframe specified by Fannie Mae. If records are maintained in a non-paper format, the seller/servicer must reproduce them at its own expense. If unable to respond timely, the seller/servicer must provide a reasonable explanation and, if appropriate, evidence that the relevant requirement was satisfied. The seller/servicer is responsible for Fannie Mae losses incurred to enforce record access unless Fannie Mae had no legal right of access.
business_rules.conditions: Applies upon Fannie Mae written request to examine mortgage records
business_rules.consequences: Timely delivery satisfies audit; failure may trigger loss liability/enforcement
business_rules.exceptions: Loss liability exception if Fannie Mae had no legal right of access

source_reference.section_id: A2-4.1-02, General Requirements for Audits of Records
source_reference.source_text: When Fannie Mae sends a written request to a seller/servicer to examine mortgage records, the seller/servicer must deliver all records to Fannie Mae or to whomever Fannie Mae designates within the time frame specified by Fannie Mae. Fannie Mae will not execute any trust receipts for documents it requests and will not pay for their delivery. If the seller/servicer is retaining any of the records in a format other than paper, the seller/servicer must reproduce them at its own expense.

examples: Fannie Mae requests a post-purchase file; lender ships imaged documents by the stated due date and satisfies the request.
Lender stores records electronically; when requested, lender prints/exports and provides them at its own expense within the deadline.
Lender cannot meet the deadline due to a system outage; lender provides a written explanation and supporting evidence of compliance for the items under review.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification for Fannie Mae losses enforcing record access

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_008_007 requires delivering all requested mortgage records within the specified timeframe and reproducing non-paper records. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 requires maintaining a complete loan file and providing electronic copies upon request—this is foundational to meeting record examination requests.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If complete loan files are not maintained or cannot be produced in required formats, the seller/servicer may miss the examination deadline, leading to enforcement and potential loss liability.

provenance.reasoning: Both require producing paper copies of loan file/records when maintained in non-paper formats upon the GSE’s written request. Freddie is specific to paper production/transfer; Fannie includes broader delivery timing and explanation duties.

g1_rule.rule_name: Seller must deliver records within Fannie Mae deadline
g1_rule.description: When Fannie Mae issues a written request to examine mortgage records, the seller/servicer must deliver all requested records to Fannie Mae (or its designee) within the timeframe specified by Fannie Mae. If records are maintained in a non-paper format, the seller/servicer must reproduce them at its own expense. If unable to respond timely, the seller/servicer must provide a reasonable explanation and, if appropriate, evidence that the relevant requirement was satisfied. The seller/servicer is responsible for Fannie Mae losses incurred to enforce record access unless Fannie Mae had no legal right of access.

g2_rule.rule_name: Non-Paper Records Must Be Produced as Paper on Request
g2_rule.description: If the Servicer maintains copies of documents/loan files in any media other than paper, the Servicer must produce a paper copy at Freddie Mac’s prior written request. If Freddie Mac requests transfer of any documents, the Servicer must transfer those documents as paper copies unless otherwise agreed by Freddie Mac.

business_rules.rule_name: Forward borrower funds within 1 business day after receipt
business_rules.description: Within one (1) Business Day of receipt, Seller must transfer and forward to Servicer (via wire transfer or overnight delivery service) all funds received from the mortgagor after the Purchase Date.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller receives mortgagor funds after the Purchase Date under the agreement
business_rules.consequences: Timely forwarding meets contractual duty; late forwarding may constitute breach and trigger disputes
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 6.2 After Receipt. Within one (1) Business Day of receipt, Seller shall transfer and forward to Servicer via wire transfer or overnight delivery service all funds received from the mortgagor after the Purchase Date.

examples: Seller receives a borrower’s payment on Tuesday after Purchase Date; Seller must wire/overnight forward it by Wednesday (1 Business Day).
Seller receives funds on Friday; forwarding by Monday is timely if Monday is the next Business Day.
Seller holds funds for 3 Business Days to batch transfers; this violates the 1 Business Day requirement.
Contract requires record retention and examination of mortgage files for all delivered loans.
Contract defines remedies available to Fannie Mae for material defects.

provenance.reasoning: Both require promptly forwarding funds received after a transfer event to the proper counterparty within about one day. Rule A: Seller forwards mortgagor funds post-Purchase Date within 1 business day to Servicer. Rule B: transferor delivers post-transfer funds to transferee within one day.

g1_rule.rule_name: Forward borrower funds within 1 business day after receipt
g1_rule.description: Within one (1) Business Day of receipt, Seller must transfer and forward to Servicer (via wire transfer or overnight delivery service) all funds received from the mortgagor after the Purchase Date.

g2_rule.rule_name: Post-Transfer Funds Must Be Sent to Transferee in 1 Day
g2_rule.description: After a transfer of servicing becomes effective, any funds for, or correspondence regarding, transferred mortgages that the transferor receives on or after the effective date must be delivered to the transferee within one day of receipt.

business_rules.rule_name: Late Delivery Data Delays Funding to Next Business Day
business_rules.description: If the required delivery data and documentation are not received by the applicable cutoff time, Fannie Mae will disburse whole loan purchase proceeds on the business day following the date it receives the required materials, assuming the materials are in order and the mortgage is eligible for purchase.
business_rules.conditions: Required delivery data/docs missed the cutoff time for whole loan funding
business_rules.consequences: Funding shifts to the next business day after receipt; unresolved issues can further delay purchase
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C2-2-04 (Impact of Late Submissions of Required Data and Documentation)
source_reference.source_text: If the required delivery data and documentation are not received by the applicable cutoﬀ time, Fannie Mae will disburse the purchase proceeds on the business day following the date it receives these materials, assuming they are in order and the mortgage is eligible for purchase.

examples: Custodian package arrives one day late; Fannie Mae disburses proceeds the business day after receiving the complete, in-order package.
Data arrives after cutoff and contains errors; disbursement does not occur until corrected materials are received and in order.
Delivery materials arrive on Friday after cutoff; disbursement occurs Monday (next business day) if eligible and in order.
Approved Seller/Servicer originates a fixed-rate purchase loan intended for delivery.
Seller/Servicer originates an ARM subject to ARM policy requirements.

dependencies.rationale: BR_034_008 only applies if required delivery data/documentation are not received by the cutoff described in BR_034_007. Therefore, the evaluation of BR_034_008 depends on the timing outcome relative to BR_034_007 conditions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_034_007 cutoff is missed, funding shifts to the next business day after receipt; operational timelines and settlement expectations change.

provenance.reasoning: Both impose a delivery cutoff time for required loan delivery materials and define that missing the cutoff constitutes/permits treatment as late, affecting downstream processing (funding timing vs late delivery handling). Different programs and consequences, but similar compliance concept.

g1_rule.rule_name: Late Delivery Data Delays Funding to Next Business Day
g1_rule.description: If the required delivery data and documentation are not received by the applicable cutoff time, Fannie Mae will disburse whole loan purchase proceeds on the business day following the date it receives the required materials, assuming the materials are in order and the mortgage is eligible for purchase.

g2_rule.rule_name: SBL final delivery due by noon ET on Mandatory Delivery Date with email notice
g2_rule.description: For SBL Mortgages under the standard delivery option, the Seller/Servicer must deliver all documents listed in the Final Delivery Table of Contents – SBL at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date. Freddie Mac may treat as a late delivery if the Seller/Servicer fails to deliver the Final Delivery Package, including sending an email to mf_purchase_boarding_mgrs@freddiemac.com notifying Multifamily Purchase of the delivery of the Electronic Delivery Package, at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date.

business_rules.rule_name: PMI lapse requires reinstate or repurchase within 30 days; reimburse in 15 days
business_rules.description: If failure of a Private Mortgage Insurance policy to be in force on or after the Purchase Date is due to Seller negligence, Seller must, no later than 30 days after receiving notification, either take actions necessary to cause the policy to be in force/reinstated or repurchase the related Servicing Rights at the Servicing Rights Repurchase Price. If Seller fails to comply, Servicer must notify Fannie Mae to initiate the repurchase process. If a rescission is notified and the policy cannot be reinstated, Seller must reimburse Servicer within 15 business days of Servicer’s reimbursement request.
business_rules.conditions: PMI policy not in force/rescinded post-Purchase Date due to Seller negligence
business_rules.consequences: If cured timely, servicing continues; if not, repurchase process is initiated
business_rules.exceptions: Servicer not required to advance funds to reinstate PMI

source_reference.source_text: Seller shall, no later than thirty (30) days after Seller’s receipt of notiﬁcation thereof, either (a) take all such actions as are necessary to cause such policy to be in force or to be reinstated by the applicable insurer, or (b) repurchase the related Servicing Rights from Servicer at the Servicing Rights Repurchase Price. If Seller fails to comply with the preceding sentence with respect to a Mortgage Loan, Servicer shall notify Fannie Mae, to initiate the repurchase process with respect to such Mortgage Loan. At no time shall Servicer be made to advance funds to reinstate any Private Mortgage Insurance Policy... Seller shall reimburse Servicer for any amounts due within ﬁfteen (15) Business Days

examples: Seller receives notice on May 1 that PMI lapsed due to Seller error → by May 31 Seller must reinstate PMI or repurchase servicing rights.
Seller does not act within 30 days → Servicer notifies Fannie Mae to initiate repurchase process for the affected mortgage loan.
PMI policy is rescinded and cannot be reinstated; Servicer requests reimbursement on June 10 → Seller must reimburse by 15 business days after June 10.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Initiate repurchase process / servicing rights repurchase

examples: Seller/Servicer contract with representations and warranties for delivered mortgages
Contractual record retention and examination provisions for mortgage files
Contract remedy section enabling repurchase or make-whole payments

dependencies.rationale: BR_018_010 is a post-purchase cure/repurchase pathway when a PMI policy is not in force due to seller negligence. BR_039_001 establishes when MI coverage is required by LTV/loan attributes, making PMI existence and continuity a compliance-critical element. Thus, MI coverage requirements conditionally trigger the operational risk controlled by BR_018_010.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_039_001-required MI is not properly maintained and then lapses post-purchase, BR_018_010 escalates into cure within 30 days or repurchase/servicing-rights repurchase. Failure increases repurchase exposure and servicing disruption.

provenance.reasoning: Both impose a mandatory repurchase-related response within 30 days after receiving notice/request. However, Rule A is specific to PMI lapse due to seller negligence and allows cure (reinstate) instead of repurchase; Rule B is a general repurchase/indemnify demand with appeal/15-day post-denial timing.

g1_rule.rule_name: PMI lapse requires reinstate or repurchase within 30 days; reimburse in 15 days
g1_rule.description: If failure of a Private Mortgage Insurance policy to be in force on or after the Purchase Date is due to Seller negligence, Seller must, no later than 30 days after receiving notification, either take actions necessary to cause the policy to be in force/reinstated or repurchase the related Servicing Rights at the Servicing Rights Repurchase Price. If Seller fails to comply, Servicer must notify Fannie Mae to initiate the repurchase process. If a rescission is notified and the policy cannot be reinstated, Seller must reimburse Servicer within 15 business days of Servicer’s reimbursement request.

g2_rule.rule_name: Repurchase or indemnify within 30 days; appeal within 30 days; 15 days after denial
g2_rule.description: When Freddie Mac requires repurchase or indemnification, the Seller or Servicer must repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest or indemnify Freddie Mac within 30 days of Freddie Mac’s request. The Seller/Servicer may file one written appeal within 30 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s letter; if the appeal is denied, repurchase/indemnification must be completed within 15 days from the date of the denial letter. Failure to comply may result in suspension or termination of selling and/or servicing privileges or termination of servicing.

business_rules.rule_name: Price differential due same day if notice by 10:00 a.m.
business_rules.description: When Fannie Mae provides notice to satisfy the price differential on the lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk, the deadline depends on when notice is sent: if notice is sent by 10:00 a.m. on a business day, the lender must satisfy the price differential by close of business that same business day; if notice is sent after 10:00 a.m. on a business day, the lender must satisfy by 12:00 noon on the next business day.
business_rules.conditions: When Fannie Mae sends notice to satisfy a trading account price differential
business_rules.consequences: Failure to meet deadline constitutes nonperformance and may trigger default remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C3-7-01 (10/06/2021), Default of Lender Under the Terms of Trading Account
source_reference.source_text: Instances of nonperformance that constitute a default include the failure to: ... if notice is sent by Fannie Mae by 10:00 a.m. on a business day, satisfy the price diﬀerential on lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk by the close of business on that same business day; or if notice is sent by Fannie Mae after 10:00 a.m. on a business day, satisfy the price diﬀerential on lender’s trades with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk by 12:00 noon on the next business day.

examples: Notice sent 9:45 a.m. Tuesday; lender must wire/settle by COB Tuesday.
Notice sent 10:30 a.m. Wednesday; lender must satisfy by 12:00 noon Thursday.
Notice sent 9:30 a.m. Friday before a Monday holiday; lender must satisfy by COB Friday (business day).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Default declaration; accelerate settlement; UCC secured party remedies

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_002 governs the lender’s deadline to satisfy a price differential after Fannie Mae notice. In practice, Fannie Mae would only issue (or the lender would only treat) a margin call notice as actionable when the price differential meets the threshold and minimum transfer rules in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_001 (unless modified/otherwise agreed).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the threshold/minimum transfer logic in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_001 is not satisfied (or is misapplied), the lender may either (a) fail to respond to a valid margin call (default risk) or (b) unnecessarily move funds for a non-actionable call (liquidity/operational error).

business_rules.rule_name: Fannie Majors must be certified by 6:00 p.m. ET 4th-to-last day
business_rules.description: All Fannie Major deliveries must be error-free and certified by 6:00 p.m. Eastern time on the fourth to last business day before the end of the month.
business_rules.conditions: When delivering into Fannie Majors MBS pools for a given month-end cycle
business_rules.consequences: If not certified by deadline, delivery may miss issue month/settlement eligibility
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: A lender may submit data for mortgages in the same Fannie Majors at diﬀerent times and on diﬀerent days of the month, as long as all of the mortgages submitted are the same product type and have the same remittance cycle and servicer. All Fannie Major deliveries must be error-free and certiﬁed by 6:00 p.m. Eastern time on the fourth to last business day before the end of the month.

examples: Fourth-to-last business day is Fri 26th; certification must be completed by 6:00 p.m. ET on the 26th.
Submission on the 26th at 6:05 p.m. ET is late; lender may need to roll delivery to next issue month.
If errors remain at 5:55 p.m. ET, lender must correct and certify before 6:00 p.m. ET to meet requirement.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_004 requires Major deliveries be “error-free and certified” by a specific monthly deadline. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_001_003 imposes pool-count limits for high pass-through rate trades; exceeding those limits creates a delivery defect that prevents an “error-free” certification state or forces restructuring before certification.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the pool-count constraint is violated, certification may fail before the monthly cutoff, causing missed issue month/settlement eligibility and requiring pool restructuring and re-certification.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_001_004 imposes a monthly certification deadline for Major deliveries. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006 requires UCD Successful status before delivery of any conventional loan. Therefore, UCD completion must precede (or be satisfied before) the Major delivery certification process can be completed.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCD submission fails or is incomplete, Major delivery certification cannot be completed for affected loans/pools, risking missed issue month and settlement eligibility.

business_rules.rule_name: Guaranty fee drafted on 7th day or prior business day
business_rules.description: Fannie Mae drafts monthly guaranty fee remittances on the 7th day of each month; if the 7th is a holiday or weekend, the draft occurs on the preceding business day. The lender must manage its designated custodial bank draft account so the draft can occur as scheduled.
business_rules.conditions: When Fannie Mae drafts guaranty fee remittances from lender’s designated draft account
business_rules.consequences: Sufficient funds enables successful drafting; insufficient funds can trigger collection/escalation
business_rules.exceptions: If 7th is holiday/weekend, draft on preceding business day

source_reference.section_id: C3-3-01, Remitting Guaranty Fees to Fannie Mae
source_reference.source_text: Remitting Guaranty Fees to Fannie Mae On the 7th day of each month (or, if the 7th is a holiday or weekend, on the preceding business day), Fannie Mae will draft guaranty fee remittances from the custodial bank account the lender designates as its draft account.

examples: If the 7th falls on a Sunday, Fannie Mae drafts on Friday the 5th; lender must fund the draft account by that Friday.
If the lender changes its designated draft account, it must ensure the new account is able to be drafted on the next 7th-day cycle.
If the pool has no collection activity, the guaranty fee still drafts on the scheduled date.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_003_009 states Fannie Mae drafts monthly guaranty fee remittances on the 7th (or preceding business day) and the lender must manage the custodial bank draft account so the draft can occur. To manage funding and reconciliation, the lender must compute the expected remittance amount via BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_003_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the remittance amount is not computed correctly (or not computed in time), the draft account may be underfunded or overfunded, causing failed drafts, collections/escalations, or accounting breaks.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_003_009 expects ongoing drafting of guaranty fee remittances. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 states that upon a valid OFAC match, the servicer must block/segregate funds and ensure all servicing activities cease, including remittances and disbursements—this directly overrides normal remittance processing.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If OFAC cessation requirements are not applied, the servicer may transmit prohibited payments/remittances and mishandle blocked funds, creating sanctions exposure and severe enforcement risk.

business_rules.rule_name: CPM Eligibility Impact Notice Due Within 5 Business Days
business_rules.description: If the lender becomes aware of information that could impact the project eligibility status reflected in CPM (e.g., significant deferred maintenance or major litigation), it must notify the CPM Management team as soon as practicable but no later than five business days after becoming aware. Before selling a loan secured by a unit in such a project, the lender must confirm the project retains its approved status as of the note date unless the loan received and retains the CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message in DU.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when lender learns new project info affecting CPM eligibility status for an approved project.
business_rules.consequences: If met: CPM can be re-evaluated; sale may proceed if status confirmed. If violated: delivery risk and remedies.
business_rules.exceptions: Confirm-as-of-note-date step not required if DU retains CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message.

source_reference.section_id: B4-2.2-02, Additional Obligations of the Lender for Projects Approved by Fannie Mae
source_reference.source_text: If a lender becomes aware of any information that could impact the eligibility status reflected in CPM (such as, significant deferred maintenance or major litigation), the lender must notify the CPM Management team (see E-1-02, List of Contacts) with the relevant data and information. Fannie Mae will evaluate the new information and its impact on eligibility. Before selling a loan secured by a unit in such a project, the lender must confirm that the project retains its approved status as of the note date, unless a loan has received and retains a CPM Approved by Fannie Mae message in DU. Notification must occur as soon as practicable but no later than five business days after becoming aware of such information.

examples: On Monday, lender learns of newly filed major HOA litigation; lender must notify CPM Management by the following Monday (5 business days).
Lender discovers significant deferred maintenance on an approved project; before selling a loan with note date next week, lender confirms project still approved as of note date.

Loan retains DU message 'project has an Approved by Fannie Mae status in CPM'; lender is not required to re-confirm status as of note date, but still must follow delivery restrictions and other obligations.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery ineligibility / potential repurchase or remedies if defect later identified

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_023_010 requires certain projects to be submitted to PERS as a required review method. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_005_008 governs post-approval monitoring/notification for projects with eligibility status reflected in CPM and requires confirmation of approved status as of note date. Operationally, lenders move from initial required review (PERS) to ongoing CPM governance (notification/confirmation) once the project is approved and tracked.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If PERS-required review is skipped, the project may never be properly approved/tracked, making later CPM confirmation/notification moot and resulting in unit ineligibility for delivery.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 is triggered by information impacting 'project eligibility status reflected in CPM' and requires pre-sale confirmation of approved status. In SFHA attached condo contexts, inadequate HOA RCBAP coverage directly impacts project/loan eligibility; therefore BR_004_004 becomes a prerequisite check/input into the 'confirm retains approved status' step.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_004_004 fails: (1) project may not retain approved CPM status, (2) lender should not proceed with sale/delivery until cured or re-evaluated, (3) delivery defect/remedy exposure if delivered without compliant flood coverage.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 requires confirming a project retains approved status as of note date before sale. For projects subject to Full Review, the approved status is built and maintained on meeting criteria such as owner-occupancy/second home conveyance thresholds and phase limitations (BR_020_008). Thus BR_020_008 is a key input to ongoing status confirmation workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_020_008 fails: (1) project may fail Full Review acceptability, (2) CPM approved status may be lost/incorrect, (3) lender must not proceed with sale until addressed, or faces delivery/repurchase risk.

dependencies.rationale: A project that is not permitted under BR_005_007 cannot be treated as eligible/approved for delivery. Therefore, when executing BR_005_008’s pre-sale confirmation step, the lender must ensure the project does not violate BR_005_007 (or that the stated exception applies).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_005_007 fails: (1) project/unit is ineligible for delivery, (2) BR_005_008 confirmation cannot be satisfied legitimately, (3) selling/delivering the loan would create a delivery defect and remedies exposure.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 obligates the lender to confirm project approved status as of note date before selling a loan (absent certain CPM approvals) and to notify CPM when learning information that could impact eligibility. If an inspection within 3 years exists, BR_011_008 mandates review and sets clear eligibility triggers (critical repairs, evacuation orders, regulatory actions). The lender cannot credibly confirm status or decide on notification without this required review when applicable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_008 is skipped, the lender may sell a loan into an ineligible project (delivery defect) or fail to notify CPM timely, increasing repurchase/remedy risk and reputational/audit findings.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 is triggered by information that could impact CPM project eligibility status (explicit example: deferred maintenance). BR_011_007 defines a major deferred-maintenance threshold that renders a project ineligible. While BR_005_008 can be triggered by other factors (e.g., litigation), critical repairs is a central category of eligibility-impacting information and should be linked for navigation and workflow prompts.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_007 is not considered, the lender may fail to recognize deferred maintenance as eligibility-changing information, resulting in late/no CPM notification and increased delivery risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Self-employed income generally requires 2-year earnings history
business_rules.description: Fannie Mae generally requires the lender to obtain a two-year history of the borrower’s prior earnings to demonstrate the likelihood that self-employment income will continue to be received.
business_rules.conditions: When underwriting self-employment income as qualifying income
business_rules.consequences: If 2-year history obtained, proceed; if not, must meet <2-year exception rules
business_rules.exceptions: Income may be considered with <2-year history if requirements are met

source_reference.source_text: Fannie Mae generally requires lenders to obtain a two-year history of the borrower’s prior earnings as a means of demonstrating the likelihood that the income will continue to be received.

examples: Borrower has filed 2 years of returns for the current business: satisfies the general 2-year history expectation.
Borrower has only 1 year self-employment history: lender must evaluate under the <2-year history provisions before using income.
Borrower has 3+ years of self-employment: still analyze stability, but 2-year history requirement is met.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_012_003 requires demonstrating likelihood of continued self-employment income via a two-year prior earnings history. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005 provides the core verification mechanism (two years returns or IRS transcripts) that evidences that history.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If returns/transcripts are not obtained (or are incomplete/illegible), the lender may not be able to substantiate the two-year history, forcing exception analysis or exclusion of self-employment income.

business_rules.rule_name: Verbal VOE within 10 business days or 120 days before note date
business_rules.description: A verbal verification of employment (verbal VOE) must be obtained for each borrower using employment or self-employment income to qualify. The verbal VOE must be obtained within 10 business days prior to the note date for employment income, and within 120 calendar days prior to the note date for self-employment income. Alternatively, the lender may obtain the verbal VOE after closing up to the time of loan delivery; if the verbal VOE (or allowable alternative) cannot be obtained prior to delivery, the loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.conditions: When borrower uses employment or self-employment income to qualify for the loan
business_rules.consequences: If obtained timely, proceed; if not obtained by delivery, loan is ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: Allowed to obtain after closing up to loan delivery

source_reference.source_text: A verbal veriﬁcation of employment (verbal VOE) must be obtained for each borrower using employment or self-employment income to qualify. The verbal VOE must be obtained within 10 business days prior to the note date for employment income, and within 120 calendar days prior to the note date for self-employment income. Alternatively, the lender may obtain the verbal VOE after closing, up to the time of loan delivery. If the verbal VOE (or allowable alternative) cannot be obtained prior to delivery, the loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: W-2 borrower closing on June 20: verbal VOE must be dated within 10 business days before June 20 (or use an allowed alternative within required window).
Self-employed borrower note date June 20: verbal VOE must be dated within 120 calendar days before June 20, unless obtained after closing but before delivery.
Lender cannot obtain VOE or alternative before delivering the loan: loan is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_012_009 applies when the borrower is using employment or self-employment income to qualify and imposes timing rules for verbal VOE. In most workflows, the lender first validates self-employment income via acceptable returns/transcripts (BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_012_005) and then completes/refreshes VOE within the required window before note date (or by delivery).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sequencing breaks (VOE not refreshed within required window), the loan becomes ineligible at delivery even if income documentation was otherwise acceptable.

business_rules.rule_name: Seller must self-report selling warranty breach within 30 days
business_rules.description: If, after conducting due diligence, the lender determines that a breach of a selling warranty has likely occurred, the lender must notify Fannie Mae within 30 days using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect.
business_rules.conditions: After due diligence, lender determines a selling warranty breach likely occurred
business_rules.consequences: Failure to timely report may increase remedy/sanction risk under the contract
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the cited passage

source_reference.section_id: A2-2-01, Lender Reporting Requirements for Breach of Selling Warranty
source_reference.source_text: The lender must notify Fannie Mae within 30 days if, after conducting due diligence, it determines that a breach of a selling warranty has likely occurred, by using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect .

examples: QC review identifies misstatement of occupancy; lender completes due diligence and self-reports via Loan Quality Connect within 30 days: meets reporting requirement.
Lender discovers missing title exception documentation but does not report for 75 days: violates the 30-day reporting requirement.
Third-party fraud is discovered; lender determines a selling warranty breach likely occurred and self-reports within 30 days: meets requirement even though lender did not commit fraud.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_013_007 mandates notice using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 mandates registration for Loan Quality Connect and maintaining a complete loan file needed to support the report.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If LQC registration/file readiness is missing, the lender may fail to notify within 30 days, increasing contractual remedy exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Value acceptance may be exercised only if offer ≤ 4 months old
business_rules.description: A lender may only exercise value acceptance if the final DU submission resulted in a value acceptance offer, no appraisal is obtained for the transaction, and the offer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. If exercised, the lender must include Special Feature Code (SFC) 801 at delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Seller/Servicer intends to deliver a loan using DU value acceptance
business_rules.consequences: If conditions met, deliver without appraisal; if violated, must order appraisal
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the exercise criteria excerpt

source_reference.source_text: A lender may only exercise value acceptance if the ﬁnal submission of the loan caseﬁle to DU resulted in a value acceptance oﬀer, an appraisal is not obtained for the transaction, and the value acceptance oﬀer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and the mortgage. Lenders that elect to exercise value acceptance must include SFC 801 at delivery.

examples: DU value acceptance offer dated 3 months before note date and no appraisal ordered → lender may exercise value acceptance and deliver with SFC 801.
DU value acceptance offer is 5 months old at note date → lender may not exercise; must obtain an appraisal.
Lender orders an appraisal after receiving value acceptance → lender may not exercise value acceptance for that transaction.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand / remedies for breach of requirements

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Value acceptance plus data requires API submit before note and 12-month data
business_rules.description: A lender may only exercise value acceptance + property data when the final DU submission resulted in an eligibility message for the option, the property data collection is submitted to the Property Data API prior to the note date, no appraisal is obtained, and the offer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. The lender must include SFC 774 at delivery. Property data collection is valid for 12 months from the collection date and must be performed prior to the note date.
business_rules.conditions: Seller/Servicer uses value acceptance + property data instead of an appraisal
business_rules.consequences: If satisfied, appraisal not required; if not, must obtain hybrid/desktop/traditional
business_rules.exceptions: If offer lost, property data may support a hybrid appraisal per Guide

source_reference.source_text: A lender may only exercise value acceptance + property data when the ﬁnal submission of the loan caseﬁle to DU resulted in an eligibility message for value acceptance + property data, property data collection is submitted to the Property Data API prior to the note date, an appraisal is not obtained for the transaction, and the oﬀer is not more than four months old on the date of the note and mortgage. Lenders that elect to exercise value acceptance + property data must include Special Feature Code 774 at loan delivery. The property data collection is only valid for 12 months from date of collection and must be performed prior to the note date.

examples: Property data collected 2 weeks before note date and submitted to API before note date; offer is 2 months old → lender may deliver without appraisal using SFC 774.
Property data collected after the note date → lender cannot exercise this option; must obtain appraisal solution required by DU.
Property data collected 13 months ago (even if resubmitted) → invalid; lender must obtain new property data collection or appraisal.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand / delivery rejection risk

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_007 covers DU value acceptance without appraisal and requires SFC 801 at delivery. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 covers value acceptance + property data, requires Property Data API submission and SFC 774. A loan should follow the path corresponding to DU’s eligibility message and whether property data collection was submitted; using the wrong path or coding both can create delivery defects.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender misclassifies the waiver type, it may deliver with the wrong SFC and missing prerequisite artifacts (e.g., missing Property Data API submission), causing delivery findings, repurchase risk, or forced appraisal.

business_rules.rule_name: eNote Registration Must Occur Within 1 Business Day After Signing
business_rules.description: Lenders must have process controls to ensure eNotes are registered in the MERS eRegistry as soon as possible after the tamper-evident seal is applied, but no later than one business day after signing. The MERS eRegistry record must show the originating lender (the lender whose name is on the eNote) as the first Controller. If trade/assumed names are used on eNotes, the legal entity name must also be included on the eNote, and the initial MERS eRegistry registration must reflect the legal entity name as the initial Controller. Failure to comply could render the loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when originating and closing an eMortgage with an eNote.
business_rules.consequences: If registered timely with correct Controller, compliant; if not, may be ineligible for sale.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: Lenders must have process controls in place to ensure eNotes are registered in the MERS eRegistry as soon as possible after the tamper-evident seal has been applied, but no later than one business day after signing. The record in the MERS eRegistry must reflect the originating lender - the lender whose name is on the eNote - as the first Controller. The following applies to lenders using tradenames or assumed names on eNotes: the legal entity name must also be included on the eNote... and the initial MERS eRegistry registration must reflect the legal entity name as the initial Controller... Failure to comply with these requirements could render the loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: Borrower signs Tuesday; lender registers eNote in MERS eRegistry by Wednesday (next business day) with lender as first Controller → compliant.
Borrower signs Friday; lender registers the following Tuesday (2 business days later) → violates timing requirement; loan may be ineligible.
eNote shows 'XYZ Mortgage' only; legal entity 'ABC Financial, Inc.' not included and Controller set to XYZ → violates tradename/legal-entity requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Seller Must Return Servicing Docs Within 5 Days
business_rules.description: When the Servicer requires Seller execution of servicing-related documentation, Seller must execute and return the documentation as promptly as reasonably feasible, but in no event more than five (5) days after delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Execution by Seller is required for servicing documentation after delivery
business_rules.consequences: If met, servicing transfer proceeds; if violated, transfer delays and disputes
business_rules.exceptions: Alternative: Seller may provide POA allowing Servicer execution

source_reference.source_text: alternative when execution by Seller is required, Seller shall, as promptly as reasonably feasible, but in no event exceeding ﬁve (5) days after delivery, execute and

examples: Servicer sends a satisfaction document to Seller on Monday; Seller must return executed document by Saturday (5 days).
Servicer requests Seller-signed assignment correction delivered on June 1; Seller returns signed version by June 5 (compliant).
Instead of signing each document, Seller provides a power of attorney enabling Servicer to execute required documents (alternative compliance).
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Escrowed Taxes Must Be Paid Within 30 Days After Transfer
business_rules.description: If the mortgage loan requires the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller must pay all real estate taxes that are due and payable on or before the Purchase Date and within thirty (30) days after the Purchase Date. Seller must not pay any bill for which Seller has not specifically retained escrow funds. If the loan does not require escrow, Seller must cause the mortgagors to pay the described real estate taxes.
business_rules.conditions: Servicing rights transferred; taxes due and payable and escrow requirement applies
business_rules.consequences: If met, tax obligations stay current; if violated, tax delinquency and transfer issues
business_rules.exceptions: If no escrow required, Seller must cause mortgagors to pay taxes

source_reference.source_text: Real Estate Tax Payment .
