In the event the Mortgage Loan requires the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller shall pay all real estate taxes that are due and payable (i) on or before the Purchase Date, and (ii) within thirty (30) days after the Purchase Date. Seller agrees to follow transfer procedures with respect to real estate tax service contracts in a manner described in the Servicing Transfer Instructions. In addition, Seller shall not pay any bill for which Seller has not specifically retained escrow funds pursuant to Section 6.2 herein. In the event the Mortgage Loan does not require the mortgagor to escrow funds, Seller shall cause the mortgagors to pay the above-described real estate taxes.

examples: Escrowed loan has property taxes due 10 days after Purchase Date; Seller must pay within 30 days after Purchase Date.
Tax bill arrives but Seller did not retain escrow funds under Section 6.2; Seller must not pay that bill (non-payment required).
Non-escrow loan has taxes due after transfer; Seller must ensure mortgagor pays the taxes as described.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_002 requires paying real estate taxes under certain escrow/transfer conditions. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 requires blocking/segregating funds (including escrow) and ceasing servicing activities including disbursements upon a valid OFAC match. That creates an exception state overriding routine tax disbursement obligations until properly handled under OFAC controls.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender continues tax disbursements from blocked escrow, it can violate OFAC requirements. If it ceases disbursements without appropriate OFAC handling/notification, it can create tax delinquency risk—requiring a controlled, documented resolution path.

business_rules.rule_name: Loan File Due in 3 Business Days or $500 Late Fee
business_rules.description: Within three (3) Business Days after the Purchase Date, Seller must furnish Servicer (or designated document custodian) a complete loan file as described in the Fannie Mae Selling Guide (unless otherwise directed by Fannie Mae), must retain a copy of the complete loan file per the Fannie Mae Servicing Guide, and may be charged a late fee up to $500.00 per mortgage loan if Seller fails to comply.
business_rules.conditions: After Purchase Date when servicing rights are transferred and file delivery required
business_rules.consequences: If met, Servicer receives compliant file; if violated, late fee up to $500/loan
business_rules.exceptions: Complete file delivery may differ if directed otherwise by Fannie Mae

source_reference.source_text: Within three (3) Business Days after the Purchase Date, Seller at its own expense shall furnish Servicer or the designated document custodian, as applicable, with the following: ... (b) File. A complete loan file as described in the Fannie Mae Selling Guide to Servicer unless otherwise directed by Fannie Mae. Seller must also retain a copy of the complete loan file and maintain that copy in accordance with the requirements of the Fannie Mae Servicing Guide and any other applicable requirements set forth therein. ... (d) Late Fee . If Seller fails to comply with this Section, Servicer may, in its sole discretion, charge Seller a late fee of up to $500.00 per mortgage loan.

examples: Purchase Date is Tuesday; Seller must deliver the complete file by Friday (3 business days) to avoid late fee exposure.
Seller delivers file on day 6; Servicer may charge $500 late fee for that mortgage loan.
Fannie Mae directs an alternative file routing; Seller follows that instruction instead of standard delivery destination.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Late fee up to $500 per mortgage loan

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_003 requires furnishing a complete loan file within 3 business days after purchase date and retaining a copy. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_011_009 defines the expectation that the lender maintains a complete loan file including all documents used to support the underwriting decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender does not maintain a complete file, it cannot timely furnish a complete transfer file, leading to late fees and servicing transfer defects.

business_rules.rule_name: Annual audited financial statement due within 90 days
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must submit its annual Audited Financial Statement (AFS) within 90 days after the end of its fiscal year. Failure to timely provide required financial statements and information may result in Fannie Mae declaring a breach of the Lender Contract.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to every Seller/Servicer fiscal year-end AFS submission to Fannie Mae
business_rules.consequences: If late/not provided, Fannie Mae may declare a Lender Contract breach
business_rules.exceptions: See separate exceptions for supervised depository institutions and HUD audits

source_reference.source_text: The seller/servicer must demonstrate its ﬁnancial adequacy to Fannie Mae. To accomplish this, Fannie Mae requires the seller/servicer to submit ﬁnancial statements and other information described in this topic. A seller/servicer’s failure to timely provide the required information may result in Fannie Mae declaring a breach of the Lender Contract. A seller/servicer must submit their annual Audited Financial Statement (AFS) within 90 days after the end of the seller/servicer’s ﬁscal year.

examples: Seller/Servicer fiscal year ends 12/31; AFS must be submitted by 03/31; submission on 04/15 is a late submission exposing a breach finding.
Seller/Servicer fiscal year ends 06/30; AFS due by 09/28; failure to submit can trigger Lender Contract breach remedies.
Seller/Servicer submits AFS by day 75 after fiscal year end; requirement satisfied and no breach risk on timeliness.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1002 quarterly due 30 days; Dec 31 due 60
business_rules.description: If the Seller/Servicer is a mortgage banker, subsidiary of a federally supervised depository institution, housing finance agency, or REIT, it must submit Form 1002 at the end of each calendar quarter, certified by the CEO, CFO, or equivalent. The Seller/Servicer must electronically submit within 30 days for the March 31, June 30, and September 30 reports, and within 60 days for the December 31 report. Each report must include only the financial data related to the quarterly reporting period.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer type requires Form 1002 quarterly reporting
business_rules.consequences: Late/inaccurate quarterly reporting may support eligibility actions or breach
business_rules.exceptions: Non-calendar accounting cycles may be used if report includes only required quarter

source_reference.source_text: In addition to the AFS, the seller/servicer must submit a Mortgage Bankers’ Financial Reporting Form (Form 1002) that has been certiﬁed by the chief executive oﬃcer, the chief ﬁnancial oﬃcer, or equivalent at the end of each calendar quarter. Each report must include only the ﬁnancial data related to the quarterly reporting period for which the report is being submitted. The seller/servicer must electronically submit this information within 30 days for the March 31, June 30, and September 30 reports and within 60 days for the December 31 report.

examples: For Q1 ending 03/31, Form 1002 must be submitted by 04/30; submission on 05/10 violates the deadline.
For Q4 ending 12/31, Form 1002 must be submitted by 03/01 (60 days); submission on 02/15 meets the rule.
If a Seller/Servicer uses a non-standard quarterly accounting cycle, it may keep its methodology but must ensure each report contains data for only the required quarter.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1002A monthly due 30 days; skip quarter 3rd month
business_rules.description: If the Seller/Servicer is a large non-depository Seller/Servicer, it must electronically submit Form 1002A within 30 days of the end of each month, certified by the CEO, CFO, or equivalent. Each report must include only the financial data related to the month preceding the month of submission. The Seller/Servicer is not required to provide a monthly report for the third month of any fiscal quarter because the data will be captured in the quarterly report.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to large non-depository Seller/Servicers subject to monthly Form 1002A
business_rules.consequences: Missing/late reports may drive enhanced oversight or eligibility actions
business_rules.exceptions: No monthly report required for each third month of any fiscal quarter

source_reference.source_text: In addition to the required quarterly reporting, a large non-depository seller/servicer must electronically submit a Mortgage Bankers' Financial Reporting Short Form (Form 1002A) within 30 days of the end of each month. The report must be certiﬁed by the chief executive oﬀer, the chief ﬁnancial oﬃcer, or equivalent. Each report must include only the ﬁnancial data related to the month preceding the month in which the report is being submitted. The seller/servicer is not required to provide a monthly report for each third month of any ﬁscal quarter, as the data will be captured in the quarterly report.

examples: A large non-depository Seller/Servicer’s January month-end is 01/31; Form 1002A must be submitted by 03/02 (30 days after 01/31).
For a fiscal quarter April–June, the Seller/Servicer submits monthly reports for April and May but does not submit a June monthly report in July because June is the third month of the quarter.
If a monthly report includes two months of data (e.g., April+May), it violates the requirement to include only the prior month’s data.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Form 582 update within 90 days; notify changes in 5 days
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must update Form 582 electronically no later than 90 days after the end of its fiscal year. If lender record information changes, the Seller/Servicer must update Form 582 and provide written notification by email to the designated mailbox within five business days of the occurrence of specified events (e.g., actions pending/threatened that could reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to annual eligibility maintenance and upon any lender record information change
business_rules.consequences: Failure/late notice increases eligibility risk and may lead to Fannie Mae action
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The Lender Record Information ( Form 582 ) provides information needed to verify that the seller/servicer continues to meet Fannie Mae's basic eligibility requirements as well as certiﬁcations regarding compliance with our requirements. The seller/servicer must update its Form 582 electronically no later than 90 days after the end of the seller/servicer’s ﬁscal year. If there are changes to the lender's record information, the seller/servicer must update Form 582 and provide Fannie Mae with written notiﬁcation in the form of an email to the Changes in Lender Organization mailbox ... within ﬁve business days of the occurrence of any of the following: any actions pending, starting, or to the seller/servicer's knowledge, threatened against or involving the seller/servicer that could reasonably be expected to have a material adverse eﬀect

examples: Fiscal year ends 09/30; Form 582 must be updated by 12/29 (90 days).
A regulator initiates an enforcement action on 02/01 that could materially affect operations; the Seller/Servicer must email notice and update Form 582 by 02/08 (five business days).
A corporate reorganization occurs on 03/10; Seller/Servicer must provide written notification within five business days and update Form 582 accordingly.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: PERS Required for Florida Attached Condos and 4+ Unit Conversions
business_rules.description: Lenders must submit certain projects to Fannie Mae’s Project Eligibility Review Service (PERS) as a required review method. Required Standard PERS includes, among other cases: newly converted non-gut rehabilitation condo and co-op projects with attached units that contain more than four units, and new/newly converted condo projects consisting of attached units located in Florida. Other projects are optional or may be required under the streamlined PERS process as specified.
business_rules.conditions: When lender seeks project eligibility for project types designated as PERS-required
business_rules.consequences: Meets: project reviewed/eligible path supported; Violates: missing required PERS; project/unit ineligible for delivery
business_rules.exceptions: Projects not listed may be optional per table; manufactured-home references include single/multi-width unless noted

source_reference.section_id: B4-2.2-06, Overview (PERS required vs optional table)
source_reference.source_text: Required for: • newly converted non-gut rehabilitation condo and co-op projects with attached units that contain more than four units; • new and newly converted condo projects consisting of attached units located in Florida; and • limited or shared equity co-op projects, provided the limited or shared equity provisions are designed to preserve or promote access to aﬀordable housing. Optional for: • all other new or newly converted condo projects not listed above.

examples: Newly converted non-gut rehab co-op with 12 attached units: PERS submission is required before delivering a unit loan to Fannie Mae.
New attached condo project in Florida: lender must use Standard PERS (cannot treat as optional).
Newly converted attached condo project outside Florida and not otherwise listed: PERS may be optional per the table.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for delivery / potential repurchase demand

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Termination and tech access ended within 60 days if minimums not met
business_rules.description: If the seller fails the minimum loan sale requirement, Fannie Mae will terminate approval and terminate access to technology licensed only to approved sellers within 60 days. If the servicer fails the minimum servicing requirement, Fannie Mae will terminate approval and terminate access to technology licensed only to approved servicers.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when prior-year minimum loan sale or servicing requirements are not met.
business_rules.consequences: Approval is terminated; technology access is terminated within 60 days; reapproval requires new application.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: If a seller does not meet the minimum loan sale requirement below, the seller's approval to sell loans to Fannie Mae will be terminated and access to all technology that is licensed only to approved sellers will be terminate within 60 days. If a servicer does not meet the minimum loan servicing requirement below, the servicer's approval to service loans for Fannie Mae will be terminated

examples: Seller sold 9 loans last year: Fannie Mae terminates seller approval and ends seller-only tech access within 60 days.
Servicer has no Fannie Mae loans as of Dec 31: Fannie Mae terminates servicer approval and ends servicer-only tech access.
Entity terminated as seller re-applies via Seller/Servicer application process to regain eligibility.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_025_003 states that if the seller fails the minimum loan sale requirement, Fannie Mae will terminate approval and technology access within 60 days. That minimum requirement is defined by BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_025_001 (≥12 loans sold in prior calendar year).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the minimum sale requirement is not met, seller approval and technology access are terminated, blocking future deliveries and requiring reapplication for approval.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_025_003 states that if the servicer fails the minimum servicing requirement, Fannie Mae will terminate approval and technology access. The minimum requirement is defined by BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_025_002 (service at least one Fannie Mae loan as of Dec 31).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the minimum servicing requirement is not met, servicer approval is terminated, restricting servicing activities and requiring reapproval.

business_rules.rule_name: Large non-depository plan due 90 days with 5-day and 1-day notices
business_rules.description: Within 90 days after the end of each calendar year, Large Non-depository Seller/Servicers must submit a capital and liquidity plan to Fannie Mae describing how they will manage capital and liquidity consistent with Fannie Mae requirements. The plan must require written notice to Fannie Mae (a) within five business days following any material change to or material deviation from the plan, and (b) within one business day of any material changes during times of stress.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Large Non-depository Seller/Servicers; measured annually with event-driven notice triggers
business_rules.consequences: If completed timely, meets reporting obligation; if late/missed notices, Fannie Mae may require remediation or impose eligibility actions
business_rules.exceptions: None stated (stress test may be in-house or third-party)

source_reference.source_text: Within 90 days after the end of each calendar year, Large Non-depository sellers/servicers must submit a plan to Fannie Mae that describes how the seller/servicer intends to manage its capital and liquidity in a manner consistent with Fannie Mae requirements. ... and • a requirement to provide written notice to Fannie Mae ⚬ within ﬁve business days following any material change to or material deviation from the plan; and ⚬ within one business day of any material changes during times of stress.

examples: Calendar year ends 12/31; Large non-depository submits its plan by 3/31 (within 90 days) and satisfies the timing requirement.
Seller/Servicer updates contingency funding assumptions on 6/15 (material change) → must notify Fannie Mae in writing by 6/22 (within 5 business days).
During a stress event, Seller/Servicer makes a material change on Monday → must provide written notice by Tuesday (within 1 business day).
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_005 requires a plan describing how the lender will manage capital and liquidity consistent with Fannie Mae requirements. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_027_001 defines a core minimum adjusted net worth requirement (base + UPB-based add-ons) that the plan must address and monitor.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If minimum adjusted net worth requirements are not incorporated into the plan, the plan may be noncompliant/inadequate, increasing the risk of eligibility actions and forced remediation during stress.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_027_002 imposes a minimum adjusted net worth to total assets ratio for non-depositories. A capital plan required by BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_005 must include monitoring and actions to remain above this ratio, especially in stress scenarios.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the ratio requirement is not planned for, asset growth or valuation changes can cause a breach, triggering corrective action and potential eligibility restrictions.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_027_003 defines the calculated minimum allowable liquidity for non-depositories based on servicing UPB/remittance types. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_005 requires a liquidity plan and rapid notice of material deviations/changes, which necessarily depends on understanding/measuring this minimum.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If liquidity minima are not measured/embedded in the plan, the lender may breach liquidity requirements without timely mitigation or notice, increasing eligibility action risk.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_027_004 imposes an additional origination liquidity requirement for certain high-volume non-depository originators. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_005 requires a plan consistent with Fannie requirements; when the threshold condition is met, this constraint becomes a required input into plan design and reporting triggers.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lender crosses the >$1B threshold and the plan does not adapt, the lender may become noncompliant with liquidity requirements and face restrictions or mandated liquidity increases.

business_rules.rule_name: Fidelity and E&O policies require 30-day and 10-day notices
business_rules.description: A Seller/Servicer must have a blanket fidelity bond and an errors and omissions (E&O) insurance policy in effect at all times. Each fidelity bond and E&O policy must include: (1) notice to Fannie Mae at least 30 days before insurer cancels, reduces, declines to renew, or imposes a restrictive modification (other than partial/full exhaustion of liability limits); and (2) a provision that the insurer will notify Fannie Mae within 10 days after receiving the Seller/Servicer’s request to cancel or reduce coverage. The Seller/Servicer must provide Fannie Mae a copy of the fidelity bond or E&O insurance certificate within 30 days of Fannie Mae’s request.
business_rules.conditions: Seller/Servicer maintains fidelity bond and E&O coverage while approved
business_rules.consequences: If provisions/certificates not provided, approval risk and contract noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: 30-day notice exception when change due to exhaustion of liability limits

source_reference.section_id: A3-5-01, Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Coverage Provisions
source_reference.source_text: A seller/servicer must have a blanket fidelity bond and an errors and omissions insurance policy in effect at all times in an amount sufficient to meet Fannie Mae’s minimum coverage requirements described in A3-5-02, Fidelity Bond Policy Requirements and A3-5-03, Errors and Omissions Policy Requirements. Each fidelity bond and errors and omissions insurance policy must include the following provisions: Fannie Mae will be notified at least 30 days before the insurer cancels, reduces, declines to renew, or imposes a restrictive modification to the seller/servicer’s coverage for any reason other than a partial or full exhaustion of the insurer’s limit of liability under the policy; and a provision that the insurer will notify Fannie Mae within ten days after the insurer receives a seller/servicer’s request to cancel or reduce any coverage. The seller/servicer must provide Fannie Mae a copy of its fidelity bond or errors and omissions insurance certificate within 30 days of Fannie Mae’s request.

examples: Insurer plans nonrenewal effective July 31; insurer must notify Fannie Mae by at least July 1 (30 days prior).
Seller/Servicer emails insurer requesting reduced limits; insurer must notify Fannie Mae within 10 days of receiving the request.
Fannie Mae requests insurance certificate on May 1; Seller/Servicer must provide it by May 31.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing requirements (30 days, 10 days, 30 days) and always-in-effect requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension or termination of Seller/Servicer approval

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: C1-2-03 UCD XML Successful Status Before Delivery
business_rules.description: Before delivering a conventional loan to Fannie Mae, the lender must electronically submit a Uniform Closing Dataset (UCD) XML file and receive a “Successful” status from the UCD collection solution. The UCD XML must contain borrower data and the PDF of the borrower Closing Disclosure. This submission is required for all conventional loans, even when Regulation Z does not require a Closing Disclosure, including non-owner-occupied property loans.
business_rules.conditions: Before delivery of any conventional loan to Fannie Mae (all occupancies)
business_rules.consequences: If met: loan may proceed to delivery; if violated: delivery is blocked until successful
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; applies regardless of Regulation Z CD requirement

source_reference.source_text: Lenders are required to electronically submit a UCD XML ﬁle of the loan to Fannie Mae and receive a “Successful” status from the UCD collection solution prior to the delivery of the loan. The UCD XML ﬁle must contain the borrower data and the PDF of the borrower Closing Disclosure. Lenders must submit the UCD XML ﬁle for all conventional loans, regardless of whether the TILA/Regulation Z Rule requires the Closing Disclosure, including non-owner-occupied property loans.

examples: Primary residence conventional loan: lender uploads UCD XML plus CD PDF; status returns Successful; delivery proceeds.
Investment property conventional loan with CD not required by Reg Z: lender still submits UCD XML and gets Successful.
UCD submission returns non-Successful status; lender must correct/resubmit before delivery.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection (cannot deliver until Successful status)

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_020_006 requires timely MERS eRegistry registration (no later than one business day after signing) and correct Controller designation; failure may make the loan ineligible for sale. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006 gates delivery of conventional loans on successful UCD submission—delivery readiness depends on underlying eMortgage eligibility controls being satisfied.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If eRegistry registration/controller is incorrect or late, the loan may be ineligible for sale even if UCD submission is successful, causing delivery suspension and corrective re-registration work.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_005_010 explicitly prohibits delivery of a loan to Fannie Mae if any borrower is on an OFAC sanctions list. Therefore, before completing delivery steps (including UCD submission success as a delivery gate) under BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006, OFAC screening must clear.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If OFAC screening fails (valid match), delivery must stop; attempting delivery creates severe sanctions/compliance exposure and contractual violations.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SUBJECT_PROPERTY_ELIGIBILITY_004_002 makes loans ineligible where a principal/residential detached structure is in an SFHA and the community does not participate in NFIP (with a CBRS/OPA nuance). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006 governs delivery readiness; eligibility checks should gate delivery because an ineligible loan cannot be purchased.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If flood/NFIP status is not validated before delivery, the lender may deliver an ineligible loan leading to rejection, pipeline disruption, and potential contractual remedies.

business_rules.rule_name: Whole loan docs overnight same day for next-day certification
business_rules.description: Mortgage document submission packages for whole loans (and ASAP Plus/ASAP Sale) must be submitted to the assigned document custodian via overnight delivery with instructions for first morning delivery. To ensure timely delivery, the seller should submit the document package to its overnight carrier the same day the seller submits loan data to Fannie Mae for purchase. Loan data delivered will be certified the next business day provided documents were delivered via first morning delivery and there are no discrepancies and no unresolved lien issues.
business_rules.conditions: When seller submits whole loan data to Fannie Mae for purchase and custodian delivery
business_rules.consequences: If timely and error-free, certification occurs next business day; delays if late/discrepancies
business_rules.exceptions: Certification timing conditioned on no discrepancies and no unresolved lien issues

source_reference.source_text: Mortgage document submission packages must be submitted to the document custodian via overnight delivery (with instructions for ﬁrst morning delivery) for the following: whole loans, As Soon As Pooled Plus (ASAP Plus), and As Soon As Pooled (ASAP Sale)... the seller should ensure the loan document submission package is submitted to its overnight carrier (with instructions for ﬁrst morning delivery) the same day that the seller submits the loan data to Fannie Mae for purchase. Loan data delivered by the seller to Fannie Mae will be certiﬁed the next business day provided the documents have been delivered to the document custodian via ﬁrst morning delivery, and there are no discrepancies and no unresolved lien issues.

examples: Seller submits loan data Monday and tenders custodian package to overnight carrier Monday for first-morning Tuesday delivery → eligible for Tuesday (next business day) certification if no discrepancies.
Seller submits loan data Monday but ships package Tuesday → certification delayed beyond next business day.
Package arrives first morning but custodian identifies missing note endorsement → certification delayed until discrepancy resolved.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Purchase/certification delay; potential delivery failure remedies per agreements

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_037_004 describes operational timing for whole-loan document package submission aligned to the day loan data is submitted for purchase. Since BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_006 requires UCD XML submission and Successful status before delivering a conventional loan, UCD success must occur before/with the delivery submission events that BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_037_004 supports.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCD Successful status is not obtained, delivery is blocked; document shipment and certification timelines become irrelevant or cause rework and missed purchase windows.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_037_004 anticipates next-business-day certification when documents are delivered properly and data is submitted. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_037_003 limits the maximum delivery/overdelivery amounts; if delivery exceeds permitted limits without approval, delivery may be rejected or repriced, preventing smooth certification under the process timeline.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If overdelivery constraints are not met, the loan set may be rejected or require permission/price adjustments, delaying certification and increasing operational costs.

business_rules.rule_name: Servicing rights pledge approval requested 30 days prior
business_rules.description: Before entering into a pledge of servicing or a transfer of an interest in servicing income, the lender must obtain Fannie Mae’s prior written consent and must request Fannie Mae’s prior approval at least 30 days before the proposed effective date of the transaction.
business_rules.conditions: When pledging servicing rights or transferring servicing-income interest
business_rules.consequences: If requested ≥30 days prior, can proceed subject to consent; if not, noncompliant transaction risk
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; applies only to permitted-purpose transactions

source_reference.section_id: A3-3-03, Pledge of Servicing Rights and Transfer of Interest in Servicing Income
source_reference.source_text: A lender may enter into one of the following transactions, provided that the purpose of the transaction is a purpose permitted by Fannie Mae and the lender obtains Fannie Mae’s prior written consent: a pledge, or grant of a security interest in, the servicing rights to all or part of its Fannie Mae servicing portfolio... The lender must request Fannie Mae’s prior approval of a speciﬁc pledging transaction or transfer of an interest in servicing income at least 30 days prior to the proposed eﬀective date.

examples: On 2026-08-01 a Seller/Servicer plans a servicing-rights pledge effective 2026-09-15; it must request prior approval no later than 2026-08-16 (30 days prior).

A lender requests consent 10 days before the effective date of a transfer of excess servicing compensation; the request timing violates the 30-day requirement and must be rescheduled or otherwise corrected.
A lender obtains written consent but submits the approval request 20 days before the effective date; the lender is still noncompliant with the ≥30 day lead-time requirement.
Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Payee-code security breach must be reported within 24 hours
business_rules.description: If a breach in the lender’s security arrangements (or failure to give timely notice of an error/omission/irregularity in payee arrangements) results in transfer instructions/transactions being initiated, the lender is solely liable for the transfers. The lender must notify Fannie Mae Asset Acquisitions by telephone and submit written confirmation of the call to Fannie Mae within 24 hours of the breach.
business_rules.conditions: When security breach or payee arrangement irregularity affects wire transfer instructions
business_rules.consequences: Lender remains liable for transfers; late notice may prevent suspension/correction actions
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C2-2-03, Procedures Related to the Secure Transfer of Funds
source_reference.source_text: A lender is solely liable for transfers that are initiated (either directly or indirectly) as the result of a breach in its security arrangements or as the result of its failure to give Fannie Mae timely notice of an error, omission, or irregularity in establishing payee arrangements. The lender is fully responsible for notifying the Fannie Mae Asset Acquisitions by telephone and submitting written conﬁrmation of the call to Fannie Mae within 24 hours of the breach.

examples: Fraudster compromises lender email and submits altered wire instructions; lender must call Asset Acquisitions and provide written confirmation within 24 hours.
Lender discovers incorrect payee code set-up that could route funds improperly; lender must provide timely notice and confirm in writing within 24 hours after detection of breach/irregularity.
Vendor system breach changes payee code file; lender remains solely liable for resulting transfers and must complete the 24-hour notification steps.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Lender liability for transfers; operational suspension/removal of payee codes

examples: Fannie Mae-approved Seller/Servicer with active approval
Seller/Servicer under probationary status
Seller/Servicer terminated for breach of obligations

business_rules.rule_name: Stand-Alone Policy Required for Excluded Master Peril
business_rules.description: If the master property insurance policy excludes or limits coverage for any required peril, the HOA or co-op corporation must obtain an acceptable stand-alone property insurance policy that provides adequate coverage for the excluded or limited peril.
business_rules.conditions: When a project master policy excludes/limits any required peril coverage
business_rules.consequences: Meets: peril coverage complete; Violates: coverage gap; must obtain stand-alone policy or loan may be ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: If a master property insurance policy excludes or limits coverage of any of the required perils, the HOA or co-op corporation must obtain an acceptable stand-alone property insurance policy which provides adequate coverage for the limited or excluded peril

examples: Master policy excludes sinkhole collapse; HOA must add a stand-alone sinkhole policy with adequate limits.
Master policy caps water damage at a low sublimit for the building; HOA must add a stand-alone policy/endorsement that adequately covers water damage.
Co-op master policy excludes windstorm; co-op must obtain stand-alone wind coverage before the loan can be delivered.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for delivery / potential repurchase demand

examples: One-unit single-family detached primary residence
Condominium unit in an eligible project
Property affected by a declared disaster requiring additional review

dependencies.rationale: A stand-alone peril policy (BR_SUBJECT_PROPERTY_PROCESS_023_003) is a cure for coverage gaps, not a cure for an unacceptable settlement basis (actual cash value). Therefore, workflows generally confirm the master policy meets replacement-cost requirements first before deciding whether a stand-alone policy is needed for exclusions/limitations.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If replacement-cost acceptability is not confirmed first, lenders may incorrectly attempt to cure an unacceptable master policy with add-on coverage, delaying remediation and risking closing/delivery timelines.

business_rules.rule_name: HomeStyle renovation must complete within 15 months; extension capped at 18 months
business_rules.description: For HomeStyle Renovation mortgages, renovation work must be completed no later than 15 months from the date the loan is closed. If a renovation project exceeds 15 months, the lender must submit information to Loan Quality Connect describing the circumstances and determine potential remedies. Remedies may include a limited extension (not to exceed 18 months from closing), curtailment of work, repurchase of the loan, or other remedies. Fannie Mae has sole discretion to determine the acceptable remedy when the timeframe exceeds 15 months.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to HomeStyle Renovation loans from closing through renovation completion.
business_rules.consequences: If >15 months, lender must submit to LQC; remedy may include extension ≤18 months or repurchase.
business_rules.exceptions: Extension beyond 15 months is rare and cannot exceed 18 months from closing.

source_reference.source_text: Renovation work must be completed no later than 15 months from date the loan is closed. In the rare circumstance a renovation project exceeds 15 months, the lender must submit this information to Loan Quality Connect to describe the circumstances resulting in the delay and determine potential remedies. These options may include a limited extension of the timeframe (not to exceed 18 months from the date the loan was closed), curtailment of the work to be completed, repurchase of the loan, or other remedies applicable to the speciﬁc circumstance. Fannie Mae has sole discretion

examples: Renovation completes 13 months after closing; meets the 15-month requirement and no LQC submission is needed.
Renovation delayed to 16 months; lender submits circumstances in Loan Quality Connect and receives an extension to 18 months.
Renovation projected to take 20 months; extension cannot exceed 18 months, so lender faces curtailment or repurchase remedy.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Repurchase or other remedy at Fannie Mae discretion

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_011 allows delivery immediately after closing even if renovation is not complete, but only with recourse and potential repurchase if default persists 120 days before completion. BR_019_010 requires completion within 15 months and outlines remedies/extension process. In practice, once a loan is delivered incomplete with recourse, the lender must then ensure the BR_019_010 timeline is managed and escalated appropriately.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_019_011 recourse delivery occurs but BR_019_010 monitoring is weak, projects can exceed 15 months without LQC submission/remedy selection, increasing repurchase/remedy exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Buydown account must be fully funded before delivery and cannot pay past due
business_rules.description: For loans with temporary interest rate buydowns, the lender must establish and fully fund the buydown account by the time the lender submits the mortgage to Fannie Mae for purchase or securitization. Buydown funds must be deposited into custodial bank accounts and cannot be included in accounts with the lender’s other corporate funds. Buydown funds cannot be used to pay past-due payments and cannot be used to reduce the mortgage amount for purposes of determining the LTV ratio.
business_rules.conditions: When submitting a temporary buydown loan to Fannie Mae for purchase/securitization
business_rules.consequences: If not funded/custodial or misused, delivery/eligibility defect and remedy risk
business_rules.exceptions: Refundability addressed only if paid off early (not covered in this rule)

source_reference.source_text: Buydown Funds ... Buydown accounts must be established and fully funded by the time the lender submits the mortgage to Fannie Mae for purchase or securitization. Funds for buydown accounts must be deposited into custodial bank accounts. Note: Buydown funds cannot be included in accounts with the lender’s other corporate funds. ... Buydown funds cannot be used to pay past-due payments. Buydown funds cannot be used to reduce the mortgage amount for purposes of determining the LTV ratio.

examples: Lender funds the buydown escrow account at closing and maintains it as a separate custodial account before delivery: compliant.
Lender delivers the loan while buydown account is partially funded: noncompliant; subject to delivery defect/remedy.
Borrower becomes 30 days past due; servicer attempts to use remaining buydown funds to cure delinquency: prohibited.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection / repurchase or make-whole remedy

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_028_006 defines whether a temporary buydown is permitted (3% max reduction, 1% annual increase max, ≤3-year period). BR_028_008 then prescribes how to establish and fund the buydown account by submission to Fannie Mae, including prohibitions on commingling and misuse. The operational process in BR_028_008 depends on the buydown being an allowed structure under BR_028_006.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_028_006 fails but BR_028_008 is followed, the lender may still attempt to deliver an ineligible buydown structure, resulting in purchase/securitization ineligibility.

business_rules.rule_name: Green loans must be sold or pooled within 5 years of certification
business_rules.description: For Green whole loans, the loan must be sold within five years of the property's certification year. For Green MBS pools, the pool must have a pool issuance year within five years of the property's certification year.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when delivering a loan as Green whole loan sale or Green MBS
business_rules.consequences: If met, Green delivery timing is satisfied; if not, loan/pool not Green eligible
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For whole loans: The loan must be sold within ﬁve years of the property's certiﬁcation year. For loans in MBS: The pool must have a pool issuance year within ﬁve years of the property's certiﬁcation year. Example : For a loan secured by a property with a green building certiﬁcation received in the year 2020, the loan must be sold to Fannie Mae by the end of 2025.

examples: Certification year 2020 and whole loan sold by 2025-12-31; timing requirement is met.
Certification year 2020 but whole loan sold in 2026; loan cannot be delivered as Green whole loan.
Certification year 2022 but MBS pool issuance year 2028; pool cannot be treated as Green MBS.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for Green MBS designation

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_029_001 defines what makes a loan Green MBS-eligible (approved certification within last five years and efficiency threshold). BR_029_002 then restricts sale/pool issuance timing to within five years of the certification year. The timing test assumes the existence of an eligible certification and its year.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_029_001 is not satisfied, BR_029_002 could be misapplied to classify a non-Green loan as Green based solely on dates, creating mislabeling/compliance defects for Green execution.

business_rules.rule_name: Alternative path required when P&I increases by more than 20%
business_rules.description: A high LTV refinance option loan must comply with the Alternative Qualification Path requirements if any specified trigger applies to the new loan, including when the P&I payment increases by more than 20% from the current P&I payment, when a borrower is excluded without death and remaining borrower(s) cannot evidence making payments on their own for the prior 12 months, or when the loan is a higher-priced mortgage loan/higher-priced covered transaction under Regulation Z.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to high LTV refinance option loans when listed triggers occur
business_rules.consequences: If triggered, lender must follow Alternative Path; if not followed, loan is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Exclusion due to death is excluded from the trigger

source_reference.source_text: If any of the following apply to the new loan... Then the loan... the P&I payment increases by more than 20% from the current P&I payment must comply with the Alternative Qualiﬁcation Path requirements. a borrower on the loan being reﬁnanced is being excluded from the new loan other than due to death, and the remaining borrower(s) cannot provide evidence of making payments on their own for the prior 12 months the loan is a higher-priced mortgage loan or a higher-priced covered transaction under Regulation Z

examples: Current P&I is $1,200 and new P&I is $1,500 (+25%); loan must be underwritten using Alternative Qualification Path rules.
A co-borrower is removed for divorce (not death) and remaining borrower cannot document making payments alone for prior 12 months; Alternative Path is required.
Refinance is classified as a higher-priced covered transaction under Reg Z; Alternative Path is required even if P&I change is small.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for delivery or subject to loan repurchase demand

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Alternative Qualification Path loans require manual underwriting in 2025
business_rules.description: For loans subject to the Alternative Qualification Path, the lender is required to manually underwrite the loan (i.e., manual underwriting is required for all loans subject to the path).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when high LTV refinance loan is subject to Alternative Qualification Path
business_rules.consequences: If performed, underwriting method complies; if not, loan is noncompliant/ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Veriﬁed assets needed to close, when applicable. The lender is required to manually underwrite all loans subject to the Alternative Qualiﬁcation Path.

examples: P&I increases by 22%; lender completes a full manual underwrite and documents the file accordingly; requirement is met.
Loan is HPML under Reg Z and lender relies solely on DU Approve/Eligible without manual underwriting; requirement is violated.
Borrower excluded (not due to death) and remaining borrower lacks 12-month solo payment evidence; manual underwriting is required under the path.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for delivery or subject to loan repurchase demand

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_029_004 states that a high LTV refinance option loan must comply with Alternative Qualification Path requirements if certain triggers apply. BR_029_007 states that for loans subject to the Alternative Qualification Path, manual underwriting is required. Thus, the trigger determination is the gating decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_029_004 triggers are missed, the lender may incorrectly underwrite via DU/standard path instead of manual underwriting, creating ineligibility and compliance defects.

business_rules.rule_name: Delinquent taxes and liens must be paid off by closing
business_rules.description: Delinquent credit items that could affect Fannie Mae’s lien position or diminish borrower equity—including taxes, judgments, charge-offs of non-mortgage accounts (subject to stated exceptions), tax liens, mechanic’s/materialmen’s liens, and similar liens—must be paid off at or prior to closing.
business_rules.conditions: When delinquent credit (taxes/judgments/liens) exists and could affect lien position/equity
business_rules.consequences: If paid off by closing, loan may proceed; if not paid, loan is not compliant and may be ineligible/subject to repurchase
business_rules.exceptions: Manual underwrite thresholds for non-medical collections/charge-offs (see separate rule)

source_reference.section_id: B3-6-07, Collections, Charge-Offs, Judgments, and Liens
source_reference.source_text: Delinquent credit—including taxes, judgments, charge-oﬀs of non-mortgage accounts (see below for exceptions), tax liens, mechanic's or materialmen’s liens, and liens that have the potential to aﬀect Fannie Mae’s lien position or diminish the borrower’s equity—must be paid oﬀ at or prior to closing.

examples: Borrower has a recorded mechanic’s lien; lender requires payoff and lien release prior to or at closing.
Borrower has delinquent property taxes; taxes are paid off at closing and documented on the closing statement.
Borrower has an outstanding judgment; lender must obtain satisfaction of judgment at or prior to closing to comply.
30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Senior UCC Fixture Filing for Solar Panels Must Be Subordinated
business_rules.description: When solar panels are financed and treated as fixtures via a UCC fixture filing recorded in land records, any UCC fixture filing that is senior in priority to the mortgage loan must be subordinated to preserve first-lien position.
business_rules.conditions: Solar panel financing includes a UCC fixture filing recorded in land records
business_rules.consequences: If not subordinated, first-lien position may be impaired and loan may be noncompliant/ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B2-3-04 (Solar panels underwriting table: fixture filing)
source_reference.source_text: Note: If a UCC ﬁxture ﬁling* is in the land records as a priority senior to the mortgage loan, it must be subordinated.

examples: Title shows a recorded UCC-1 fixture filing for solar panels dated before the mortgage; lender obtains executed subordination agreement → compliant.
UCC fixture filing remains senior and cannot be subordinated → lender should not deliver the loan to Fannie Mae due to lien priority risk.
Solar financing is paid off and fixture filing is terminated/released before closing → no subordination needed because no senior fixture lien remains.
30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Annual Property Insurance Verification Required for Eligibility
business_rules.description: The servicer must verify at least annually that any property insurance policy for a one- to four-unit property or a unit in a project development meets Fannie Mae's requirements. If a lender is unable to determine whether the applicable insurance policy meets Fannie Mae's requirements, the associated loan is not eligible for purchase by Fannie Mae.
business_rules.conditions: Loan requires property insurance for 1–4 unit or project unit collateral
business_rules.consequences: If verified annually and compliant, eligible; if unverifiable/noncompliant, loan is ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The servicer must verify at least annually that any property insurance policy for a one- to four-unit property or a unit in a project development meets Fannie Mae's requirements. Note:  If a lender is unable to determine whether the applicable insurance policy meets Fannie Mae's requirements, the associated loan is not eligible for purchase by Fannie Mae.

examples: Servicer performs annual review confirming coverage amount, deductibles, and paid premiums; policy meets requirements and loan remains eligible.
Lender receives an incomplete insurance binder and cannot determine whether terms meet requirements; loan becomes ineligible for purchase.
Project unit: annual verification confirms master policy covers unit; requirement satisfied.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for purchase / potential repurchase demand

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Whole loan first payment date within 2 months of disbursement
business_rules.description: For whole loans delivered to Fannie Mae, the first payment date must be no later than two months from the final disbursement date of the loan proceeds. For a single-close construction-to-permanent loan, the two-month period begins at the time of conversion to permanent financing.
business_rules.conditions: When delivering a whole loan to Fannie Mae and setting the first payment date
business_rules.consequences: If first payment date exceeds 2 months, delivery is not compliant with schedule requirement
business_rules.exceptions: Construction-to-permanent: 2 months measured from conversion to permanent financing

source_reference.section_id: C2-2-01, Amortization Schedule Requirements for Whole Loans
source_reference.source_text: For whole loans delivered to Fannie Mae, the ﬁrst payment date must be no later than two months from the ﬁnal disbursement date of the loan proceeds. In the case of a single-close construction-to-permanent loan, the two month period begins at the time of the conversion to permanent ﬁnancing.

examples: Final disbursement occurs on 2026-01-15; first payment date must be no later than 2026-03-15.
Final disbursement occurs on 2026-11-30; first payment date must be no later than 2027-01-30.
Single-close construction-to-permanent converts on 2026-06-10; first payment date must be no later than 2026-08-10 even if earlier construction draws occurred.
30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_010 states the seller/servicer “may not deliver a loan to Fannie Mae if any borrower is on an OFAC sanctions list.” Therefore, even if BR_038_009 first payment date requirements are met, delivery must not proceed when OFAC prohibits it.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_005_010 fails, a prohibited loan may be delivered, creating sanctions risk and severe enforcement exposure. If BR_005_010 triggers correctly, it stops the delivery workflow regardless of BR_038_009 compliance.

dependencies.rationale: BR_005_008 explicitly states, “Before selling a loan secured by a unit in such a project, the lender must confirm the project retains its approved status as of the note date...” Delivery processes like BR_038_009 (whole loan delivery schedule setup) should only proceed once this eligibility gating is satisfied for affected loans.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_005_008 fails, the lender may deliver/sell a loan secured by a now-ineligible project, leading to delivery rejection, repurchase/remedy exposure, and governance findings for late CPM notification.

business_rules.rule_name: Lender-paid MI must be kept until paid in full and delivered with SFC 019
business_rules.description: When providing lender-purchased mortgage insurance (LPMI), the lender must: (i) pay for MI as a corporate obligation with an initial premium and renewal premiums (or acceptable lump-sum life-of-loan plan); (ii) increase servicing compensation by at least the MI renewal premium amount (not required for lump-sum life-of-loan plans); (iii) keep MI coverage in effect until the mortgage is paid in full; and (iv) deliver the loan with Special Feature Code (SFC) 019.
business_rules.conditions: Loan uses lender-purchased MI; applies through delivery and ongoing coverage maintenance
business_rules.consequences: Failure can cause delivery issues, servicing transfer defects, and contractual remedies
business_rules.exceptions: Servicing comp increase not required for lump-sum life-of-loan MI plans

source_reference.source_text: When providing lender-purchased mortgage insurance, the lender must: pay for the mortgage insurance coverage as a corporate obligation with an initial premium and renewal premiums for each subsequent period of coverage... Lump-sum premium plans that provide coverage for the life-of-the-mortgage loan also are acceptable; increase the servicing compensation... by at least the amount of the mortgage insurance renewal premium. (This is not required for lump-sum premium plans...); keep the mortgage insurance coverage in eﬀect until the mortgage is paid in full; deliver the loan with SFC 019;

examples: Monthly-renewal LPMI: lender pays initial premium + monthly renewals, increases servicing comp by at least the renewal premium, and delivers loan with SFC 019.
Single-premium life-of-loan LPMI: lender does not need to increase servicing compensation but still must deliver with SFC 019 and keep coverage in effect until payoff.
Lender delivers an LPMI loan without SFC 019: delivery data defect requiring correction and potential remedy exposure.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection or repurchase demand

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Revolving balance paid off may be excluded from DTI
business_rules.description: If a revolving account balance will be paid off at or prior to closing, the monthly payment on the current outstanding balance does not need to be included in long-term debt/DTI. The revolving account does not need to be closed as a condition of excluding the payment from DTI.
business_rules.conditions: When a revolving account will be paid off at or prior to closing
business_rules.consequences: If met, exclude revolving payment from DTI; if violated, DTI calculation is incorrect and may trigger DU resubmission/defect
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B3-6-07, Payoff or Paydown of Debt for Qualification
source_reference.source_text: If a revolving account balance is to be paid oﬀ at or prior to closing, a monthly payment on the current outstanding balance does not need to be included in the borrower's long-term debt, i.e., not included in the debt-to-income (DTI) ratio. Such accounts do not need to be closed as a condition of excluding the payment from the DTI ratio.

examples: Borrower pays off a $4,000 credit card at closing; lender excludes the credit card payment from DTI and does not require account closure.
Borrower intends to pay off a revolving balance but cannot document payoff at/before closing; lender includes the payment in DTI.
Borrower pays off two revolving cards at closing; lender excludes both payments without requiring either account to be closed.
U.S. citizen borrower purchasing an owner-occupied home
Permanent resident borrower applying for a conforming loan
Borrower with multiple financed properties seeking an additional mortgage

business_rules.rule_name: Homeownership education required before closing for >95% LTV
business_rules.description: At least one borrower on the loan must complete homeownership education prior to loan closing for specific purchase-related scenarios: (1) purchase underwritten in DU where none of the borrowers has at least one credit account or installment account on the credit report (as instructed by DU); (2) manually underwritten purchase where all borrowers do not have a credit score; (3) HomeReady purchase when all occupying borrowers are first-time homebuyers; or (4) purchase transactions with LTV, CLTV, or HCLTV ratios greater than 95% when all borrowers are first-time homebuyers. These requirements also apply to construction-to-permanent transactions processed as a purchase.
business_rules.conditions: When processing a purchase (or CTP treated as purchase) meeting listed triggers
business_rules.consequences: If not completed pre-closing, loan is noncompliant with Guide requirements
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; can satisfy via housing counseling in lieu of education

source_reference.source_text: Transactions that Require Homeownership Education For the following transactions, at least one borrower on the loan must complete homeownership education prior to loan closing: if none of the borrowers on a purchase transaction underwritten in DU has at least one credit account or installment account reported on their credit report, regardless of the loan product or whether the borrowers are ﬁrst-time homebuyers (as instructed by DU); if all borrowers on a manually underwritten purchase transaction do not have a credit score, regardless of the loan product or whether the borrowers are ﬁrst-time homebuyers; HomeReady purchase transactions when all occupying borrowers are ﬁrst-time homebuyers; or purchase transactions with LTV, CLTV, or HCLTV ratios greater than 95%, when all borrowers are ﬁrst-time homebuyers. Note: The requirements that apply to purchases also apply to construction-to-permanent transactions that are processed as a purchase.

examples: DU purchase: both borrowers have no reported credit accounts; one borrower completes education before closing: compliant.
Manual underwrite purchase: all borrowers are non-scored; no education completed before closing: noncompliant.
First-time HomeReady purchase: all occupying borrowers are first-time; one borrower completes housing counseling instead: compliant (in lieu of education).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Post-purchase remedial action / repurchase exposure for eligibility breach

examples: Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

dependencies.rationale: BR_028_003 requires homeownership education in scenarios such as no credit accounts on the credit report or no credit score. Those determinations depend on having a compliant credit report. BR_032_006 defines acceptability of the in-file credit report used for underwriting; without it, the lender cannot reliably determine whether education is required.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_032_006 fails (e.g., incomplete repository coverage), the lender may incorrectly skip required education or impose it unnecessarily, causing compliance defects or borrower friction and closing delays.

dependencies.rationale: One education trigger in BR_028_003 is specifically "as instructed by DU." BR_008_001 requires retention of the final DU Underwriting Analysis Report. In compliance workflows, the DU report is the evidentiary artifact that supports that the education trigger applied (or did not).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_008_001 is not met, the lender may be unable to demonstrate that the DU-based education requirement was correctly applied, leading to post-close audit findings even if education was completed.

business_rules.rule_name: Credit documents older than 4 months must be updated
business_rules.description: If credit documents are older than allowed under the four-month age requirement, the lender must update the credit documents before proceeding.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when any required credit document exceeds 4 months on note date
business_rules.consequences: Updated docs restore eligibility; failure leaves file noncompliant/stale
business_rules.exceptions: Disaster-impact exceptions referenced elsewhere (B2-3-05)

source_reference.section_id: B1-1-03, Allowable Age of Credit Documents
source_reference.source_text: If the credit documents are older than allowed, the lender must update them. For age requirements related to appraisals, see B4-1.2-04, Appraisal Age and Use Requirements. Also see B2-3-05, Properties Affected by a Disaster for exceptions to the allowable age of credit documents for loans impacted by a natural disaster.

examples: Credit report is 140 days old at note date; lender re-pulls a new credit report prior to closing.
Most recent bank statement is 5 months old; lender obtains a more recent statement or alternative current verification.
Loan impacted by a declared disaster: lender applies the separate disaster exception guidance instead of standard updating.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting condition; potential post-purchase defect if not updated

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_008_004 defines the age limit. BR_008_005 states that if documents are older than allowed under that requirement, the lender must update them before proceeding. Therefore BR_008_004 condition outcome controls applicability and necessity of BR_008_005.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_008_004 fails and BR_008_005 is not executed, the file proceeds with stale credit/income/asset/employment documentation, creating clear compliance defects and potential eligibility issues at delivery.

business_rules.rule_name: Square Footage Must Follow ANSI Z765-2021 for Hybrid Appraisals
business_rules.description: Appraisers must follow the ANSI Z765-2021 standard when measuring, calculating, and reporting above- and below-grade square footage for attached and detached single-family dwellings, including manufactured homes. This applies to all hybrid appraisals and appraisals requiring interior and exterior inspections. If state law or another regulatory requirement mandates a different standard, the appraisal report must note the required standard and explain how it was applied.
business_rules.conditions: When reporting above-/below-grade square footage for eligible SFR appraisals
business_rules.consequences: Meets: measurement compliant; Violates: appraisal defect; may require revision/replacement and impact deliverability
business_rules.exceptions: If state/reg mandates different standard, it must be noted and explained

source_reference.source_text: Appraisers must follow the American National Standard Institute (ANSI) Square Footage-Method for Calculating : ANSI® Z765-2021 ("ANSI standard") when measuring, calculating and reporting the above- and below-grade square footage(s). This includes ﬁnished and unﬁnished area(s) of the subject for attached and detached single- family dwellings, including manufactured homes. This is applicable to all hybrid appraisals and appraisals requiring interior and exterior inspections. If state law or a regulatory requirement mandates adherence to a diﬀerent measurement standard, the appraisal report must note the standard required and explain how it was applied.

examples: Hybrid appraisal on a manufactured home: appraiser reports GLA using ANSI Z765-2021; lender accepts as compliant.
Appraiser uses a non-ANSI local method without noting a state requirement; lender must require correction/revision to meet ANSI requirement.
State law mandates a different measurement standard; appraiser states the mandated standard in the report and explains application—acceptable under the exception.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Appraisal revision/replacement; potential repurchase demand if not cured

examples: State-certified residential appraiser engaged for a purchase transaction
Appraiser meeting lender selection criteria and independence requirements
Appraiser rejected due to unacceptable appraisal practice indicators

business_rules.rule_name: CLT appraisal must use 3-step leasehold valuation method
business_rules.description: For a community land trust (CLT) ground lease, the appraiser must develop the value opinion using a required three-step process: (1) determine fee simple value using Sales Comparison; (2) determine a market-derived capitalization rate and convert annual ground rent into leased fee value; (3) determine leasehold value by reducing fee simple value by leased fee value. When using this technique, the appraiser does not need to document actual land value of the security property.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when subject property is under CLT ground lease/leasehold estate
business_rules.consequences: If followed, valuation method is Guide-compliant; if not, appraisal is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The appraiser must use a three-step process to develop an opinion of value. Step The appraiser must determine 1 the fee simple value of the property by using the sales comparison analysis approach to value, 2the applicable capitalization rate and convert the income from the ground lease into a leased fee value by using the market-derived capitalization rate, and 3the leasehold value by reducing the fee simple value by the lease fee value.

examples: CLT purchase: appraiser first estimates fee simple value at $250,000 using fee simple comps, then capitalizes ground rent to leased fee value, then subtracts to reach leasehold value.
CLT loan file where appraiser only reports contract price without fee simple analysis: fails required 3-step process and is a material appraisal defect.
CLT appraisal where appraiser documents land value but skips leased fee calculation: noncompliant because step 2/3 are required for leasehold valuation.
Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (URAR) with UAD-compliant fields
Appraisal report reused within permitted age and use requirements
Appraisal report flagged for unacceptable practices or missing exhibits

dependencies.rationale: BR_014_001 mandates determining fee simple value using Sales Comparison. BR_014_002 specifies which comparables may/may not be used for that fee simple value (must use similar fee simple properties; CLT leasehold sales must not be used as comps). Therefore, compliant execution of BR_014_001’s Step 1 requires BR_014_002 compliance.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_014_002 is violated, the fee simple value conclusion in BR_014_001 is noncompliant, contaminating the entire 3-step CLT valuation. Appraisal is deficient and may require revision/replacement; delivery may be blocked.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SUBJECT_PROPERTY_ELIGIBILITY_011_001 establishes that leasehold collateral can be eligible only under specific conditions (market acceptance and eligible property types). When the leasehold is specifically a CLT ground lease, the appraisal must be developed using the CLT three-step process in BR_APPRAISAL_REPORT_PROCESS_014_001. In practice, the eligibility determination and underwriting acceptance for leasehold/CLT is highly dependent on having a Guide-compliant value opinion that reflects the correct property interest (leasehold vs fee simple).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If leasehold/CLT status is identified but the CLT appraisal process is not followed: (1) appraisal is deficient, (2) underwriter may be unable to establish acceptable collateral value/interest, (3) delivery to Fannie Mae may be suspended pending appraisal correction, (4) higher repurchase/indemnification risk if delivered with a defective appraisal.

business_rules.rule_name: HomeStyle Refresh requires completion escrow and 180-day completion
business_rules.description: For HomeStyle Refresh loans with incomplete improvements, the lender must establish a completion escrow for the improvement funds, and the improvements must be completed no later than 180 days from the date of the mortgage note.
business_rules.conditions: HomeStyle Refresh loan has incomplete improvements at closing/delivery
business_rules.consequences: If escrow set and work completes by 180 days, loan stays compliant; else defect
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The lender must establish a completion escrow for incomplete improvements. The improvements must be completed no later than 180 days from the date of the mortgage note.

examples: Loan closes on 2026-04-01 with $12,000 in incomplete improvements → completion escrow required; all work must be finished by 2026-09-28.
Lender fails to set up completion escrow at closing → noncompliance; subject to QC defect and remedies.
Escrow is set, but improvements remain incomplete after 180 days → violates requirement and may trigger repurchase exposure.
Escrow account established for property taxes and homeowners insurance
Escrow waiver granted where permitted and documented
Escrow analysis identifying shortage and updated monthly payment

business_rules.rule_name: QC data discrepancies require DU resubmission or manual risk assessment
business_rules.description: When post-closing QC finds inconsistencies between DU (or manual underwriting) data and source documents, the lender must (1) determine if differences are within Selling Guide/DU tolerances, (2) resubmit the loan to DU with corrected data when outside DU tolerances, and (3) if DU resubmission is not possible, manually perform a comprehensive risk assessment using DU-required documentation (unless the loan is a DU-only product). If a loan exceeds DU tolerances and is not resubmitted to DU, the DU limited waiver of representations and warranties is invalidated.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when QC identifies data/source-document inconsistencies post-closing.
business_rules.consequences: If outside tolerances: must resubmit/assess; if not, DU waiver invalidated.
business_rules.exceptions: DU-only products are not eligible for manual comprehensive risk assessment.

source_reference.source_text: When there are inconsistencies between the data and/or information submitted to DU (or used for manual underwriting) and the source documents, the lender must complete the steps in the following table. Step The lender must.... 1 determine whether discrepancies are within the tolerances permitted by the Selling Guide... 2 resubmit the loan to DU with the correct data, if discrepancies are outside the DU allowed tolerances... The DU limited waiver of representations and warranties is invalidated when loans that exceed DU tolerances are not resubmitted to DU... Note: DU-only products are not eligible for a manual comprehensive risk assessment.

examples: QC finds employment start date entered in DU is incorrect and outside DU tolerances; lender corrects and resubmits to DU, then re-determines eligibility.
QC finds asset balance mismatch outside tolerances; lender cannot resubmit DU due to closed casefile, so performs comprehensive manual risk assessment using DU-required docs (if not DU-only).
QC finds DTI error outside tolerances and lender does not resubmit to DU; DU limited waiver of representations and warranties is invalidated.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loss of DU limited waiver; potential repurchase/other remedies

examples: Lender QC program performing pre-funding and post-closing audits
QC program with documented defect categories and corrective action tracking
QC program updated to reflect new appraisal or DU policy changes

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_003 is scoped to "DU (or manual underwriting)" and directs DU resubmission when outside DU tolerances. Therefore, when the underwriting method is DU, BR_006_006 must have occurred to create a DU casefile and baseline data against which QC compares.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_006_006 was not satisfied for a DU-intended loan, QC cannot perform DU tolerance comparisons or resubmissions properly, and any DU waiver/tolerance reliance may be invalidated, increasing defect and remedy risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Ineligible-as-delivered loans must be self-reported in Loan Quality Connect
business_rules.description: If, after QC data verification and underwriting reassessment, the lender determines a loan was not eligible as delivered to Fannie Mae, the lender must advise Fannie Mae of the findings using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when QC reassessment determines the loan was not eligible as delivered.
business_rules.consequences: Must self-report via Loan Quality Connect; failure may worsen remedies/exposure.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.
