source_reference.source_text: 4 document the underwriting ﬁle to reﬂect its decision when the lender determines the loan remains eligible as delivered. 5 advise Fannie Mae of these ﬁndings using the self-report functionality in Loan Quality Connect when the lender determines the loan was not eligible as delivered.

examples: QC discovers undisclosed debt that changes eligibility; lender determines not eligible as delivered and self-reports via Loan Quality Connect.
QC identifies income documentation does not support DU inputs; after reassessment loan fails Selling Guide requirements, so lender self-reports.
QC finds appraisal documentation missing and cannot be cured; lender self-reports the delivery ineligibility through Loan Quality Connect.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Potential repurchase demand or other remedies following self-report

examples: Lender QC program performing pre-funding and post-closing audits
QC program with documented defect categories and corrective action tracking
QC program updated to reflect new appraisal or DU policy changes

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_004 begins "If, after QC data verification and underwriting reassessment..." which is the workflow defined in BR_019_003 (tolerance check, DU resubmission, or manual risk assessment). Therefore BR_019_003 is the prerequisite process that produces the determination that triggers BR_019_004.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_019_003 is not performed correctly, the lender may fail to identify ineligible delivered loans, leading to unreported defects, increased remedy severity, and heightened repurchase/indemnification exposure. If ineligibility is identified but BR_019_004 is not executed, reporting noncompliance may worsen outcomes.

business_rules.rule_name: First DU underwriting submission must occur before closing
business_rules.description: Loans may be submitted to DU before or after closing; however, the first submission to DU for underwriting purposes must occur before closing of the mortgage loan.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when underwriting method is DU for a mortgage loan application.
business_rules.consequences: If first DU submission occurs after closing, loan fails DU process requirement and may be ineligible for delivery.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text:
Loans may be
submitted to DU before or after the closing of the mortgage loan; however, the ﬁrst submission to DU for
underwriting purposes must occur before closing of the mortgage loan.

examples: Loan is submitted to DU, receives Approve/Eligible, then closes: compliant (first submission pre-closing).
Loan closes first and is then submitted to DU for the first time: noncompliant with this DU timing requirement.
Loan is submitted to DU pre-closing, then resubmitted post-closing prior to delivery: allowed (first submission was pre-closing).
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit must requirement for first DU submission relative to closing.

examples: DU evaluates an application and generates an Approve/Eligible recommendation.
DU evaluates an application and returns Refer with Caution requiring manual underwriting.

business_rules.rule_name: DU resubmission required when DTI exceeds 45% or rises 3 points
business_rules.description: When certain changes occur (e.g., interest rate increase, new debts, verified income lower than submitted), the lender must resubmit the loan casefile to DU if the lender’s recalculated DTI would (1) exceed 45% OR (2) increase by 3 percentage points or more when the recalculated DTI is 50% or less.
business_rules.conditions: When post-submission changes require DTI recalculation based on verified/updated data
business_rules.consequences: If trigger met, DU resubmission is required; if not resubmitted, underwriting decision is unsupported and loan may be defective
business_rules.exceptions: None stated (applies to listed trigger events and DTI thresholds)

source_reference.section_id: B3-2-10, Ensuring DU Data and Delivery Information Accuracy
source_reference.source_text: DU loans — the result of these changes causes the DTI ratio recalculated by the lender to • now exceed 45%, or • increase by 3 percentage points or more (if the recalculated DTI ratio is 50% or less) * See resubmission examples below. Loan caseﬁle must be resubmitted to DU

examples: Original DTI 44%; new debt discovered increases recalculated DTI to 46% (>45%); lender must resubmit to DU.
Original DTI 35%; verified income lower increases recalculated DTI to 38% (increase 3 points and ≤50%); lender must resubmit.
Original DTI 46%; recalculated DTI becomes 48% (increase 2 points); resubmission is not required by this DTI trigger.
DU evaluates an application and generates an Approve/Eligible recommendation.
DU evaluates an application and returns Refer with Caution requiring manual underwriting.

dependencies.rationale: BR_032_010 requires resubmitting the loan casefile to DU when certain changes occur and DTI thresholds trigger. This can only happen if the loan has already been submitted to DU under BR_006_006.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_006_006 is not satisfied, the resubmission control (BR_032_010) cannot be executed, causing stale underwriting findings and unsupported approval/delivery.

business_rules.rule_name: Cash or MBS cannot be requested until 1 business day after disbursement
business_rules.description: For purchase money loans, and for loans where a right of rescission has been waived, the lender may not request or receive cash or MBS until at least one business day after the lender disburses the funds to (or on behalf of) the borrower.
business_rules.conditions: Purchase money loans or rescission-waived loans; lender has disbursed borrower funds
business_rules.consequences: Requesting/receiving cash or MBS earlier breaches delivery requirements/remedies apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the cited passage

source_reference.source_text: For purchase money loans and also for loans that have a right of rescission that has been waived, a lender may not request or receive cash or MBS until at least one business day after the lender disburses the funds to (or on behalf of) the borrower.

examples: Purchase loan funds disbursed Monday; lender requests cash/MBS Monday afternoon: violates the 1 business day waiting period.
Purchase loan funds disbursed Monday; lender requests cash/MBS Tuesday: meets the “at least one business day after” requirement.
Rescission waived on a refinance; disbursement occurs Thursday; lender requests MBS Friday: meets the 1 business day timing.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension of deliveries, indemnification, or repurchase demand

examples: Contract requires record retention and examination of mortgage files for all delivered loans.
Contract defines remedies available to Fannie Mae for material defects.

business_rules.rule_name: Required disclosure needs 10 business days notice to Fannie Mae
business_rules.description: If a Seller/Servicer is required by applicable law to disclose Fannie Mae Confidential Information, it must use reasonable efforts to give Fannie Mae notice at least ten business days prior to disclosure, disclose only the legally required portion (as determined by legal counsel), and request confidentiality. The notice requirement is waived for confidential disclosures to governmental agencies/regulators/self-regulatory authorities with oversight authority if the Seller/Servicer formally requests confidential/FOIA-exempt treatment.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer must disclose Confidential Information by law
business_rules.consequences: If met: controlled lawful disclosure; if violated: Fannie Mae may seek injunction
business_rules.exceptions: Notice waived for confidential regulator requests with FOIA-exempt request

source_reference.source_text: Note : These restrictions do not apply to the extent the seller/servicer is required to disclose the Conﬁdential Information by applicable law, provided that the seller/servicer • uses all reasonable eﬀorts to give Fannie Mae notice at least ten business days prior to such disclosure; and • discloses only that portion of the Conﬁdential Information that the seller/servicer’s legal counsel determines is legally required to be furnished, and request that the information remain conﬁdential. The requirement to provide Fannie Mae notice is waived if the seller/servicer • is required by law to disclose in conﬁdence Conﬁdential Information in response to requests from a governmental agency, regulator, or self-regulatory authority...

examples: Subpoena requires limited borrower NPI disclosure: Seller/Servicer provides notice ≥10 business days before responding and limits disclosure to required items.
Regulator examination requests confidential production: Seller/Servicer does not provide 10-day notice but formally requests FOIA/open-records exemption.
Seller/Servicer discloses Fannie Mae proprietary pricing to a third party without approval and without required notice: Fannie Mae may seek immediate equitable relief.
Contract requires record retention and examination of mortgage files for all delivered loans.
Contract defines remedies available to Fannie Mae for material defects.

business_rules.rule_name: ARM fixed period 5 years or less must use ARM qualifying rate
business_rules.description: To limit potential payment shock, lenders must qualify borrowers using the ARM qualifying rate described in B3-6-04 for any ARM with an initial fixed-rate period of five years or less. This underwriting qualification requirement applies when generating the underwriting decision for such ARMs.
business_rules.conditions: ARM initial fixed-rate period is 5 years or less during underwriting
business_rules.consequences: If qualified per ARM qualifying rate, decision is compliant; if not, underwriting defect
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Lenders must limit the impact of any potential payment shock on an ARM with an initial ﬁxed-rate period of ﬁve years or less by qualifying borrowers based on the qualifying rate described in B3-6-04, Qualifying Payment Requirements .

examples: Borrower applies for a 5/1 ARM; lender qualifies using the B3-6-04 ARM qualifying rate, not just the introductory rate.
Borrower applies for a 3/1 ARM; qualifying uses the ARM qualifying rate to assess post-intro payment increase risk.
Lender qualifies a 5/1 ARM using only the teaser rate payment; QC later flags a payment shock qualifying defect.
DU generates Approve/Eligible decision with standard documentation conditions.
DU generates Approve/Ineligible decision requiring changes to achieve eligibility.

business_rules.rule_name: Repurchase Not Required Until 30 Days After Litigation Ends
business_rules.description: If Fannie Mae issues a repurchase demand for a failure to comply with laws while litigation on the same issue is pending, or while a government agency with authority to decide the issue has publicly stated it is reviewing it, the Seller/Servicer is not required to repurchase the loan until 30 days after the matter is resolved (dismissed/settled/concluded at trial/adjudication) or the agency issues a final determination. After resolution, the Seller/Servicer may request Fannie Mae to review appropriateness of the demand, and Fannie Mae will withdraw where appropriate.
business_rules.conditions: When repurchase demand is issued for compliance-with-laws issue with pending litigation/agency review
business_rules.consequences: Repurchase obligation is deferred; after resolution, demand may be reviewed/withdrawn
business_rules.exceptions: Applies only when the same issue is in litigation or under public agency review

source_reference.section_id: A3-2-01 (12/10/2025), Repurchase demands for compliance with laws violations
source_reference.source_text: If Fannie Mae issues a repurchase demand involving a failure to comply with laws when there is pending litigation underway for that same issue, or when a government agency with authority to make a determination regarding the issue has publicly stated that it is reviewing the issue, the seller/servicer is not required to repurchase the loan until 30 days after the litigation is • dismissed, • settled, or • concluded at trial in an adjudication, or • the government agency has made a ﬁnal determination (collectively, the “Resolution”). After the Resolution, the seller/servicer may request that Fannie Mae review the appropriateness of the repurchase demand in light of the Resolution. Fannie Mae will withdraw the repurchase demand where appropriate.

examples: Repurchase demand issued while state AG lawsuit is pending on the same practice; seller waits until 30 days after final settlement before repurchasing.
Agency publicly states it is reviewing the issue; seller defers repurchase until 30 days after the agency’s final determination is issued.
After litigation is dismissed, seller requests Fannie Mae review; Fannie Mae withdraws demand where appropriate based on the resolution.
Fannie Mae issues repurchase request due to ineligible loan attribute discovered in QC.
Fannie Mae issues make-whole request based on appraisal-related defect findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Repurchase demand appeals: 60 days first, 15 days second, 30 days impasse
business_rules.description: A lender may submit a written appeal of a repurchase/make-whole “demand.” The lender must submit a first appeal in writing within 60 days of receiving the demand, and Fannie Mae must respond in writing within 60 days of receipt. If the first appeal is denied and the lender has additional material information, the lender may submit a second appeal in writing within 15 days of receiving the denial; Fannie Mae must respond within 60 days. If challenging the existence of the defect after the first or second appeal, the lender must initiate the impasse process in writing within 15 days of receiving Fannie Mae’s denial; then Fannie Mae and the lender have 30 days to attempt to resolve (unless mutually extended).
business_rules.conditions: After Fannie Mae issues a demand and lender elects appeal/impasse processes
business_rules.consequences: Meeting timelines preserves appeal rights; missing deadlines risks losing escalation options
business_rules.exceptions: Fannie may specify a shorter or longer first-appeal period in the demand

source_reference.source_text: First Appeal: A lender may submit a written appeal of a demand.The lender must submit an appeal in writing within 60 days of receiving a demand. Note: Fannie Mae, in its discretion, may identify a shorter or longer appeal period in the demand... Fannie Mae must respond in writing to the lender’s appeal within 60 days of its receipt. Second Appeal: ... The lender must submit a second appeal in writing within 15 days of receiving a denial of the ﬁrst appeal... Impasse: ... The lender must initiate the impasse process in writing within 15 days of receiving Fannie Mae’s denial... Fannie Mae and the lender will have 30 days in which to attempt to resolve the dispute...

examples: Demand received 2026-02-01; lender must submit first appeal by 2026-04-02 (60 days). Fannie Mae must respond within 60 days of receiving the appeal.
First appeal denied 2026-05-15; lender has additional material information and must file second appeal by 2026-05-30 (15 days).
After denial, lender initiates impasse on day 20; it misses the 15-day impasse initiation deadline and risks losing that challenge route.
Fannie Mae issues repurchase request due to ineligible loan attribute discovered in QC.
Fannie Mae issues make-whole request based on appraisal-related defect findings.

dependencies.rationale: BR_ISSUES_REPURCHASE_REQUEST_PROCESS_038_010 defines timelines to appeal a repurchase/make-whole demand. Without the issuance of a demand under BR_ISSUES_REPURCHASE_REQUEST_COMPLIANCE_008_012, there is no appealable event and no appeal clock to start.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the system does not anchor the appeal workflow to an issued demand date, the lender may miss the 60-day/15-day deadlines and lose escalation rights, forcing repurchase/make-whole without further process.

business_rules.rule_name: Revised appraisal must be 100% Successful in UCDP before loan sale
business_rules.description: If an appraisal report is revised after the original, the final version that was used to make the underwriting decision must be submitted through UCDP and must receive a “Successful” status prior to sale of the loan to Fannie Mae. When submitting through UCDP, the lender must ensure the appraisal is the unaltered report submitted by the identified appraiser.
business_rules.conditions: Appraisal has subsequent revisions; final version used for underwriting decision
business_rules.consequences: Without UCDP Successful final version, loan must not be sold to Fannie Mae
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the cited passage

source_reference.section_id: B4-1.2-01, UCDP submission requirement for revised appraisal
source_reference.source_text: If there are subsequent revisions to the appraisal report, the ﬁnal version of the report that was utilized in making the underwriting decision must be submitted through the UCDP and receive a “Successful” status from the UCDP prior to sale of the loan to Fannie Mae. When submitting an appraisal report through the UCDP, lenders must ensure it is the unaltered report submitted by the identiﬁed appraiser.

examples: Appraisal updated after reconsideration of value; lender sells loan without resubmitting final version to UCDP: violates requirement and risks delivery rejection/repurchase.
Appraisal revised and resubmitted; UCDP returns “Successful” before delivery: meets the condition.
Lender alters PDF after appraiser signs (e.g., edits commentary) and submits: violates “unaltered report” requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection or repurchase demand

examples: Lender submits appraisal file to UCDP and receives hard-stop messages.
Lender resubmits corrected appraisal after resolving UAD formatting errors.

dependencies.rationale: BR_013_001 requires that the final revised appraisal used for underwriting be submitted through UCDP and receive Successful prior to sale. If the appraiser is not properly licensed/active (BR_021_009), the final appraisal cannot be relied upon for delivery even if UCDP is Successful.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Final revised appraisal may need replacement, causing rework and sale delays; possible delivery defect if sold without curing.

dependencies.rationale: BR_031_005 sets the baseline requirement to submit required appraisal forms through UCDP and obtain 'Successful' prior to sale. BR_013_001 adds a controlling rule for revised appraisals: the final version used for underwriting must be the one submitted and Successful. Thus, in revised-appraisal scenarios, BR_013_001 supersedes the generic 'submit the appraisal' approach by specifying the correct version control requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_013_001 is not followed, the lender may have a Successful status for an outdated version, leading to delivery defects and potential inability to sell until corrected.

business_rules.rule_name: UCDP Successful Status Required Before Sale 06/04/2025
business_rules.description: For conventional loans sold to Fannie Mae, the lender must submit required appraisal report forms (including all exhibits, addenda, and photographs) through UCDP and the submission must receive a “Successful” status prior to sale of the loan to Fannie Mae. Required forms listed include Form 1004, Form 1004 Desktop, Form 1004 Hybrid, Form 1004C, Form 1025, Form 1073, Form 1073 Hybrid, and Form 2090.
business_rules.conditions: When selling a conventional loan to Fannie Mae that requires listed appraisal forms
business_rules.consequences: If met: loan may be sold; if violated: sale is blocked until UCDP Successful
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Uniform Collateral Data Portal (UCDP) The UCDP is a portal through which lenders are required to electronically submit appraisal reports for conventional loans sold to Fannie Mae. The following appraisal report forms including all exhibits, addenda, and photographs, must be submitted through the UCDP and receive a “Successful” status from the UCDP prior to sale of the loan to Fannie Mae: Uniform Residential Appraisal Report ( Form 1004 ), Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Desktop) (Form 1004 Desktop ), Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Hybrid) (Form 1004 Hybrid ) Manufactured Home Appraisal Report ( Form 1004C ), Small Residential Income Property Appraisal Report (Form 1025 ), Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report ( Form 1073 ), Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report (Hybrid) ( Form 1073 Hybrid ), and Individual Cooperative Interest Appraisal Report ( Form 2090 ).

examples: One-unit SFR conventional loan uses Form 1004; lender uploads full report with photos/addenda and obtains Successful before sale.
Manufactured home loan uses Form 1004C; UCDP must show Successful prior to sale to Fannie Mae.
Co-op loan uses Form 2090; lender cannot sell the loan until UCDP status is Successful.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan sale blocked / delivery rejection until Successful status

examples: Lender submits appraisal file to UCDP and receives hard-stop messages.
Lender resubmits corrected appraisal after resolving UAD formatting errors.

dependencies.rationale: BR_021_009 requires state-licensed/state-certified appraisers with active credentials as of the appraisal effective date. BR_031_005 requires submitting appraisals through UCDP with a Successful status prior to sale. A UCDP 'Successful' status does not guarantee licensing compliance; therefore licensing validation should occur before (or at least alongside) UCDP submission to prevent submitting an appraisal that will later be deemed unacceptable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If licensing is invalid, the appraisal becomes unacceptable for delivery even if UCDP is Successful, forcing appraisal replacement, re-underwriting, and delaying sale.

dependencies.rationale: BR_024_010 requires manufactured home appraisals to be reported on Form 1004C with specified data elements. BR_031_005 lists Form 1004C among required forms to submit through UCDP for conventional loans sold to Fannie Mae. Therefore, for manufactured housing, producing the appraisal on the correct form is a gating condition for a correct UCDP submission set.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Incorrect form/content leads to appraisal not acceptable for delivery, UCDP re-submission, and sale delays.

dependencies.rationale: BR_010_001 defines an appraisal acceptability requirement (actual site size; cannot appraise a hypothetical portion). BR_031_005 is the UCDP submission requirement prior to sale. In practice, lenders should validate BR_010_001 before finalizing the appraisal package for UCDP submission to avoid late-stage appraisal rejection/revision cycles.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_010_001 fails, lender may need appraisal revision/new appraisal, causing UCDP resubmission and sale delays.

dependencies.rationale: BR_031_005 requires UCDP submission with 'Successful' status prior to sale. BR_005_010 prohibits delivery of a loan if any borrower is on OFAC sanctions lists. While neither rule is a strict computational prerequisite for the other, an automated sale readiness workflow should require both checks before allowing delivery.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_005_010 is not checked alongside BR_031_005, a loan might satisfy appraisal/UCDP requirements but still be prohibited from delivery due to OFAC, leading to last-minute sale stoppage, repurchase risk, or sanctions exposure.

dependencies.rationale: BR_023_009 governs how square footage must be measured/reported (ANSI Z765-2021) and requires noting alternate standards if mandated. BR_031_005 requires submitting the appraisal report used for sale. A lender should not submit a report for delivery if it is known to be defective on a core property characteristic like GLA, because that triggers appraisal revision, underwriting changes, and potential delivery defects.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_023_009 fails, the lender may still submit to UCDP but later face appraisal defect findings, requiring revisions and re-submission, delaying sale and potentially changing LTV/eligibility.

business_rules.rule_name: Whole Loan Funding Requires Clean Data by 9:00 p.m. ET
business_rules.description: For whole loan deliveries of regularly amortizing mortgages, Fannie Mae will fund purchase proceeds only after: (1) the lender has electronically submitted clean, error-free loan delivery data through Loan Delivery by 9:00 p.m. Eastern time; (2) the document custodian has received a complete and accurate mortgage document submission package by first morning delivery the following day; and (3) the document custodian has certified the package.
business_rules.conditions: Whole loan delivery of a regularly amortizing mortgage for purchase by Fannie Mae
business_rules.consequences: If all steps met, proceeds funded and status becomes Purchased and Funded; if not, funding delayed
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C2-2-04 (Timing of Distribution of Proceeds for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages)
source_reference.source_text: Fannie Mae will fund the purchase proceeds for whole loan deliveries of regularly amortizing mortgages provided the following procedure has already occurred: lender has electronically submitted clean, error-free loan delivery data through Loan Delivery by 9:00 p.m. (Eastern time), the document custodian has received a complete and accurate mortgage document submission package from a lender by ﬁrst morning delivery the following day, and the document custodian has certiﬁed the mortgage document submission package.

examples: Seller submits clean delivery data at 8:45 p.m. ET, custodian receives and certifies package next morning; Fannie Mae funds purchase proceeds.
Seller submits delivery data at 9:05 p.m. ET; procedure not met for same-cycle funding and proceeds may be delayed.
Custodian receives package but cannot certify due to missing docs; proceeds are not funded until the package is complete and certified.
Approved Seller/Servicer originates a fixed-rate purchase loan intended for delivery.
Seller/Servicer originates an ARM subject to ARM policy requirements.

dependencies.rationale: BR_034_007 requires 'clean, error-free loan delivery data' and custodian certification before funding. BR_015_009 states that fatal edits must be resolved for Fannie Mae to acquire the loan. If fatal edits exist, the delivery data cannot be considered clean/error-free and acquisition/funding is blocked.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Funding does not occur; purchase is delayed; rate lock/hedge exposure and operational escalations increase.

business_rules.rule_name: Post-Purchase Adjustment Request Due Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: The lender must submit a post-purchase adjustment request to notify Fannie Mae of any error, omission, or irregularity in the transfer of funds or repetitive transfer processing within 30 days of the date of the Purchase Advice; otherwise, Fannie Mae will assume the Purchase Advice information is correct. The request must include the lender’s nine-digit seller/servicer number, the ten-digit Fannie Mae loan number, the Post-Purchase Adjustment Request form, and applicable documentation.
business_rules.conditions: Lender identifies a funds transfer error/omission after receiving Purchase Advice
business_rules.consequences: If request submitted within 30 days, Fannie reviews/adjusts; if late, Purchase Advice deemed correct
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: C2-2-05 (Reconciliation of Purchase Advices and After Sale Corrections)
source_reference.source_text: The lender must submit a post-purchase adjustment request to provide notice of any error, omission, or irregularity in the transfer of funds or in processing the request for the repetitive transfer within 30 days of the date of the Purchase Advice. Otherwise, Fannie Mae will assume that the information on its Purchase Advice is correct. The request must include the following: the lender’s nine-digit Fannie Mae seller/servicer number, the ten-digit Fannie Mae loan number, the Post-Purchase Adjustment Request form, and applicable documentation.

examples: Purchase Advice dated 06/01: lender discovers wire was credited to wrong internal account; submits adjustment request by 07/01 with required identifiers and docs.
Lender submits adjustment request 45 days after Purchase Advice date; Fannie Mae may assume Purchase Advice is correct due to missed 30-day window.
Lender submits within 30 days but omits the Post-Purchase Adjustment Request form; request is incomplete and may not be processed until corrected.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Adjustment denied due to timeliness / funding reconciliation risk

examples: Approved Seller/Servicer originates a fixed-rate purchase loan intended for delivery.
Seller/Servicer originates an ARM subject to ARM policy requirements.

dependencies.rationale: BR_034_009 requires action within 30 days of the Purchase Advice date to correct funds transfer errors/omissions. Purchase Advice is associated with a purchased/funded loan; BR_034_007 defines the conditions for reaching 'Purchased and Funded' status. Operationally, BR_034_007 must occur before BR_034_009 can be triggered.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If purchase/funding does not occur, there is no Purchase Advice timeline to act upon; if funding is delayed, the adjustment window shifts accordingly.

business_rules.rule_name: Renovation draws only by joint check or consented wire each time
business_rules.description: Renovation escrow funds may only be disbursed by (1) a check issued jointly to the borrower and the contractor, or (2) a wire transfer to the contractor after the lender obtains written borrower consent prior to each disbursement. Each consent must specify borrower name, property address, amount, contractor, and date of consent.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to HomeStyle Renovation escrow disbursements of renovation funds
business_rules.consequences: If met: compliant draw controls; if violated: improper disbursement; delivery risk
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Renovation funds may only be disbursed using the following processes: a check issued jointly to the borrower and the contractor; or • a wire transfer to the contractor after the lender has obtained written consent from the borrower to release the funds to the contractor. The written consent must be received prior to each disbursement. It must specify the borrower’s name, property address, amount of funds to be disbursed, contractor to which the funds are to be disbursed, and date of the consent.

examples: Draw #2 released via joint check payable to borrower and contractor after work completion verified: compliant.
Lender wires $15,000 to contractor after receiving a signed consent listing borrower name, property address, amount, contractor, and date: compliant.
Lender wires funds based on a single blanket authorization signed at closing (no per-draw consent): violates the rule.
Loan requires escrow for property taxes and homeowners insurance.
Loan includes an escrow waiver only where permitted and properly documented.

dependencies.rationale: BR_024_006 requires that PITIA funds included in renovation costs be held in a renovation escrow account and applied only to payments due during non-occupancy. BR_024_007 then restricts how renovation escrow funds may be disbursed (joint check or contractor wire with borrower consent). Without establishing the escrow structure and use restrictions, the disbursement controls are not properly scoped or enforceable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Improper escrow setup/use leads to noncompliance, potential misuse of funds, and delivery risk for HomeStyle transactions.

business_rules.rule_name: Post-closing QC cycle completed within 90 days
business_rules.description: Loans must be selected for post-closing QC reviews at least monthly. The entire post-closing QC cycle—selection, review, rebuttal, and reporting—must be completed within 90 days from the month of loan closing. If the QC cycle is in arrears by more than one 30-day cycle, the lender must provide written notice to its Fannie Mae customer account team or QC Specialist.
business_rules.conditions: Post-closing QC program for loans closed in a given month
business_rules.consequences: If timely, QC meets timing standard; if late, written notice is required
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: D1-3-01, Timing of QC Review Process
source_reference.source_text: Loans must be selected for post-closing QC reviews on at least a monthly basis. The entire QC cycle (selection, review, rebuttal, and reporting) must be completed within 90 days from the month of the loan closing. Note : If the lender's QC cycle is in arrears more than one 30-day cycle, the lender must provide written notice to its Fannie Mae customer account team or QC Specialist.

examples: Loans closed in January are selected monthly; selection/review/rebuttal/reporting are finished by April 30 to satisfy the 90-day completion requirement.
Lender finishes February QC cycle in June (more than one 30-day cycle in arrears); lender must send written notice to its Fannie Mae account team/QC Specialist.
Lender selects loans quarterly instead of monthly; violates the monthly selection requirement and triggers QC process noncompliance.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit monthly cadence, 90-day completion window, and arrears trigger language are directly stated.

examples: Seller/Servicer maintains a QC program with post-closing sampling and defect tracking.
Seller/Servicer updates QC procedures following policy changes in appraisal requirements.

dependencies.rationale: BR_026_001 requires monthly selection and completion of the post-closing QC cycle within 90 days. BR_026_002 specifies the required selection volume and methodology when the 10% method is used, including separate retail vs third-party sampling. Thus, for the 10% method, BR_026_002 is a gating input to the BR_026_001 cycle’s selection step.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sampling is below minimums, the QC cycle can be timely but still noncompliant due to under-selection; lender may face QC findings and corrective action demands.

dependencies.rationale: BR_026_001 mandates monthly selection and completion timing of the QC cycle. BR_026_003 governs how statistical sampling must be computed (95% confidence, 2% precision, six-month statement) and documented. Therefore, for lenders using statistical sampling, BR_026_003 is prerequisite logic for compliant monthly selection under BR_026_001.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Noncompliant statistical sampling undermines the QC program even if reviews are performed; may require re-selection, re-review, and reporting corrections.

business_rules.rule_name: QC Document Request Response Due Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: When Fannie Mae selects a loan for underwriting, servicing, or data validation review and requests documentation, the lender must send the requested documentation within 30 days after notification, unless Fannie Mae specifies a shorter or longer period based on circumstances. If the lender delays, Fannie Mae may require indemnification or repurchase; extensive patterns of delays/unresponsiveness may be considered a breach of contract leading up to termination.
business_rules.conditions: After Fannie Mae notifies lender that a loan is selected for QC review
business_rules.consequences: If met: review proceeds; if violated: indemnification/repurchase and possible termination
business_rules.exceptions: Fannie Mae may set shorter/longer timeline; may work with extenuating circumstances

source_reference.source_text: Lenders must send the requested documentation for an underwriting, servicing, or data validation review within 30 days after Fannie Mae notiﬁes the lender that it has selected a loan for review. Fannie Mae, in its sole discretion, may request the documentation in a shorter or longer period of time based upon circumstances at the time. However, if a lender delays in providing the requested information, Fannie Mae, in its sole discretion, may require indemniﬁcation or repurchase... up to and including termination.

examples: Fannie Mae requests the underwriting file on April 1; lender provides all requested documents by May 1 (within 30 days).
Fannie Mae specifies a 10-day response due to urgent review; lender must meet the shorter timeframe.
Lender repeatedly fails to respond within timelines across multiple QC selections; Fannie Mae treats as breach and may terminate.
Fannie Mae performs a post-purchase QC review and identifies an underwriting documentation defect.
Fannie Mae performs a review focused on appraisal compliance and UCDP submission results.

dependencies.rationale: BR_031_009 requires sending requested documentation within 30 days of notification. BR_021_010 requires retaining QC reverification documentation for three years and providing it upon request. While BR_031_009 can sometimes be met by reconstructing documents, retention under BR_021_010 materially enables compliance with the response deadline.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If retention fails, lender may miss the 30-day window, increasing indemnification/repurchase risk and potential breach-of-contract findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Utility Efficiency Improvements Completion Deadline 2 Years
business_rules.description: Utility Efficiency Improvements work must be completed within two years. An exception applies for Utility Efficiency Improvements associated with Value-Add Mortgages, Mod Rehab, and Preservation Rehab Mortgages, which must follow Freddie Mac’s product-specific completion requirements instead of the standard two-year deadline.
business_rules.conditions: When loan includes Utility Efficiency Improvements commitment
business_rules.consequences: Completed on time: compliant; late completion: servicing/noncompliance issue
business_rules.exceptions: Value-Add, Mod Rehab, Preservation Rehab follow product requirements

source_reference.source_text: Work on the Utility Efficiency Improvements must be completed within two years, with the exception of Utility Efficiency Improvements for Value-Add Mortgages, Mod Rehab and Preservation Rehab Mortgages, which must be completed in accordance with Freddie Mac’s requirements for those products.

examples: Non-rehab fixed-rate mortgage closes 2026-01-15; improvements must be completed by 2028-01-15: compliant if finished by deadline.
Value-Add Mortgage closes 2026-04-01; improvements completion governed by Value-Add requirements, not automatically the two-year rule.
Non-rehab mortgage closes 2025-12-31 and improvements complete 2028-03-01: missed the two-year deadline; requires remediation per Guide/loan documents.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action / default remedies per loan documents

examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Benchmarking Consultant Required Life of Loan; Annual Metrics
business_rules.description: For Utility Efficiency loans, the Borrower must retain a Benchmarking Data Consultant for the life of the loan to collect, input, and monitor energy and water usage. The Borrower (through the consultant) must provide Benchmarking Metrics to Freddie Mac annually.
business_rules.conditions: When mortgage is a Utility Efficiency Loan option with benchmarking
business_rules.consequences: Annual metrics provided: compliant; failure: servicing reporting noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Borrower must retain a Benchmarking Data Consultant for the life of the loan to collect, input and monitor the energy and water usage in accordance with Section 39.10.

examples: Borrower retains a consultant through maturity; consultant submits annual PRS/benchmarking data package each year: compliant.
Borrower terminates the consultant in year 3 and stops tracking water usage: noncompliant with life-of-loan retention requirement.
Borrower retains consultant but fails to provide the annual Benchmarking Metrics to Freddie Mac for the prior year: noncompliant until cured.
Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Loan Differential paydown required by Conversion Date
business_rules.description: If the Borrower has funds (or can secure an Additional Source of Funds) to pay the Loan Differential, the Borrower must prepay a portion of its mortgage loan on or prior to the Conversion Date in an amount equal to the Loan Differential, causing a corresponding prepayment of the TEL. If the Borrower incurs additional debt to fund the paydown, that debt must be subordinated to the TEL and meet Guide Subordinate Financing requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Loan Differential exists and Borrower can fund it at TEL Conversion
business_rules.consequences: If done, TEL paydown equals Loan Differential; if not, Conversion funding condition is unmet
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: If the Borrower has the necessary funds or can secure an Additional Source of Funds to fund payment of the Loan Differential, the Borrower must  make a prepayment of a portion of its mortgage loan  so as to cause a corresponding prepayment of the TEL  on or prior to the Conversion Date in an amount equal to the Loan Differential.   Moreover, if the Borrower will incur additional debt to cover all or a portion of the Loan Differential in order to effectuate a paydown of the TEL, such additional debt must be subo rdinated to the TEL, and the terms, conditions and documentation of the additional debt must meet the requirements for Subordinate Financing set forth in this Guide.

examples: Loan Differential is $250,000: Borrower prepays $250,000 on or before the Conversion Date, resulting in a $250,000 TEL prepayment.
Borrower uses a new $100,000 loan to fund part of the paydown: the new debt must be documented as subordinate to the TEL and meet Subordinate Financing requirements.
If Borrower has funds but does not prepay by the Conversion Date, the Conversion condition fails and the TEL may not convert/purchase as scheduled.
Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Preservation Rehab Stabilization Must Occur Within 24 Months
business_rules.description: For a cash Mortgage under the Preservation Rehabilitation product, all rehabilitation work must be completed and the Property must reach stabilization within 24 months of the Origination Date.
business_rules.conditions: When Mortgage is a cash Mortgage using Preservation Rehabilitation execution
business_rules.consequences: If met: timeline compliance; if violated: noncompliance/remedies per documents
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 19 e.1 Definition of Preservation Rehabilitation (12/16/25)
source_reference.source_text: For a cash Mortgage, all work must be completed and the Property must reach stabilization within 24 months of the Origination Date.

examples: Origination date 01/15/2026; stabilization must be achieved by 01/15/2028 (24 months).
If rehab completes by month 20 but lease-up stabilization occurs at month 26, the Mortgage misses the stated stabilization deadline.
If the plan shows completion and stabilization by month 18, it meets the 24-month requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Remedies under Loan Documents/Rehabilitation Escrow Agreement

examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Complete PR-90 Repairs Within 90 Days and Others Within 365 Days
business_rules.description: The most urgent Priority Repairs must be identified as PR-90 Repairs on Form 1105 and must be completed within 90 days after the Origination Date. All other Priority Repairs must be completed within 365 days after the Origination Date.
business_rules.conditions: When Form 1105 identifies Priority Repairs including PR-90 Repair designation
business_rules.consequences: If completed by deadlines, loan remains compliant; if missed, default remedies/servicing actions may apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The most urgent Priority Repairs must be identified as PR -90 Repairs on Form 1105, Property Condition Assessment , and completed within 90 days after the Origination Date. All other Priority Repairs must be addressed as soon as possible and must be completed within 365 days after the Origination Date.

examples: Origination Date is 2026-03-01; PR-90 repair must be completed by 2026-05-30 (90 days after origination).
Origination Date is 2026-03-01; a non-PR-90 priority repair must be completed by 2027-03-01 (within 365 days).
If multiple PR-90 items exist, each must be completed within 90 days of the same Origination Date.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing default remedies per Loan Documents
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit completion deadlines (90 and 365 days) tied to Origination Date and Form 1105 classification.

examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Commitment Fee Due 2:00 p.m. ET on 2nd Business Day
business_rules.description: For a Forward Commitment, the Seller must deliver the refundable Commitment Fee to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock. The fee may be cash or a letter of credit (which must meet Section 11.2). If the Mortgage is not delivered by the Mandatory Delivery Date, Freddie Mac retains the Commitment Fee and accrued interest; if delivered by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased, the fee is refunded per the Guide.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Rate Lock under a Forward Commitment requiring a Commitment Fee
business_rules.consequences: Late/non-delivery risks loss of refund; non-delivery by Mandatory Date causes retention
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Commitment Fee must be delivered to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock. At the option of the Seller, the Commitment Fee can be in the form of cash or a letter of credit. A letter of credit must satisfy the requirements set forth in Section 11.2. If the Mortgage is not delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date in accordance with the Forward Commitment for any reason, the Commitment Fee, along with any interest accrued thereon, will be retained by Freddie Mac. If the Mortgage is delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased by Freddie Mac, the Commitment Fee will be refunded

examples: Rate Lock occurs Monday; Seller must deliver Commitment Fee by 2:00 p.m. ET Wednesday (2nd Business Day).
Seller delivers fee on time but fails to deliver the Mortgage by the Mandatory Delivery Date; Freddie Mac retains the fee and accrued interest.
Seller delivers Mortgage by Mandatory Delivery Date and Freddie Mac purchases; Commitment Fee is refunded per Guide.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL loss statement paid within 10 business days
business_rules.description: After Freddie Mac issues an SBL Loss Statement, the Seller/Servicer must remit the amount due to Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days after the date of the Loss Statement. Payment must be made by wire transfer to the account designated by Freddie Mac in the Loss Statement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues an SBL Loss Statement
business_rules.consequences: If timely paid, obligation satisfied; if late, noncompliance with Guide duty
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 46SBL, e. Loss Statement (08/18/22), Remittance Due Date
source_reference.source_text: 2. Remittance Due Date. The Seller /Servicer must remit the amount due and payable to Freddie Mac identified on the Loss Statement within 10 Business Days after the date of the Loss Statement. Payment must be made by wire transfer of funds to the account as Freddie Mac designates in the Loss Statement.

examples: Loss Statement dated 2026-03-01: wire must be received by end of day on the 10th Business Day after 2026-03-01.
Loss Statement dated 2026-07-03 (Friday): Business Day count starts from 2026-07-03; remit no later than the 10th Business Day (excluding weekends/holidays).
Seller/Servicer mails a check instead of wiring funds: violates 'Payment must be made by wire transfer' requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must remit' with 10 Business Days and wire-transfer method.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL repurchase must be elected at least 2 business days before pricing and completed within 10 business days after confirmation
business_rules.description: When an SBL Mortgage is subject to a Repurchase Option Eligible Default, the Seller/Servicer must: (i) exercise the Repurchase Option not less than two Business Days prior to the initial pricing date of a Secondary Market Transaction for which the SBL Mortgage has been preliminarily designated as collateral, and (ii) before receipt of any Freddie Mac Repurchase Notice for an SBL Mortgage subject to a SBL Repurchase Obligation. The Seller/Servicer must provide written notice of its election including specified fields, and must repurchase within 10 Business Days after receiving Freddie Mac’s written confirmation of Repurchase Price, plus pay any Repurchase Expenses.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when SBL repurchase option is being exercised due to eligible default
business_rules.consequences: If timely elected and completed, Seller/Servicer repurchases as required; if late, Freddie Mac may issue repurchase notice and enforce repurchase obligation
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 2. The Seller/Servicer must exercise the Repurchase Option (i) not less than two Business Days prior to the initial pricing date of the Secondary Market Transaction for which the SBL Mortgage has been preliminarily designated as collateral, and (ii) prior to the Seller/Servicer’s receipt of a Repurchase Notice from Freddie Mac ... 3. The Seller/Servicer must provide written notice to Freddie Mac of its Repurchase Option election ... • Freddie Mac loan number • Property name • Reason for the repurchase ... • Seller/Servicer’s estimate of the Repurchase Price ... 5. The Seller/Servicer must repurchase the SBL Mortgage within 10 Business Days following the receipt of written confirmation of the Repurchase Price from Freddie Mac.

examples: Initial pricing date is 08/15; Seller/Servicer must elect repurchase no later than 08/13 (2 business days prior) and then close repurchase within 10 business days after Freddie Mac confirms price.
Seller/Servicer sends election notice but omits the Freddie Mac loan number; notice is incomplete and must be corrected to meet the required content.
Freddie Mac confirms Repurchase Price on 09/01; Seller/Servicer must complete repurchase by 09/15 if there are no intervening holidays/weekends (10 business days).
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit timing thresholds and required notice fields are directly stated.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment extension request due 60 days before maturity
business_rules.description: For a first extension of a Forward Commitment, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request no later than 60 days prior to the Forward Commitment Maturity Date. The email must specify it is a first extension request and must include: a synopsis of the deal, a reason for the extension request, and a projected stabilization timeline.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller seeks a first six-month Forward Commitment extension.
business_rules.consequences: Timely complete request: Freddie can review/approve; late/incomplete: approval jeopardized.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated for the 60-day timing requirement.

source_reference.source_text: No later than 60 days prior to the Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify that the request is for a first extension, and must include the following: • A synopsis of the deal • A reason for the extension request • Projected stabilization timeline

examples: Maturity date is 2026-09-30; Seller emails request on 2026-08-01 with synopsis/reason/timeline: timely and complete.
Maturity date is 2026-09-30; Seller emails request on 2026-09-10: late (less than 60 days) and risks denial.
Seller emails on time but omits stabilization timeline: request is incomplete and may delay or prevent approval.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Refusal to extend Forward Commitment; transaction delay/cancellation

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Extension Request Email Due 60 Days Prior
business_rules.description: No later than 60 days prior to the applicable Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify whether the request is for a first extension or a subsequent extension and must include a synopsis of the deal, a reason for the extension request, and a projected stabilization timeline.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests any Forward Commitment extension
business_rules.consequences: If timely and complete, Freddie Mac can review; if late/incomplete, extension risk increases
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: No later than 60 days prior to the applicable Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify whether the request is for a first extension or a subsequent extension, and must include the following: A synopsis of the deal A reason for the extension request Projected stabilization timeline

examples: Forward Commitment Maturity Date is 2026-12-31; Seller emails extension request by 2026-11-01 including synopsis, reason, and stabilization timeline.
Seller emails request 45 days before maturity; request violates the 'no later than 60 days' requirement.
Seller submits email on time but omits projected stabilization timeline; request is incomplete and may delay Freddie Mac decisioning.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: Letter of Credit Must Extend by Extension Term Plus 60 Days
business_rules.description: If a Forward Commitment Maturity Date is extended, any letter of credit (LOC) applicable to the transaction must be extended for the length of the extension plus 60 days. The Seller must provide Freddie Mac with amendments or replacements extending the LOC stated term(s) no later than 30 days after the Forward Commitment Maturity Date and no later than 30 days prior to the expiration of the applicable LOC.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when transaction has an LOC and Forward Commitment is extended
business_rules.consequences: If deadlines met, LOC remains compliant; if missed, extension conditions may be unsatisfied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The term of any letter of credit applicable to the transaction must be extended for the length of the extension plus 60 days. The Seller must provide Freddie Mac with amendments or replacements of the applicable letters of credit, extending their respective stated terms, no later than 30 days after the Forward Commitment Maturity Date and no later than 30 days prior to the expiration of the applicable letter of credit.

examples: Extension length is 6 months; LOC term is extended by 6 months plus 60 days and the amendment is delivered within both 30-day deadlines.
LOC expires 2027-03-01; Seller must deliver amendment no later than 30 days before 2027-03-01 (i.e., by 2027-01-30) even if the 30-days-after-maturity deadline is later.
Seller extends Forward Commitment but does not extend the LOC for the extra 60 days; LOC extension is noncompliant.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Acceptance Letter Must Be Signed and Uploaded or Breakage Fee Applies
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac accepts an early rate lock application with no modifications or with nonmaterial modifications (without a reduction of the mortgage amount), the Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter (including its agreement to service the Mortgage) to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload the Acceptance Letter constitutes a Nondelivery, and Freddie Mac may, at its option, collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller; the Breakage Fee is calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues Acceptance Letter for early rate lock
business_rules.consequences: If uploaded, Seller bound and delivery proceeds; if not, Nondelivery and Breakage Fee may be collected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter, including its agreement to service the Mortgage, to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload to DMS the Acceptance Letter (including the agreement to service the Mortgage) will constitute a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac, at its option, will collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller. The Breakage Fee will be calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.

examples: Seller receives Acceptance Letter on Monday and uploads signed Acceptance Letter and servicing agreement to DMS per the letter instructions; no Nondelivery occurs.
Seller fails to upload the executed Acceptance Letter; this is a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac may collect a Breakage Fee based on the rate locked amount.
Seller uploads an incomplete package missing the agreement to service; treated as failure to upload the required Acceptance Letter contents.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated TAH invoice unpaid 10 business days triggers LOC draw
business_rules.description: For a Delegated TAH Mortgage, if Freddie Mac submits an invoice for loss/liability/damage/expense and the Seller does not pay within 10 Business Days after submission, Freddie Mac may draw on the Seller/Servicer's Letter of Credit or other security under the Delegated TAH Master Agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Delegated TAH Mortgage; Freddie Mac issues invoice for losses/expenses
business_rules.consequences: If unpaid in 10 BD, Freddie Mac may draw on LOC/other security
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; timing and remedy per Delegated TAH Master Agreement

source_reference.source_text: To the extent that Freddie Mac has suffered loss, liability, damage or expense, submit an invoice to the Seller for such loss, liability, damage or expense and, if not paid within 10 Business Days after submission, draw upon the Letter of Credit or other security pursuant to the Seller/Servicer’s Delegated TAH Master Agreement as to which any case or event stated in Section 47.2 relates
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are estimated from provided chunk excerpt.

examples: Freddie Mac invoices $75,000 loss on a Delegated TAH loan on April 1; if not paid by April 15 (10 BD), Freddie Mac may draw on the LOC.
Seller disputes invoice but does not pay within 10 BD; Freddie Mac may still draw on LOC per agreement, with dispute handled separately.
Seller pays invoice on day 8 after submission; Freddie Mac does not have basis to draw on LOC for non-payment.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Draw upon Letter of Credit or other security

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Insurance Proof Due Within 30 Days of Renewal
business_rules.description: Within 30 days of obtaining or renewing fidelity and/or mortgagee’s E&O insurance, the Seller/Servicer must submit acceptable proof of insurance to Multifamily Counterparty Risk & Compliance. Acceptable proof includes ACORD 25 (or other appropriate ACORD form), certificate of insurance, evidence of insurance, declarations page, or the policy.
business_rules.conditions: Applies upon obtaining new or renewed fidelity and/or E&O insurance
business_rules.consequences: If not submitted timely, Seller/Servicer is out of compliance and subject to action
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 3 – Seller/Servicer Eligibility Requirements (documentation timing)
source_reference.source_text: Within 30 days of obtaining or renewing fidelity and/or mortgagee’s E&O insurance, the Seller/Servicer must submit acceptable proof of insurance to Multifamily Counterparty Risk & Compliance on one of the following: • ACORD 25, Certificate of Liability Insurance (or other appropriate ACORD form) • Certificate of insurance • Evidence of insurance • Declarations page • Policy

examples: E&O policy renews on 06/01; Seller/Servicer must submit proof by 07/01.
Fidelity bond is obtained on 03/15; proof must be submitted by 04/14 (30 days after).
Seller/Servicer sends only an email summary with no certificate/policy; this is not acceptable proof.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination risk; required corrective action plan

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Seller Cash Management Authorization Required Before Payments
business_rules.description: Before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire transfer, ACH (if applicable), check, or any other electronic payment system, the Seller/Servicer must submit Multifamily Cash Management authorization documentation per Section 32.12(a). Freddie Mac cannot make payments unless the authorization documentation is on file with Freddie Mac and is compliant; wire payments also require compliance with Section 32.12(b).
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer instructs Freddie Mac to make a payment
business_rules.consequences: If authorization is on file, payment may proceed; if not, payment cannot be made
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; wire transfers also require Section 32.12(b) compliance

source_reference.source_text: Before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire transfer, Automated Clearing House (ACH) (if applicable), check or any other electronic payment system, a Seller/Servicer must submit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Cash Management authorization documentation in accordance with the requirements of Section 32.12(a). Payments cannot be made if such authorization documentation is not on file with Freddie Mac and in compliance with the requirements of Section 32.12(a). For payments to be made via wire transfer, a Seller/Servicer also must comply with the requirements of Section 32.12(b).

examples: Seller/Servicer requests a wire to remit a fee but has not submitted Section 32.12(a) authorization; Freddie Mac does not process the payment
Seller/Servicer submits compliant cash management authorization and then instructs Freddie Mac to pay via ACH; payment can be made
Seller/Servicer requests a wire transfer after filing 32.12(a) docs but does not meet 32.12(b) wire requirements; wire is not permitted until compliant
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL application fee due 2:00 p.m. ET on 2nd business day after lock or Lost
business_rules.description: For SBL application fees deemed earned by Freddie Mac, the Seller must remit by wire transfer per timing rules: (i) if the Seller locks the Coupon Rate, remit by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the Coupon Rate Lock; (iv) if Under Application status in PMT is changed to Lost by the Seller or Freddie Mac, remit by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the status change to Lost; (ii) if Freddie Mac determines it will not issue a Letter of Commitment for any reason, remit by the date that is 90 days after the fee was deemed earned (unless already remitted); (iii) if Freddie Mac issues a Letter of Commitment and Seller fails to accept or fails to lock by Commitment Expiration Date, remit by the date that is 90 days after the fee was deemed earned (unless already remitted).
business_rules.conditions: SBL Mortgage application fee is deemed earned under Chapter 18SBL
business_rules.consequences: If remitted on time, fee obligation satisfied; if late, noncompliance with remittance timing
business_rules.exceptions: 90-day deadlines do not apply if already remitted under 18SBL.6(e)(iv)

source_reference.source_text: e. Application fee ( 12/16/25 ) The application fee will be deemed earned by Freddie Mac ... payable by the Seller by wire transfer to Freddie Mac as follows: (i) If the Seller locks the Coupon Rate ... the Seller must remit the application fee to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the Coupon Rate Lock. (ii) If Freddie Mac determines that it will not issue a Letter of Commitment ... remit ... by the date that is 90 days after the date the application fee was deemed earned ... (iii) If Freddie Mac issues a Letter of Commitment and the Seller ... fails to lock ... by the Commitment Expiration Date, the Seller must remit ... by ... 90 days ... (iv) If the Under Application status ... is changed to Lost ... remit ... by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the status change to Lost.

examples: Coupon Rate locked on Monday: application fee must be wired by 2:00 p.m. ET on Wednesday (2nd business day).
PMT status changed to Lost on Thursday: application fee must be wired by 2:00 p.m. ET on Monday (assuming no holiday).
Freddie Mac decides not to issue a commitment; fee deemed earned on Jan 10: Seller must remit by Apr 10 (90 days) unless already remitted.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_020_008 requires remittance of 'SBL application fees deemed earned by Freddie Mac' by specified wire deadlines. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_029_002 defines (a) the fee amount and (b) when it is deemed earned (2:00 pm ET on the 5th Business Day after Under Application status in PMT). Without that calculation and earning timestamp, the remittance obligation is ambiguous and cannot be validated or automated.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_029_002 is not applied/available: (1) Seller may remit the wrong dollar amount, (2) Seller may miss the 'earned' trigger timing, (3) reconciliation breaks (earned vs remitted), (4) Freddie Mac can cite noncompliance and require cure or assess remedies/fees per Guide/commitment terms.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_020_008 mandates remittance 'by wire transfer'. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 requires authorization documentation before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire/ACH/check and states Freddie Mac cannot make payments without it. Operationally, the remittance-by-wire workflow should validate authorization-on-file before initiating payment instructions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 fails: (1) wire instructions cannot be executed/accepted, (2) remittance timing in BR_020_008 is missed, (3) late remittance noncompliance is triggered, (4) transaction status may be frozen until authorization is cured.

business_rules.rule_name: Screen employees at hire and at least annually
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must screen Freddie Mac Matters Employees and Freddie Mac Matters Contractors against the Restricted Party Lists at the time of hire/engagement and at least annually thereafter to ensure compliance with the fraud prevention requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Freddie Mac Matters Employees/Contractors screened vs Restricted Party Lists
business_rules.consequences: If performed, meets minimum screening cadence; if not, violates Guide screening practices
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller/Servicer must also screen, or caused to be screened, all Freddie Mac Contractors against the Restricted Party Lists to ensure compliance with the provisions of this chapter . The screening of Freddie Mac Matters Contractors and Freddie Mac Matters Employees should be conducted, at a minimum, at the time of hire or engagement, and at least annually thereafter .

examples: A new QC analyst hired on 2026-01-15 is screened against OFAC/SCP lists on the hire date and again by 2027-01-15.
A contractor engaged on 2025-08-01 is screened on 2025-08-01 and again during the Seller/Servicer’s annual rescreening cycle in 2026.
If a contractor is engaged but not screened until after work begins, the Seller/Servicer fails the 'time of engagement' minimum cadence requirement.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Conversion package due at least 60 days before conversion
business_rules.description: After completing all Conversion due diligence/analysis and satisfying all Conditions to Conversion, the Seller must prepare the Conversion Underwriting Package and submit it to Freddie Mac with the proposed Conversion schedule no later than 60 days prior to the proposed Conversion date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to TAH funded forward commitment conversions after conditions satisfied
business_rules.consequences: Met: Freddie can underwrite conversion; Violated: conversion timeline disruption/denial
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 19A.13(b) Conversion Underwriting Package and Conversion schedule
source_reference.source_text: Once the Seller has completed all Conversion due diligence and analysis and all Conditions to Conversion have been satisfied, the Seller must prepare the Conversion Underwriting Package and submit it along with the proposed Conversion schedule to Freddie Mac no later than 60 days prior to the proposed date of the Conversion.

examples: Proposed conversion date is 2026-09-30; Seller must submit the Conversion Underwriting Package by 2026-08-01 at the latest.
Seller satisfies all Conditions to Conversion on 2026-07-15 but targets 2026-08-15 conversion; submission must still occur by 2026-06-16 (60 days prior).
Seller submits the package 45 days before conversion; Freddie may require rescheduling or may reject conversion for untimely submission.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Title underwriter must perform funding functions for acquisitions after 08-15-2024
business_rules.description: For acquisition Mortgage origination transactions taken under a Seller Application submitted on or after August 15, 2024, and for all Mortgage origination transactions under Seller Applications submitted on or after October 31, 2025, the Title Insurance Underwriter (or its affiliate under identical ownership, or wholly-owned subsidiary) must directly perform all Funding Functions for both the mortgage origination and (if applicable) the property acquisition. The settlement statement must be delivered directly to the Seller/Servicer or its counsel by the underwriter/affiliate/subsidiary. Either the underwriter or the title agent may perform Escrow Functions. If jurisdictional law requires a licensed attorney for Funding Functions and the underwriter group lacks an on-staff licensed attorney in that jurisdiction, Seller/Servicer’s counsel must notify the Freddie Mac transactional attorney on or prior to submission of the full underwriting package and include analysis in the Legal Issues Analysis.
business_rules.conditions: When funding functions are performed for covered origination transactions by application date
business_rules.consequences: Noncompliance may delay underwriting review/closing and jeopardize Freddie Mac acceptance
business_rules.exceptions: TEL Mortgages fiscal agent may perform certain escrow/settlement functions (not in excerpt)

source_reference.source_text: Effective for any Mortgage origination transaction that is an acquisition which was taken under Seller Application submitted on or after August 15, 2024, and effective for all Mortgage origination transactions (including acquisitions) under Seller Applications submitted on or after October 31, 2025, the Title Insurance Underwriter, its affiliate under identical ownership, or its wholly-owned subsidiary must directly perform all Funding Functions for both the Mortgage origination transaction and, if applicable, the acquisition of the Property. The settlement statement must be delivered to the Seller/Servicer or the Seller/Servicer’s counsel directly by the Title Insurance Underwriter or such affiliate or subsidiary. (See Section 32.3(c) for additional settlement statement requirements.) Either the Title Insurance Underwriter or Title Agent may perform Escrow Functions.

examples: A property acquisition is taken under a Seller Application dated 2024-08-20; the title underwriter (or identical-ownership affiliate/wholly-owned subsidiary) must directly perform all Funding Functions.
A refinance (not an acquisition) is taken under a Seller Application dated 2025-11-15; Funding Functions must still be performed directly by the title underwriter group per the 10/31/2025 trigger.

A state requires attorney closings and the title underwriter has no in-state licensed attorney; Seller/Servicer’s counsel notifies Freddie Mac transactional attorney before submitting the full underwriting package and includes a Legal Issues Analysis.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 assigns core settlement/funding duties to the Title Insurance Underwriter (or qualifying affiliate). If that underwriter is on the Restricted Vendor List, BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits use—making the funding-function model non-executable until a compliant underwriter is selected.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 fails: (1) settlement/funding structure violates Guide, (2) underwriting review/closing may be delayed, (3) Freddie Mac acceptance jeopardized due to prohibited vendor usage.

dependencies.rationale: Because BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 requires the title underwriter to perform core settlement functions, Freddie Mac will scrutinize the underwriter relationship. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 is a baseline compliance condition for selecting/accepting that underwriter. Therefore, vendor integrity is a prerequisite check to avoid invalidating the funding functions structure.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may refuse/condition acceptance, (2) transaction may require vendor replacement and re-papering, (3) delays to closing and delivery.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL repurchase notice 5 days and repurchase 10 days
business_rules.description: For SBL Mortgages, within five Business Days after discovering a 60 Day Delinquency or Non-Monetary Default, the Seller/Servicer must provide Freddie Mac written notice. If Freddie Mac elects repurchase and issues a Repurchase Notice, the Seller/Servicer must repurchase the SBL Repurchase Mortgage no later than 10 Business Days after receipt of the Repurchase Notice (Mandatory Repurchase Date) by paying the Repurchase Price. Failure to repurchase by the Mandatory Repurchase Date constitutes a Repurchase Obligation Default and can result in additional fees and remedies including suspension or termination of selling and servicing rights.
business_rules.conditions: When an SBL Mortgage has a 60 Day Delinquency or Non-Monetary Default
business_rules.consequences: If met: timely notice/repurchase; if violated: default and remedies apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 46SBL – Page 9 (Notice and Mandatory Repurchase Date)
source_reference.source_text: Within five Business Days following its discovery of the occurrence of a 60 Day Delinquency or a Non- Monetary Default, the Seller /Servicer will provide Freddie Mac written notice of the occurrence of such event.
