Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Tenant notices and unit list due before inspection date
business_rules.description: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide tenant notification and must handle unit selection for inspection access as follows: (a) properties with 30 units or less: the Borrower must notify all tenants; (b) properties with 31 units or more: the Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection and the Borrower must notify tenants of those selected units. In all cases, a list of units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection. Failure to follow these steps may cause inspection access issues and delay underwriting.
business_rules.conditions: Applies before scheduled property inspection for complete inspection/lease audit
business_rules.consequences: If completed, inspection access prep compliant; if not, inspection readiness deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 2. Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants and select units for inspection as follows: a. Properties with 30 units or less: The Borrower must provide notification to all tenants. b. Properties with 31 units or more: The Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection, and the Borrower must provide notification to the tenants of those selected units. c. In all cases, a list of the units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.

Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants and select units for inspection as follows: a. Properties with 30 units or less: The Borrower must provide notification to all tenants. b. Properties with 31 units or more: The Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection... c. In all cases, a list of the units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection... If Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller will select units to be inspected... Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.

examples: A 24-unit property → Borrower must notify all tenants before inspection.
A 120-unit property requiring 12-unit inspection sample → Seller selects 24 units; Borrower notifies tenants in those 24 units.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit unit thresholds (30 vs 31+) and 'twice the required number' plus submission timing.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules describe the same pre-inspection tenant notification and unit selection procedure for underwriting inspections: for properties with 30 units or less, Borrower notifies all tenants; for 31+ units, Seller selects twice the required number of units for inspection and Borrower notifies those tenants; and a list of selected units must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to inspection. 007_007 is a shortened restatement referencing a section number but does not introduce a different requirement.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_003 selected as primary: clearer pre-inspection procedural steps (tenant notification + unit selection mechanics + list delivery to Freddie Mac) with less truncation than the duplicate.

business_rules.rule_name: Rent roll due 2 business days before inspection and ≤7 days old
business_rules.description: For SBL property inspections/lease audits with a scheduled site inspection, at least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection the Seller must deliver to Freddie Mac a rent roll dated within seven Business Days. Failure to deliver a timely/current rent roll may delay Freddie Mac’s review/inspection and render underwriting incomplete until cured.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL loans requiring scheduled site inspection under 8SBL.15
business_rules.consequences: If not met, Freddie Mac review/inspection may be delayed and underwriting deemed incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: At least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection, the Seller must deliver a rent roll dated within seven Business Days to Freddie Mac.

Any employee of the Seller responsible (individually or as part of a team) for originating the Mortgage • Any employee of the Seller directly benefitting financially or otherwise from the origination of the Mortgage At least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection, the Seller must deliver a rent roll dated within seven Business Days to Freddie Mac.
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are computed from visible excerpt beginning.

examples: Site inspection on Friday → rent roll delivered by Wednesday (2 Business Days prior) and dated within the prior 7 Business Days.
Rent roll dated 10 Business Days before inspection → noncompliant; updated rent roll required.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing and recency requirements with business-day thresholds.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac underwriting suspense / delayed approval

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules require identical rent roll timing for SBL inspections: at least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection, the Seller must deliver to Freddie Mac a rent roll dated within seven Business Days. Same timing (2 BD prior), same freshness requirement (within 7 BD), same SBL inspection context, same consequence (inspection/review delayed; underwriting incomplete).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_006_005 selected as primary: includes slightly more explicit SBL reference context (8SBL.15) while matching identical timing rule.

business_rules.rule_name: Tenant notice rules include very small markets all tenants and 2x units
business_rules.description: Prior to inspection, when Freddie Mac selects units (unless delegated), the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide tenant notifications: (i) Very Small Markets: notification to all tenants; (ii) all other markets: advance notifications to a minimum of twice the required number of units for inspection; if the property has 10 or fewer units, notification must be provided to all tenants. If the initially selected units are insufficient to meet lease audit requirements, the Seller must select additional units for which the Borrower must provide notification.
business_rules.conditions: Pre-inspection unit selection and tenant notification for SBL property inspections
business_rules.consequences: If access/notification insufficient, inspection must be rescheduled and completed
business_rules.exceptions: Delegated inspections allow substitutions if access cannot be obtained (documented)

source_reference.source_text: Prior to the date of the inspection, unless delegated to the Seller, Freddie Mac must select units for the inspection and the Seller must instruct Borrowers to provide notices to the selected units: • For Properties located in Very Small Markets, the Borrowers must provide notification to all tenants... For Properties in all other markets... the Borrower must provide advance site inspection notifications to a minimum of twice the required number of units for inspection. If a Property has ten or fewer units, the Borrower must provide notification to all tenants.
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are estimated from provided chunk excerpt.

examples: Very Small Market, 24-unit property: Borrower must notify all 24 tenants before inspection.
Standard market, 60 units; required inspections = 6 units: Borrower must notify at least 12 units (2x) to ensure access/selection.
Property has 10 units in a Small market: Borrower must notify all tenants (all 10), not just 2x the sample.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Screen third-party vendors before underwriting package from 7/1/2025
business_rules.description: Effective for Mortgages under Seller Application on or after 2025-07-01, the Seller/Servicer must screen each Third-Party Vendor involved in such Mortgages against the Restricted Party Lists. Screening must occur before submission of the full underwriting package, or if the vendor is engaged after submission, as soon as practicable thereafter and in any event before closing.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Mortgages under Seller Application on/after 2025-07-01 using Third-Party Vendors
business_rules.consequences: If screened timely, vendor is eligible to perform services; if not, transaction violates screening timing
business_rules.exceptions: For Seller Applications before 7/1/2025, may follow prior version or this section

source_reference.source_text: Effective for (i) Mortgages  under Seller Application on or after July 1, 2025, and ( ii) Servicing transactions for which complete servicing review packages are  received on or after July 1, 2025, t he Seller/Servicer must screen each Third -Party Vendor involved in such Mortgages  or Servicing transactions against the Restricted Party Lists.  Such screening must occur before the submission of the full underwriting package or complete servicing review package (as applicable) , or, if such Third- Party Vendor is not engaged until after the submission of the full underwriting package or complete servicing review package ( as applicable) , as soon as practicable thereafter and in any event before the closing of such transaction.

examples: Seller Application dated 2025-07-10: appraiser and title company are screened against Restricted Party Lists before the full underwriting package is submitted.
Environmental consultant is engaged after package submission: screening occurs within 2 days of engagement and before closing, satisfying 'as soon as practicable' and 'before closing.'
Seller Application dated 2025-06-15: Seller may comply with this section or the requirements in effect immediately prior to 2025-07-01.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: On-site lease files require audit at time of site inspection
business_rules.description: If lease files are maintained on-site at the Property, the Seller/Servicer must conduct the lease audit at the time of the site inspection.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when lease files location is on-site during SBL property inspection
business_rules.consequences: Met: inspection/audit timing compliant; Violated: re-perform audit or delay
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8SBL – SBL Property Fundamentals, lease audit timing
source_reference.source_text: Also, if lease files are maintained on -site, the lease audit must be conducted at the time of the site inspection.

examples: Lease files are stored in the on-site management office; lease audit is performed during the same visit as the site inspection.
Seller performs site inspection but delays lease audit for two weeks even though files are on-site; this violates the timing requirement.
Lease files are maintained off-site (e.g., corporate office); this specific on-site timing rule does not apply.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: SBL inspection and lease audit must be within 90 days of package receipt
business_rules.description: The property inspection and lease audit requirements must be completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package. If not completed within 90 days, a new inspection and lease audit must be performed and no recertification is allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL underwriting packages submitted to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: Met: inspection/audit acceptable; Violated: new inspection/audit required; no recert
business_rules.exceptions: No recertification permitted when 90-day window is missed

source_reference.section_id: c. Timing of property inspections and lease audits (06/13/24)
source_reference.source_text: The inspection and lease audit requirements of Section 8SBL.15(b) must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package. If the Seller inspection and lease audit are not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection and lease audit must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie receives the full underwriting package on 2026-01-01; inspection and lease audit must be completed by 2026-04-01 (90 days).
Inspection completed on day 95; Seller must order a new inspection and new lease audit—recertification is not allowed.
Lease audit completed timely but inspection is late (beyond 90 days); Seller must re-perform both as required by the rule.
business_rules.enforcement_action: New inspection and lease audit required; processing delay

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: Without the full underwriting package being received, the 'within 90 days of receipt' requirement cannot be evaluated or tracked. Delivery receipt is the start event for the timing control.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the package is not delivered (or delivery is delayed), the completion window start shifts, potentially compressing downstream inspection/audit scheduling or causing re-performance if the 90-day cap is exceeded after receipt.

business_rules.rule_name: Inspections must be completed within 90 days; recertification not allowed
business_rules.description: The property inspection requirements must be completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the applicable underwriting package (preliminary or full). If the Seller inspection is not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection must be performed and no recertification is allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to inspections submitted with preliminary or full underwriting packages
business_rules.consequences: Within 90 days: inspection acceptable; beyond 90 days: new inspection required
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; explicitly prohibits recertification in lieu of new inspection

source_reference.source_text: The inspection requirements of Section 8.15(a) must have been completed within 90 days of  Freddie Mac’s receipt of the preliminary underwriting package.  • When submitted as part of the full underwriting package:  The inspection requirements of Sections 8.15(a) and 8.15(b) must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package.  If the Seller inspection is not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie Mac receives full underwriting package on May 1; inspection completed June 15 (45 days): compliant.
Package received May 1; inspection completed Aug 10 (101 days): noncompliant; new inspection required.
Seller attempts to provide a recertification letter instead of new inspection after 90 days: not allowed.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: Receipt of the underwriting package is the timing anchor. The delivery process supplies the prerequisite start point and file context (preliminary vs full).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If package delivery is not properly completed/received, timing controls for inspection acceptability cannot be managed; if inspections occur outside the 90-day window post-receipt, a new inspection is mandatory with no recertification allowed.

business_rules.rule_name: Full underwriting inspects 10% homes with minimum 2
business_rules.description: At full underwriting for MHC Borrower-Owned Homes, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection. The inspection must include (i) ten percent of Borrower-Owned Homes, excluding Down Home Sites, with a minimum of two, (ii) all Down and vacant Borrower-Owned Homes, and (iii) all non-revenue Borrower-Owned Homes.
business_rules.conditions: At full underwriting for MHC Home Sites and Borrower-Owned Homes
business_rules.consequences: If met: underwriting inspection complete; if violated: underwriting incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: At full underwriting, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection including, but not limited to, the following: 1. Inspection of: o Ten percent of Borrower -Owned Homes, excluding Down Home Sites, with a minimum of two o All Down and vacant Borrower -Owned Homes o All non -revenue Borrower -Owned Homes

examples: Property has 12 Borrower-Owned Homes (excluding down): Seller inspects 10% (=1.2) but must inspect minimum 2 units.
Property has 40 Borrower-Owned Homes and 3 vacant units: Seller inspects 4 units (10%) plus all 3 vacant and any down/non-revenue units.
Seller inspects only occupied units and skips vacant homes: inspection does not meet full underwriting requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit sampling percentage and minimum count with required inclusions.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_008 defines the pool of homes and notification steps 'Prior to the inspection date' and requires providing the list to Freddie Mac. BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_007 then requires inspection of defined minimums and categories; that inspection depends on having selected/accessible units.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If selection/notification/list delivery is not completed, the inspection may be invalid or impossible to perform (no access), requiring rescheduling and delaying underwriting completion.

business_rules.rule_name: Pre-inspection selects 2x units and submits list to Freddie
business_rules.description: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must select twice the required number of Borrower-Owned Homes for inspection and must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to tenants. A list of the Borrower-Owned Homes to be selected must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.
business_rules.conditions: Before scheduled Seller property inspection of Borrower-Owned Homes
business_rules.consequences: If met: inspection selection process valid; if violated: inspection may be reworked
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must select twice the required number of Borrower -Owned Homes for inspection, and the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants of the selected Borrower -Owned Homes. A list of the Borrower -Owned Homes to be selected must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.

examples: Required inspection sample is 4 homes: Seller pre-selects 8 homes and provides the 8-home list to Freddie before inspection.
Seller pre-selects only 4 homes (not 2x): does not comply; Freddie may require new selection and tenant notices.
Seller selects 2x but fails to send the list to Freddie before inspection date: process defect; inspection may be delayed.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery condition / require rescheduling and proper notification
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'twice the required number' and timing 'prior to the date of inspection'.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Inspection Must Be Completed Within 90 Days
business_rules.description: When a Forward Commitment full underwriting package is submitted, the Forward Commitment Property Inspection requirements must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package. If not completed within 90 days, a new inspection must be performed and recertification is not allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Forward Commitment full underwriting package submitted to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: If within 90 days, inspection is acceptable; if not, new inspection required (no recert)
business_rules.exceptions: No recertification allowed

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8, Timing of the Forward Commitment Property Inspection
source_reference.source_text: When submitted as part of the Forward Commitment full underwriting package, the Forward Commitment Property Inspection requirements must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the Forward Commitment underwriting package. If not, a new inspection must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie Mac receives the package on 2026-06-01; inspection must be dated on/after 2026-03-03 to be within 90 days.
Inspection is 120 days old at receipt; Seller must order and complete a new inspection, not a recertification letter.
Seller attempts to reuse an older inspection with a recertification; this is not permitted and triggers a new inspection requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting package rejection until new inspection performed
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing requirement (90 days) plus explicit prohibition on recertification.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Confirmation Sheet executed and uploaded next business day
business_rules.description: After Rate Lock, Freddie Mac will deliver a completed Mortgage Terms Confirmation Sheet. No later than the Business Day immediately following the Seller’s receipt of the Confirmation Sheet, the Seller must execute and upload the Confirmation Sheet to DMS to indicate acceptance of the Rate Lock terms. If the Seller fails to return the executed Confirmation Sheet within the required time, Freddie Mac may treat the failure as a Nondelivery.
business_rules.conditions: Applies immediately after Seller receives the Confirmation Sheet post Rate Lock
business_rules.consequences: If returned timely, Rate Lock terms accepted; if late, may be treated as Nondelivery
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 27.16 Rate Lock and Mortgage Terms Confirmation Sheet (04/15/21)
source_reference.source_text: After Rate Lock, Freddie Mac will deliver to the Seller a completed Confirmation Sheet. No later than the Business Day immediately following the Seller’s receipt of the Confirmation Sheet, the Seller must execute and upload to DMS the Confirmation Sheet to indicate its acceptance of the Rate Lock terms. If the Seller fails to return the executed Confirmation Sheet to Freddie Mac within the required time, Freddie Mac may, in its discretion, treat such failure as a Nondelivery.

examples: Seller receives Confirmation Sheet on Tuesday: Seller must sign and upload to DMS by Wednesday (next Business Day).
Seller receives Confirmation Sheet on Friday: Seller must sign and upload by Monday (next Business Day) unless Monday is a holiday.
Seller uploads an unsigned Confirmation Sheet: does not satisfy 'must execute and upload' requirement; Freddie Mac may treat as Nondelivery.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (next Business Day) and explicit consequence (may treat as Nondelivery).

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The confirmation sheet acceptance process is downstream of Rate Lock. Because Freddie Mac will not accept early rate-lock without the engineering report, the lock/confirmation workflow should not proceed until BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is satisfied.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Missing the property condition report can prevent or unwind Rate Lock; Seller cannot (or should not) execute the confirmation sheet because Rate Lock terms would not be validly issued/accepted.

dependencies.rationale: The Seller must complete and document management interview/property walk/unit inspections before proceeding to the lock stage. Only after lock issuance does the confirmation sheet acceptance step occur.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If pre-lock inspection steps are missed, the Seller may be out of compliance even if the confirmation sheet is executed timely; the deal may be conditioned, re-underwritten, or delayed for cure documentation.

business_rules.rule_name: Early rate lock package due 7–45 days; delivery due 45–105 days
business_rules.description: After Rate Lock, the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package by deadlines tied to the early rate lock type: 4-month (120 Day Funding): 45 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date is the earlier of 105 days after Rate Lock or 25 days after Freddie Mac acceptance of the early rate lock application. 3-month (90 Day Funding): package due 22 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 75 days after Rate Lock. 2.5-month (75 Day Funding): package due 15 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 60 days after Rate Lock. 2-month (60 Day Funding): package due 7 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 45 days after Rate Lock.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to transactions using an early rate lock after Rate Lock occurs
business_rules.consequences: If timely, meets due dates; if late, risks missing Mandatory Delivery requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise specified in the Confirmation Sheet

source_reference.section_id: 27.18 Early rate lock delivery – delivery due dates and requirements (02/17/22)
source_reference.source_text: then after Rate Lock the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package in accordance with the following chart. The Mandatory Delivery Date will also be determined as set forth below unless otherwise specified in the Confirmation Sheet. Type of early rate lock Type of extension (in addition to 120 Day Funding) Date full underwriting package is due Mandatory Delivery Date is the earlier

examples: 2-month early rate lock: Rate Lock on 2026-01-10; full package due by 2026-01-17; Mandatory Delivery Date by 2026-02-24 (45 days after).

4-month early rate lock: Rate Lock on 2026-03-01; full package due by 2026-04-15 (45 days after); Mandatory Delivery Date is earlier of 2026-06-14 (105 days after) or 25 days after Freddie Mac acceptance.
3-month early rate lock: Seller submits full package 30 days after Rate Lock; violates 22-day requirement for package due date.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit chart-driven deadlines; consequence largely operational (delivery timing).

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The full underwriting package delivery schedule is anchored to the Rate Lock event. The early rate-lock event chain is gated by the engineering report requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is not met, early rate lock acceptance may not occur, which prevents or delays starting the delivery timeline and can jeopardize transaction timelines.

dependencies.rationale: If the Seller fails to execute and upload the confirmation sheet timely, Freddie may treat the failure as a Nondelivery. In that scenario, subsequent obligations to deliver the full underwriting package under the lock may be moot or require reinstatement/exception handling.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the confirmation sheet is not returned, the lock may be treated as Nondelivery; package delivery may be rejected, timelines reset, or the transaction may require escalation and potential re-lock.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_021_005 establishes a timing mandate relative to the full underwriting package submission. Therefore, to deliver a compliant full underwriting package (BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006), the Seller/Servicer must have screened each Third-Party Vendor involved (for applicable applications).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If screening is not completed before package submission, the submission is timing-noncompliant, may require cure/resubmission, and can delay underwriting review and closing; if a vendor is on a restricted list, the transaction may require vendor replacement and rework.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_037_010 requires the SFHDF determination and a signed Notice to Borrower (plus ongoing effectiveness). The full underwriting package delivery step is the operational point where these required origination documents must be present to avoid suspense/deficiency.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If SFHDF/notice is missing or outdated, Freddie Mac may suspend review, condition the file, or require updated determinations; closing timelines may slip and delivery defects may be assessed.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_008 specifies additional required documentation based on repair magnitude/habitability triggers. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006 is the submission event for the full underwriting package, so package completeness depends on this conditional doc being present when triggered.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the remediation plan is omitted when required, Freddie Mac may condition or decline the underwriting review until the plan is provided and evaluated; this can delay delivery acceptance and jeopardize the lock timeline.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_031_010 explicitly requires delivery of the Borrower Certification of Weather-Related Event 'as part of the full underwriting package' when the triggering event occurred after the inspection.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If omitted when triggered, Freddie Mac may suspend underwriting, require updated condition verification, or delay/decline review pending the certification; can cascade into missed delivery/funding dates.

business_rules.rule_name: Final delivery package due by noon Eastern time on mandatory delivery date and notice email within 24 hours
business_rules.description: The Seller must deliver the Final Delivery Package no later than noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date stated in the applicable Purchase Contract. The Seller must send a delivery notification email to MF_Delivery_Notification@freddiemac.com with the Freddie Mac Loan Number, Property Name, Counsel name and tracking number within 24 hours of expected receipt. The original documents must be delivered in a one inch red-well folder.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to hardcopy/electronic final delivery submissions under a Purchase Contract
business_rules.consequences: If met, delivery is timely for Freddie Mac review; if missed, delivery may be deemed late and purchase/funding may be delayed or rejected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Delivery and review period; preferred funding (10/01/25), item 1
source_reference.source_text: 1. The Seller must deliver the Final Delivery Package no later than noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date identified in the applicable Purchase Contract. A delivery notification email must be sent to MF_Delivery_Notification@freddiemac.com with the Freddie Mac Loan Number, Property Name, Counsel name and tracking number within 24 hours of expected receipt. The original documents must be delivered in a one inch red-well folder.

examples: Mandatory Delivery Date is 11/15; Seller ships so Freddie Mac receives by 12:00 PM ET on 11/15 and sends notification email the day before expected receipt.
Seller delivers at 1:30 PM ET on Mandatory Delivery Date; delivery is late and funding may be delayed pending Freddie Mac direction.
Seller fails to include tracking number in the notification email; Seller must resend a corrected email within the 24-hour window.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit time-of-day deadline, 24-hour timeframe, and packaging requirement.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: A typical Freddie Mac workflow is: rate lock → deliver underwriting package → obtain approvals/close → deliver final delivery package by the Mandatory Delivery Date. While not every detail is explicitly linked, operational sequencing makes final delivery dependent on completing upstream package delivery and closing readiness.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the full underwriting package is late or incomplete, final approvals and closing may slip, increasing risk of missing the Mandatory Delivery Date and delaying/rejecting purchase/funding.

business_rules.rule_name: Preferred funding date must be at least 10 calendar days after Freddie Mac receives final delivery package and no later than mandatory funding date
business_rules.description: The Seller must request in writing its preferred funding date by inserting it on the Final Delivery Table of Contents. The preferred funding date cannot be earlier than 10 calendar days from the date Freddie Mac receives the Final Delivery Package and cannot be later than the Mandatory Funding Date. If the Seller fails to provide a preferred funding date, funding will occur once review is completed, but no later than the Mandatory Funding Date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests a preferred funding date for a delivered Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If compliant, Freddie Mac targets requested date; if noncompliant/omitted, Freddie Mac funds after review but by Mandatory Funding Date
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Delivery and review period; preferred funding (10/01/25), item 2
source_reference.source_text: 2. The Seller must request in writing its preferred funding date (no earlier than 10 calendar days from the date on which Freddie Mac receives the Final Delivery Package and no later than the Mandatory Funding Date) by insertion of the preferred funding date on the applicable blank on the Final Delivery Table of Contents. If the Seller fails to provide a preferred funding date, funding will occur once review is completed, but no later than the Mandatory Funding Date.

examples: Freddie Mac receives package on 03/01; earliest allowable preferred funding date is 03/11; Seller requests 03/15 and it is acceptable.
Seller requests preferred funding on 04/05 but Freddie Mac received package on 03/29 (only 7 days earlier); Seller must revise the request to 04/08 or later to meet 10 calendar days.
Seller leaves preferred funding date blank; Freddie Mac funds after completing review, but still by the Mandatory Funding Date.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 10-calendar-day minimum and Mandatory Funding Date cap; includes consequence if preferred date omitted.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_002 is a sub-step of final delivery: you cannot specify a preferred funding date in the final delivery TOC without preparing/submitting the final delivery package governed by BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_001.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If final delivery package is not properly delivered, the preferred funding date request is ineffective; funding will be scheduled based on when review completes (and no later than the Mandatory Funding Date), potentially harming borrower/seller funding coordination.

business_rules.rule_name: Reimburse Freddie Mac within 10 business days of request
business_rules.description: For a delivery of a pledged Mortgage, the Seller must reimburse Freddie Mac for reasonable expenses Freddie Mac incurs in connection with delivery of the pledged Mortgage by any warehouse lender. Freddie Mac will specify the nature and amount in a written notice, and the Seller must reimburse the amount within 10 Business Days of Freddie Mac’s request.
business_rules.conditions: Warehouse lender delivers a pledged Mortgage and Freddie Mac issues written expense notice
business_rules.consequences: If paid timely, delivery expense item is cleared; if not, Seller is noncompliant and may face remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: for any reasonable expenses incurred by Freddie Mac in connection with the delivery of a pledged Mortgage by any warehouse lender to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac will specify the nature and amount of such expenses in a written notice to the Seller. The Seller must reimburse Freddie Mac for the amount of

examples: Freddie Mac sends a written notice for $1,250 in courier and handling expenses; Seller pays within 10 Business Days: compliant.
Seller receives written notice on April 1; payment arrives April 20 (beyond 10 Business Days): noncompliant and subject to Freddie Mac remedies under the Guide.
Multiple expense notices are issued for separate pledged deliveries; each notice starts its own 10 Business Day reimbursement clock.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: While not a strict legal prerequisite, the reimbursement scenario is downstream of a pledged mortgage delivery that settles successfully. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_032_010 is a key delivery/settlement control ('settlements cannot be made if... documentation is not on file'), making it a practical upstream step in the same pledged mortgage delivery lifecycle in which reimbursement requests can later occur.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If authorization documentation and Form 987M are not on file, settlement cannot occur and the pledged mortgage delivery process is interrupted; as a result, the downstream reimbursement event may not occur, but the Seller faces delivery failure/operational disruption and potential rescheduling costs.

business_rules.rule_name: Submit $100 million opinions 3 business days before origination
business_rules.description: For non-SBL Mortgages, the Seller/Servicer must submit specified opinions to Freddie Mac for review and approval not less than three business days prior to the scheduled origination date when (i) the Mortgage has an original principal balance ≥ $100,000,000 (all opinion letters) and/or (ii) the opinion is a Seniors Housing Mortgage licensure opinion (#27 and #28 from the Borrower Opinion form). Opinions must be marked to clearly indicate additions and deletions from the form.
business_rules.conditions: Non-SBL; ≥$100M loan and/or Seniors Housing licensure opinion; before scheduled origination
business_rules.consequences: If timely submitted, Freddie Mac can review; if late/absent, origination/delivery may be delayed or conditioned
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text:
The Seller /Servicer  must submit a copy of the following opinions for Freddie Mac’s review and
approval not less than three  business days prior to the scheduled origination date of the
Mortgage:

• All Opinion Letters for any Mortgage with an original principal balance equal to or greater
than $100,000,000.

• Any Seniors Housing Mortgage licensure opinion, specifically opinions #27 and #28 from
the Borrower Opinion form.

Such opinions must be marked to clearly indicate the additions to and deletions from the
appropriate form of Opinion Letter.

examples: A $150,000,000 non-SBL Mortgage: Seller/Servicer must submit all Opinion Letters at least 3 business days before origination.
A $60,000,000 Seniors Housing Mortgage requiring licensure opinions #27 and #28: those opinions must be submitted ≥3 business days prior to origination even though UPB is below $100,000,000.
Seller submits the opinions 1 business day before origination: noncompliant timing; Freddie Mac review may delay origination or impose conditions.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Acceptance Letter must be acknowledged and uploaded to DMS or it is a Nondelivery with Breakage Fee
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac issues an Acceptance Letter (including revised Confirmation Sheet) for an Increased Mortgage Amount, the Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter and its agreement to service the Mortgage to DMS in accordance with the Acceptance Letter terms. Failure to sign and upload to DMS constitutes a Nondelivery, and Freddie Mac may collect the Breakage Fee calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues Acceptance Letter for Increased Mortgage Amount under early rate lock.
business_rules.consequences: If met, rate locked amount becomes Increased Mortgage Amount; if violated, Nondelivery and Breakage Fee may apply.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: The Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter, including the revised Confirmation Sheet and its agreement to service the Mortgage, to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload to DMS the Acceptance Letter, including the revised Confirmation Sheet and its agreement to service the Mortgage will constitute a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac, at its option, will collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller. The Breakage Fee will be calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.

examples: Seller uploads signed Acceptance Letter to DMS the same day received: delivery requirement satisfied.
Seller signs but does not upload to DMS: constitutes Nondelivery; Freddie Mac may assess Breakage Fee.
Seller uploads Acceptance Letter but omits agreement to service: treated as failure to upload complete package, risking Nondelivery.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Monthly rent roll required from completion through Conversion
business_rules.description: From and after completion of construction through and including Conversion, the Borrower must provide a current rent roll for each month to the Seller. Each monthly rent roll must be certified as true and correct by both the Borrower and the property manager. The Seller/Servicer must obtain these to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after construction completion through Conversion for Forward Commitment conversions
business_rules.consequences: If provided and certified, supports occupancy verification; if missing/uncertified, condition may fail
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 17A, Requirements for Conversion, Minimum Occupancy Requirement (12/16/25)
source_reference.source_text: • For each month from and after completion of construction , through and including Conversion, the Borrower will provide to the Seller a current rent roll for that month, each certified as true and correct by the Borrower and the property manager for the Property, and such other information as may be reasonably required be the Seller and/or Freddie Mac to determine the physical occupancy of the Property.

examples: Construction completed in January: Borrower provides certified rent rolls for February, March, April, and May leading up to June Conversion.
Borrower provides rent roll but only Borrower signs certification; property manager certification is missing—noncompliant with the stated requirement.
If Freddie Mac requests additional occupancy information beyond rent rolls, Borrower/Seller must provide as reasonably required.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: SBL Physical Risk Report required before Letter of Commitment issuance
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac requires the Seller/Servicer to submit an SBL Physical Risk Report that meets Chapter 62SBL requirements before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment to purchase an SBL Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL mortgages prior to Freddie Mac Letter of Commitment issuance
business_rules.consequences: Met: Freddie may issue commitment; Violated: commitment will not be issued
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 8SBL.17, requirement to submit Form 1104 report before commitment
source_reference.source_text: 1. Freddie Mac requires the Seller/Servicer to submit an SBL Physical Risk Report meeting the requirements of Chapter 62SBL before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment to purchase an SBL Mortgage.

examples: Seller submits a Chapter 62SBL-compliant Form 1104 report; Freddie proceeds with commitment review.
Seller omits the SBL Physical Risk Report; Freddie will not issue the Letter of Commitment until submitted.
Seller submits a report that does not meet Chapter 62SBL requirements; Freddie treats it as not satisfying the prerequisite.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Refuse to issue Letter of Commitment

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 determines fundamental eligibility for sale based on Exclusionary List role matches and requires use of the most current list. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 requires a compliant SBL Physical Risk Report to obtain commitment. While the risk report does not logically require screening results, compliance operations typically sequence eligibility screening early to prevent wasted third-party report costs and cycle time on ineligible transactions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails after the Physical Risk Report is ordered/submitted, the Seller may incur unnecessary vendor expense and delays, then still have to stop/withdraw the loan due to ineligibility.

dependencies.rationale: BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 creates an absolute stop for Freddie Mac approvals (purchase/servicing-related approvals). BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 is an approval step (Letter of Commitment) and is therefore blocked if sanctions screening results in a confirmed match.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sanctions screening fails (confirmed match), Freddie will not issue the SBL Letter of Commitment regardless of whether the Physical Risk Report is provided; underwriting/commitment workflow terminates and the transaction must be declined or restructured with different parties.

business_rules.rule_name: Property Inspection Report Due Annually if UPB > $2M or Risk Rating > 6
business_rules.description: For Non-SBL Mortgages, a property inspection report due date is tied to UPB and risk status. If UPB is less than or equal to $2 million, the inspection report is due at the calendar quarter end of the biennial anniversary of the Origination Date (unless otherwise notified). If UPB is greater than $2 million, or Risk Rating is greater than 6, or the mortgage is subordinate to a securitized loan, the inspection report is due at the calendar quarter end of the annual anniversary of the Origination Date (unless otherwise notified). Additionally, for a Mortgage with a Transfer of Ownership or Subordinate Financing, the report is due at the time of the transfer or Subordinate Financing; for a Mortgage with a tax lien, it is due when the tax lien is filed.
business_rules.conditions: When Non-SBL mortgage meets UPB/risk triggers or special events occur
business_rules.consequences: Timely inspection: compliant; late/missing: post-purchase reporting defect
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 40.2(b) Property inspection report – Non-SBL Mortgages (10/17/24)
source_reference.source_text: Property inspection report – Non-SBL Mortgages  A Mortgage with a UPB ≤ $2 million  The calendar quarter end of the biennial anniversary of the Origination Date. Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac: • March 31, • June 30, • September 30, or • December 31  • A Mortgage with a UPB > $2 million, or • A Mortgage with a Risk Rating > 6, or • A Mortgage held by Freddie Mac and subordinate to a securitized loan  The calendar quarter end of the annual anniversary of the Origination Date.

examples: Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $1.8M with Risk Rating 5; origination 2023-02-10: inspection due at quarter-end of biennial anniversary (e.g., 2025-03-31 if that is the applicable quarter-end).
Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $3.5M; origination 2024-07-01: inspection due at quarter-end of annual anniversary (e.g., 2025-09-30 if that is the applicable quarter-end).
Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $1.5M but Risk Rating is 7: annual inspection cadence applies despite UPB ≤ $2M.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action request / asset management escalation

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: A-CREFC assessment due March 31 or May 31 for UPB over $250,000
business_rules.description: For Mortgages with unpaid principal balance (UPB) greater than $250,000, the Servicer must submit an annual assessment using A-CREFC-MF or A-CREFC-HC (as applicable). The due date is March 31 for Mortgages with Risk Rating greater than 6 or Special Product Type Mortgages specified in the Guide (except SBL Mortgages). The due date is May 31 for all other Mortgages, including SBL Mortgages. Late or missing submission is a reporting noncompliance item requiring follow-up.
business_rules.conditions: Mortgage has UPB > $250,000 and annual assessment is required under Chapter 40 table
business_rules.consequences: If submitted by required deadline, assessment is timely; if late, Servicer is out of compliance with reporting requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Special Product Type timing excludes SBL Mortgages from March 31 trigger

source_reference.source_text: A-CREFC -MF or A -CREFC -HC, as applicable  A Mortgage with a UPB > $250,000  • March 31 – a Mortgage with a Risk Rating > 6, or a Special Product Type Mortgage specified in Section 40.16, except SBL Mortgages  • May 31 – all other Mortgages, including SBL Mortgages

Risk Rating 4 SBL Mortgage → annual assessment due May 31.

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules require the same annual assessment submission rule for Mortgages with UPB > $250,000, including the same due dates and segmentation: due March 31 for Mortgages with Risk Rating > 6 or specified Special Product Type Mortgages (except SBL), and due May 31 for all other Mortgages including SBL. Same thresholds, same dates, same scope, same consequence.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_012_009 selected as primary: equal content, retained per priority 5 (later table reference context in conditions is slightly clearer) with no loss of detail.

dependencies.rationale: Both rules require an annual assessment using A-CREFC-MF/A-CREFC-HC for UPB > $250,000 with the same March 31/May 31 split by risk rating/special product type. In a knowledge graph, linking them as complementary reduces user confusion and supports dedup/normalization in compliance workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If treated independently without linkage, automated testing and compliance checklists may create duplicate tasks, conflicting task ownership, or double-count exceptions. Linking supports control rationalization and consistent reporting calendars.

business_rules.rule_name: Quarterly in 30 days and annual in 90 days; rent roll within 5 days of quarter end
business_rules.description: For non-SBL Mortgages subject to Chapter 40.15, the Seller/Servicer must submit via PRS: (a) within 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter following purchase, a current rent roll dated no earlier than five days prior to quarter-end and income/expense reporting for the required period; (b) within 90 days after the end of each fiscal year, annual financial statements including balance sheet and related items. If Loan Documents are modified to allow the Borrower more than 90 days to deliver annual documents, the Seller/Servicer must deliver them within five days after receiving them from the Borrower.
business_rules.conditions: Portfolio loans/loans scheduled for securitization; non-Cooperative property (quarterly); post-purchase
business_rules.consequences: If timely submitted, reporting remains current; if late, Servicer/Seller-Servicer violates post-purchase reporting requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Quarterly timing excludes Mortgages secured by a Cooperative-owned property

source_reference.source_text: for Mortgages secured by a Property owned by a Cooperative, within 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter following purchase: 1. Current rent roll dated no earlier than the date five days prior to the end of such quarter 2. Income and expenses

examples: Quarter ends June 30: rent roll must be dated no earlier than June 25 and submitted by July 30 (30 days after quarter end).
Fiscal year ends December 31: annual financial statements must be submitted by March 31 (90 days after fiscal year end).
Loan documents allow borrower 120 days for annuals; Seller/Servicer receives them on April 25 and must deliver to Freddie Mac by April 30 (within 5 days).
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates

business_rules.rule_name: Loan-Level Transaction Report Due by 10th or 3rd Business Day
business_rules.description: For bond credit enhancement loan-level transactions: (1) If Borrower payments are received prior to the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report must reach Freddie Mac no later than the 10th day of that month. (2) If Borrower payments are received on or after the 10th day, the report must reach Freddie Mac no later than the third Business Day after the Servicer receives payment. If no payments are received, the report must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the 15th day of the month.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to bond credit enhancement loan-level transaction reporting timing each month
business_rules.consequences: If met, reporting is timely; if violated, reporting noncompliance and follow-up risk
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: 1. For Borrower payments received prior to the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report of loan-level transactions must reach Freddie Mac no later than the 10th day of the month in which the payment was received by or on behalf of the Borrower under the reimbursement and security agreement or the interest rate cap or swap agreement. If no payments are received, the Servicer’s report must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the 15th day of the month. ... 2. For Borrower payments received on or after the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report of loan-level transactions must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the Servicer receives any payment

examples: Payment received on the 8th: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 10th of the same month.
Payment received on the 10th: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 3rd Business Day after receipt.
No payments received in month: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 3rd Business Day after the 15th.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Securitization Reporting Must Populate Non-Cash Writedown UPB After Payment
business_rules.description: For each Mortgage sold in a Securitization, the Servicer must report via myOptigo for Investor Reporting. After reporting the regular scheduled payment, the Servicer must report in the Non-Cash Writedown field the unpaid principal balance (UPB) of the Mortgage after the regular scheduled payment is applied. If applicable, the Servicer must calculate interest and prepayment premium on the exception activity based on the amount shown in the Non-Cash Writedown field.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to each Mortgage sold in a Securitization and reported via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: If reported correctly, investor reporting reflects correct post-payment UPB; if not, reporting is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Interest/prepayment premium calculation applies only if exception activity is applicable

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 54; d. Mortgages sold in a Securitization (09/14/23)
source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must report via myOptigo for Investor Reporting each Mortgage sold in a Securitization. After reporting the regular scheduled payment, the Servicer must report in the Non-Cash Writedown field the unpaid principal balance (UPB) of the Mortgage after the regular scheduled payment is applied. If applicable, the Servicer must calculate the interest and prepayment premium on the exception activity (see Section 50.8), basing this calculation on the amount appearing in the Non-Cash Writedown field.

examples: Regular scheduled payment is applied and reduces UPB from $8,000,000 to $7,950,000; Servicer reports $7,950,000 in the Non-Cash Writedown field.
Servicer reports the pre-payment premium on exception activity using the Non-Cash Writedown amount rather than the pre-payment UPB; compliant with the basis requirement.
Servicer reports Non-Cash Writedown as the UPB before applying the scheduled payment; noncompliant because field must reflect post-payment UPB.

Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Mortgage Repurchase Must Be Reported as Exception Code 66 by Due Date
business_rules.description: For any repurchase required by Freddie Mac or requested by the Servicer and approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must report the repurchase via myOptigo for Investor Reporting coded as a Mortgage repurchase using Exception Code 66 no later than the due date specified by Freddie Mac. The Servicer must set the date on which the exception activity will occur at no more than 10 Business Days after Freddie Mac notified the Servicer that Freddie Mac is requiring or approving the repurchase.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac requires/approves a repurchase of a Mortgage or REO
business_rules.consequences: If timely, repurchase is properly recorded; if late, reporting violates Freddie Mac due date requirements
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For any repurchase required by Freddie Mac or requested by the Servicer and approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must report the repurchase via myOptigo for Investor Reporting (coded as a Mortgage repurchase using Exception Code 66) no later than the due date specified by Freddie Mac... The Servicer must set the date on which the exception activity will occur at no more than 10 Business Days after the date on which Freddie Mac notified the Servicer that Freddie Mac is requiring or approving the repurchase.

examples: Freddie Mac approval notice dated March 1; Servicer schedules exception activity date no later than March 15 (10 Business Days) and reports Exception Code 66 by Freddie Mac’s specified due date.
Servicer reports repurchase as a generic payoff code rather than Exception Code 66; noncompliant coding.
Servicer sets exception activity date 15 Business Days after Freddie Mac notice; noncompliant with the 10 Business Day limit.

business_rules.rule_name: Monthly delinquency report due by 3:00 pm ET on 3rd business day
business_rules.description: The Servicer must perform the automated transmission of the Monthly Report of Delinquencies and Foreclosures via myOptigo for Investor Reporting by 3:00 p.m. Eastern time on the third Business Day of each month. Submitted transmissions must be usable, accurate, and timely, and the Servicer is responsible for data accuracy and integrity.
business_rules.conditions: For each monthly reporting cycle for delinquencies and foreclosures via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: Meets: timely accepted report; Violated: report deemed late/omitted/unusable and may trigger fees
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must perform the automated transmission of the Monthly Report of Delinquencies and Foreclosures via myOptigo for Investor Reporting by 3:00 p.m. Eastern time on the third Business Day of each month. The Servicer is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and integrity of the data submitted to Freddie Mac. Transmissions must be usable, accurate and timely.

examples: Third Business Day is Wednesday; Servicer submits at 2:45 pm ET; compliant.
Servicer submits at 3:10 pm ET on the third Business Day; late and subject to noncompliance tracking.
Servicer submits by deadline but file is unusable; treated as unusable reporting data under fee rules.

business_rules.rule_name: PRS notice due within 30 calendar days of repair completion date with evidence
business_rules.description: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PRS within 30 calendar days of the Repair Agreement completion date that the Borrower is on track to complete all repairs by the due date. The Servicer must include evidence in PRS of the Borrower’s intention to complete repairs (e.g., executed repair contract, estimate for repair work, or itemized progress of repair work).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to each Repair Agreement with a defined completion date under Chapter 39.4
business_rules.consequences: If timely PRS notice with evidence is submitted, Freddie Mac is informed of progress; if not, Servicer is noncompliant and may be required to provide remediation/updates
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PRS within 30 calendar days of the completion date that the Borrower is on track to complete all repairs by the due date. The Servicer must include evidence in PRS of the Borrower’s intention to complete repairs. Such evidence may include an executed repair contract, estimate for repair work, or itemized progress of repair work.

examples: Completion date is June 30; Servicer submits PRS update by July 30 stating Borrower is on track and uploads an executed contractor agreement.
Servicer submits PRS update within 30 days but provides no supporting evidence: violates evidence requirement and must supplement PRS record.
Servicer uploads an itemized progress report and a repair estimate in PRS within the 30-day window: satisfies evidence requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (30 calendar days) and enumerated acceptable evidence types.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Completion notice due within 10 business days after servicer confirmation
business_rules.description: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days following the Servicer's confirmation of the Borrower's completion of all repairs under a Repair Agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Servicer confirms all repairs under a Repair Agreement are complete
business_rules.consequences: If notice is timely, Freddie Mac receives closure confirmation; if late/missing, Servicer must cure reporting deficiency and may face compliance findings
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days following the Servicer's confirmation of the Borrower's completion of all repairs under a Repair Agreement.

examples: Servicer confirms completion on March 1; Servicer must notify Freddie Mac no later than the 10th Business Day after March 1.
Servicer confirms completion but waits 15 Business Days to notify: violates the timing requirement and requires remediation.
Servicer confirms completion and submits notice within 3 Business Days: compliant.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 10 Business Day timeframe keyed to 'Servicer confirmation' event.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_001 requires PRS notification within 30 days of the Repair Agreement completion date that the Borrower is on track, with evidence. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 then requires notification within 10 Business Days after the Servicer confirms repairs are complete. They form a standard sequential monitoring-to-closure pattern for repair oversight.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the on-track notice is missed, Freddie may have reduced visibility into repair progress and may increase scrutiny. Even if completion notice is later provided, the Servicer may have a compliance finding for the earlier missed milestone and may need to provide remediation/updates.

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 sets a general completion-notification requirement. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_DOCUMENTATION_038_003 is a special-case control that replaces the generic 'notify' mechanism with a specific certification instrument and adds a 30-day certification collection requirement. Thus, for green/utility improvement repairs, compliance with 038_002 is achieved through the more specific 038_003 pathway.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer sends a generic completion notice without the required Verification Certification (or fails to collect it within 30 days), Freddie may deem the completion notification noncompliant, require re-submission in the correct form, and issue compliance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Partial repair completion notice due at least 30 days before due date via PRS
business_rules.description: At least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of partial completion of repairs using the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form submitted to PRS. If processing an extension request, the Servicer must add an update comment to PRS that an extension request will be submitted. Upon approval of the extension request, the Servicer must update PRS with approval information indicating that approval has been reflected in the Consent Request Tracker (CRT).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when repairs are partially completed approaching the Repair Agreement completion date
business_rules.consequences: If timely PRS notice and required PRS updates are made, Freddie Mac has visibility and tracking; if not, Servicer must cure and may be cited for noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: Extension request submitted only if appropriate

source_reference.source_text: At least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of partial completion of repairs on the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form, which must be submitted to PRS. ... If processing an extension request, the Servicer must add an update comment to PRS that an extension request will be submitted. Upon approval of the extension request, the Servicer must update PRS with approval information, indicating that approval has been reflected in the Consent Request Tracker (“CRT”).

examples: Completion date is August 31; by August 1 the Servicer submits the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form in PRS to report partial completion.
Servicer plans to request an extension and adds a PRS comment stating an extension request will be submitted; after approval, Servicer updates PRS noting CRT reflects approval.
Servicer submits partial completion notice 10 days before the completion date: violates the 'at least 30 days prior' requirement and requires remediation.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (≥30 days prior) plus required PRS workflow steps for extension tracking.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Easement must be perpetual and recorded before Funding Date
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage where the Property’s primary access to a public right of way is via an easement, the easement must be perpetual, must not allow loss of use upon breach (though it may allow a subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs), and must be recorded in the applicable land records prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date. The easement must also be included in the insured legal description of the Property.
business_rules.conditions: When an SBL Property relies on an easement for primary access to a public right of way
business_rules.consequences: If met: collateral access condition satisfied for purchase consideration; if violated: not eligible until cured
business_rules.exceptions: Subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs is permitted

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8SBL – SBL Property Fundamentals, d. Indirect access (06/30/16)
source_reference.source_text: 6. The easement must be perpetual. 7. Each party’s rights and responsibilities under the easement (including expenses) and remedies in the event of a breach by any other party must be generally acceptable to a prudent lender. 8. The easement may not allow for loss of use in the event of a breach. However, the easement may permit the placement of a lien which is subordinate to the SBL Mortgage for unpaid maintenance costs for the easement. 9. The easement must be recorded in the applicable land records for the jurisdiction prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date and must be included in the insured legal description of the Property.

examples: Easement is perpetual and recorded 5 days before Funding Date; title legal description includes it → eligible under the easement recording requirement.
Easement is perpetual but recorded 2 days after Funding Date → noncompliant; mortgage delivery should be held until recorded prior to Funding Date (cannot be retroactively satisfied).
Easement allows a maintenance lien that is expressly subordinate to the SBL Mortgage → permitted under item 8.
A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool

business_rules.rule_name: Remit Reimbursement and Cap or Swap Funds by 2nd Business Day
business_rules.description: For Targeted Affordable Housing Bond Credit Enhancement remittances, on the second Business Day after the Servicer receives any payments due to Freddie Mac under the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement, the Servicer must remit those payments to Freddie Mac via the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system (per Section 53.7).
business_rules.conditions: Applies while bond credit enhancement agreement is in effect and bonds not redeemed/canceled
business_rules.consequences: If met, timely remittance; if violated, late remittance exposure and reconciliation issues
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise stated; must also meet any differing Chapter 53 requirements

source_reference.source_text: So long as the bond credit enhancement agreement is in effect and all of the bonds have not been redeemed or canceled, the Servicer must remit, deposit or retain the funds collected under the Loan Documents, the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement in accordance with this section. On the second Business Day after the Servicer receives any payments due to Freddie Mac under the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement, the Servicer must remit such payments to Freddie Mac via the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance system, as described in Section 53.7.

examples: Servicer receives reimbursement payment on Monday; must remit to Freddie Mac by Wednesday (2nd Business Day after receipt).
Servicer receives cap payment due to Freddie on Friday; must remit by Tuesday (assuming Mon/Tue are Business Days).
Servicer remits on the 4th Business Day after receipt—violates timing requirement.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_013_006 mandates remittance to Freddie Mac via myOptigo on the second Business Day after receipt. Because BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 states Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after submission and imposes a 1:00 p.m. ET cutoff, it effectively constrains how the Servicer operationally meets the second Business Day remittance requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer waits until after 1:00 p.m. ET on the second Business Day, the payment may post late and breach BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_013_006, causing late remittance exposure and potential fees/reconciliation issues.

business_rules.rule_name: Remit Swap Payment to Provider Within 2 Business Days
business_rules.description: For bond credit enhancement transactions involving a swap, not later than two Business Days following receipt by the Servicer from the Borrower of any swap payment owed to the swap provider under the swap agreement, the Servicer must remit the swap payment to the swap provider.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Borrower pays swap amounts owed under the swap agreement
business_rules.consequences: If met, swap provider paid timely; if violated, swap payment delinquency risk increases
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: Not later than two Business Days following receipt by the Servicer from the Borrower of any swap payment owed to the swap provider under the swap agreement, the Servicer must remit such swap payment to the swap provider.

examples: Borrower wires swap payment Tuesday; Servicer must remit to swap provider by Thursday.
Borrower payment received Friday; Servicer must remit by Tuesday (assuming Mon/Tue are Business Days).
Servicer remits on the 3rd Business Day after receipt—violates rule.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

business_rules.rule_name: Remittance Request Due 1:00 p.m. ET 1 Business Day Early
business_rules.description: Because Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after the Servicer submits a remittance request through myOptigo Servicer Remittance, the Servicer must submit each remittance no later than 1:00 p.m. Eastern time one Business Day preceding the remittance due date. Remittances submitted after 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time will be processed the following Business Day. Remittance can be submitted in advance for up to 30 days (excluding non-business days).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to interim or monthly remittances initiated via myOptigo portal
business_rules.consequences: If late, remittance posts next Business Day and interest reimbursement fee may apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 53, Availability of funds; timing of remittances (10/19/23)
source_reference.source_text: Since Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after the Servicer submits a remittance request through the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance portal, the Servicer must submit each remittance to the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance System (see Section 53.7) no later than 1:00 p.m. Eastern time one Business Day preceding the remittance due date.
