dependencies.rationale: Manufactured home endorsement requirements apply to the title policy. Confirming baseline title policy compliance (coverage amount) is a typical step before verifying product-specific endorsements.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If baseline title policy requirements are not met, endorsement compliance cannot make the title package deliverable; remediation must start with policy reissuance.

business_rules.rule_name: Revocable trust loans: exclude trust name from delivery data and deliver with SFC 168
business_rules.description: For loans with an inter vivos revocable trust as mortgagor, only individual borrower information for the trust settlor(s) whose credit is used to qualify should be delivered (e.g., borrower name and SSN). The trust name must not be included in loan delivery data. The loan must be delivered with Special Feature Code (SFC) 168 in addition to any other applicable SFCs.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when an inter vivos revocable trust is a mortgagor/party to the security instrument.
business_rules.consequences: If delivered with trust name included or without SFC 168, delivery data is noncompliant and may require correction or impact purchase eligibility.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: Only the information related to the individual(s) establishing the inter vivos revocable trust whose credit is used to qualify for the loan should be provided at the time of loan delivery, such as the borrower name and Social Security number. The name of the inter vivos revocable trust cannot be included within the loan delivery data. A loan that has an inter vivos revocable trust as a mortgagor must be delivered with Special Feature Code 168 (in addition to any other special feature codes that may also be applicable to the transaction).

examples: Borrower qualifies using settlor income; delivery data includes borrower SSN but not the trust name; SFC 168 included—compliant.
Delivery file includes trust name in borrower name field—noncompliant and must be corrected before/after delivery.
Trust loan delivered without SFC 168—noncompliant and may trigger delivery suspense or post-delivery corrections.
30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

business_rules.rule_name: Green MBS must be 100% eligible and include SFC 784 or 881
business_rules.description: To be considered a Green MBS, 100% of the loans in the MBS must be Green MBS-eligible. The lender must deliver one of the specified special feature codes: SFC 784 (ENERGY STAR green building certification) or SFC 881 (Department of Energy Zero Ready Home), in addition to any other SFCs that apply.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when delivering an MBS pool as a Green MBS
business_rules.consequences: If met, pool can be considered Green MBS; if not, pool is not Green MBS
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: To be considered a Green MBS, 100% of the loans in the MBS must be eligible. One of the following special feature codes must be delivered in addition to any other SFCs that may apply to the transaction: SFC 784 - ENERGY STAR green building certiﬁcation, or SFC 881 - Department of Energy Zero Ready Home.

examples: Pool of 50 loans where all 50 meet Green certification rules and each loan includes SFC 784; pool can be Green MBS.
Pool includes 1 non-eligible loan (49/50 eligible); pool cannot be treated as Green MBS even if other loans have SFCs.
All loans eligible but lender omits SFC 784/881 in delivery data; pool is not considered Green MBS until corrected.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery data correction required; Green MBS designation denied

examples: 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage delivered to Fannie Mae
Adjustable-rate mortgage with initial fixed period and subsequent adjustments
Refinance mortgage loan requiring appraisal and eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_029_003 requires 100% of the loans in the MBS be Green-eligible and mandates delivery of specific SFCs tied to green certifications. BR_029_001 is the per-loan Green eligibility definition. Thus, BR_029_001 is an absolute prerequisite input to determine whether BR_029_003 can be satisfied.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_029_001 is not verified for each loan, the pool could be incorrectly labeled Green MBS and delivered with Green SFCs, causing compliance defects, pool reclassification, or remedies.

dependencies.rationale: Even if each loan has an approved certification, the Green execution also requires the sale/pool issuance be within the allowed five-year window (BR_029_002). BR_029_003 then operationalizes Green MBS delivery (100% green-eligible loans and SFC 784/881). These rules collectively define compliant Green MBS delivery and should be checked together, with BR_029_002 acting as a key eligibility attribute for BR_029_003’s 100% test.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_029_002 is missed, a pool may be coded as Green (BR_029_003) even though issuance year is outside the allowable timeframe, causing misdelivery and potential corrective action.

business_rules.rule_name: Support or Alimony Obligations Over 10 Months Must Be Counted
business_rules.description: If the borrower is required to pay alimony, child support, equalization payments, or separate maintenance under a divorce decree/separation agreement/other written legal agreement and the payments must continue for more than ten months, the payments must be treated as recurring monthly debt obligations. The lender must obtain and retain documentation confirming the obligation amount. For alimony, equalization, and separate maintenance, the lender may reduce qualifying income by the obligation amount instead of including it as a monthly DTI payment (with DU entry as negative income when applicable).
business_rules.conditions: Borrower has court-ordered support obligation continuing more than 10 months
business_rules.consequences: If included/reduced correctly, DTI is compliant; if omitted, underwriting defect risk
business_rules.exceptions: Voluntary payments excluded; special exception language noted for alimony

source_reference.source_text: 505When the borrower is required to pay alimony, child support, equalization payments, or separate maintenance payments under a divorce decree, separation agreement, or any other written legal agreement—and those payments must continue to be made for more than ten months—the payments must be considered as part of the borrower’s recurring monthly debt obligations. However, voluntary payments do not need to be taken into consideration and an exception is allowed for alimony. A copy of the divorce decree, separation agreement, court order, or equivalent documentation conﬁrming the amount of the obligation must be obtained and retained in the loan ﬁle. For alimony, equalization payments, and separate maintenance obligations, the lender has the option to reduce the qualifying income by the amount of the obligation in lieu of including it as a monthly payment in the calculation of the DTI ratio.

examples: Child support $600/month for 18 more months: must be included in recurring monthly debts for DTI.
Alimony $1,200/month for 24 more months: lender may either include $1,200 as a debt or reduce qualifying income by $1,200 per policy.
Voluntary support payment without legal agreement: does not have to be counted under this section.
U.S. citizen borrower purchasing an owner-occupied home
Permanent resident borrower applying for a conforming loan
Borrower with multiple financed properties seeking an additional mortgage

business_rules.rule_name: Nontraditional creditor references must include fields and 0 x 30, 0 x 60, 0 x 90 format
business_rules.description: When individual credit references (other than housing payments) are obtained directly from a creditor for a borrower’s nontraditional credit history, each reference must include: creditor name; name of individual providing the reference; account open date; highest credit amount; current status; required payment amount; unpaid balance; and payment history. The historical status must be stated in a “number of times past due” format using “0 X 30, 0 X 60, 0 X 90” days late. Vague statements such as “current,” “satisfactory,” or “pays as agreed” are not acceptable by themselves.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when lender documents nontraditional credit using creditor-provided references (non-housing)
business_rules.consequences: If provided as required, reference is acceptable; if missing fields/format, reference is unacceptable and credit profile may fail requirements
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B3-5.4-03, Standards for Individual Credit References
source_reference.source_text: Individual credit references (other than housing payments) from a creditor must include the following: the creditor’s name, the name of the individual providing the reference, the date the account was opened, the amount of highest credit, the current status of the account, the required payment amount, the unpaid balance, and the payment history. The historical status of each account must be stated in a “number of times past due” format using “0 X 30, 0 X 60, 0 X 90” days late. Note : Vague statements such as “current,” “satisfactory,” or “pays as agreed” are not acceptable by themselves.

examples: Utility company letter includes open date, high credit, balance, payment amount, and shows “0×30, 0×60, 0×90” → acceptable.
Cell phone provider letter says only “pays as agreed” with no late-count format → unacceptable by itself; must be corrected or replaced.
Creditor reference omits unpaid balance → incomplete and must be supplemented to qualify as an acceptable nontraditional reference.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for delivery; potential repurchase demand

examples: U.S. citizen borrower purchasing an owner-occupied home
Permanent resident borrower applying for a conforming loan
Borrower with multiple financed properties seeking an additional mortgage

business_rules.rule_name: DU tax transcript year depends on casefile date April 30 cutoff
business_rules.description: For tax return transcript income validation, the lender must provide the most recent tax transcript determined by DU casefile creation date: (a) casefiles created on or before April 30—provide prior year (current year minus 1); if that prior year return has not been filed or transcript unavailable, provide current year minus 2; (b) casefiles created after April 30—provide prior year (current year minus 1).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when DU uses Tax Return Transcript for income validation recency
business_rules.consequences: Correct transcript year enables income validation; wrong year prevents validation
business_rules.exceptions: On/before April 30 may use year-2 if year-1 not filed/available

source_reference.source_text: For loan caseﬁles created on or before April 30, the most recent tax transcript must be provided. The most recent tax transcript would be for the prior year (current year minus 1). If the prior year tax return has not yet been ﬁled or the transcript is not yet available, the most recent tax transcript will be the current year minus 2. For loan caseﬁles created after April 30, the most recent year tax transcript must be provided for validation to be completed. The most recent tax transcript will be for the prior year (current year minus 1).

examples: Casefile created April 15, 2026: lender provides 2025 transcript (2026−1) for validation; if 2025 not filed, provides 2024 transcript (2026−2).
Casefile created May 10, 2026: lender must provide 2025 transcript; providing 2024 transcript would be insufficient for validation to be completed.
Casefile created April 30, 2026: treated as 'on or before April 30' and may use 2024 transcript only if 2025 transcript unavailable.
DU casefile receiving Approve/Eligible recommendation
DU casefile receiving Refer with Caution recommendation
DU casefile using DU Validation Service for income/asset validation

dependencies.rationale: BR_014_006 may direct the lender to provide a prior-year transcript determined by DU casefile creation date. BR_008_006 can require the most recent year’s tax return in a specific date window and disallow Form 4868. If DU requests a transcript year that differs from what the file must have under BR_008_006, the lender must satisfy both by obtaining the most restrictive set (e.g., include most recent return per BR_008_006 even if DU transcript logic points elsewhere).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If unresolved, lender may follow DU transcript instruction but fail mortgage file documentation prohibition in BR_008_006, resulting in documentation deficiencies and potential delivery/QC findings.

dependencies.rationale: BR_DESKTOP_UNDERWRITER_DOCUMENTATION_014_006 defines which tax transcript year DU requires based on casefile creation date. BR_MORTGAGE_FILE_CONSTRAINT_036_007 imposes a separate, disaster-driven recency constraint on underwriting documentation (including income verifications). In FEMA disaster scenarios, satisfying the transcript-year rule is not sufficient if the income verification documentation is stale by note date; the disaster rule becomes an additional prerequisite for final acceptability.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the income verification documentation is older than 180 days: (1) updated verification/timing refresh is required, (2) underwriting conditions re-open, (3) potential closing delay while updated documentation is obtained.

business_rules.rule_name: Depository asset statements: 60 days purchase or 30 days refinance and dated within 45 days
business_rules.description: When DU requires verification of depository assets, the lender must provide two consecutive monthly bank statements (60 days) for purchase transactions, or one monthly statement (30 days) for limited cash-out and cash-out refinances. Monthly statements must be dated within 45 days of the initial loan application date. Quarterly statements must be dated within 90 days, and the lender must confirm funds were not transferred to another account verified with more current documentation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when DU requires verification of depository assets (checking/savings/MM/CD).
business_rules.consequences: If documentation meets timing/quantity rules, asset verification satisfied; if not, loan fails asset documentation requirements.
business_rules.exceptions: Verification of Deposit (Form 1006) may replace statements.

source_reference.source_text: For depository assets (checking and savings accounts, money market funds, and certiﬁcates of deposit), DU will require the following: two consecutive monthly bank statements (60 days of account activity) for all purchase transactions, or one monthly statement (30 days of account activity) for all limited cash-out and cash-out reﬁnance transactions. Monthly bank statements must be dated within 45 days of the initial loan application date. Quarterly bank statements must be dated within 90 days of the initial loan application date, and the lender must conﬁrm that the funds in the account have not been transferred to another asset account that is veriﬁed with more current documentation. A Veriﬁcation of Deposit (Form 1006) can be obtained in place of bank statements.

examples: Purchase: borrower provides March and April statements (60 days) dated within 45 days of application—acceptable.
Cash-out refinance: borrower provides a single monthly statement showing 30 days of activity dated 70 days before application—unacceptable due to 45-day dating rule.
Limited cash-out refinance: lender obtains Form 1006 instead of bank statements—acceptable substitute.
DU casefile receiving Approve/Eligible recommendation
DU casefile receiving Refer with Caution recommendation
DU casefile using DU Validation Service for income/asset validation

dependencies.rationale: BR_025_005 applies "when DU requires verification of depository assets." BR_025_004 states that for refinance transactions DU will not require documentation of assets when total funds to be verified are $500 or less. Therefore BR_025_004 overrides/turns off the applicability of BR_025_005 in that scenario.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the exception is not recognized, lender may over-collect documents (process inefficiency) or mis-handle DU conditions. If the exception is misapplied, lender may fail to obtain required statements for >$500, causing underwriting conditions and delivery defects.

dependencies.rationale: BR_025_005 requires bank statements dated within 45/90 days of initial application (depending on statement type) and sets 30/60-day coverage requirements by transaction type. BR_008_004 adds an overarching constraint: credit documents (including asset documentation) must be no more than four months old on the note date. A statement can satisfy BR_025_005 yet become stale by note date, so the rules operate together in compliance checking.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_008_004 fails, the lender must update asset documents even if BR_025_005 was met at application; otherwise the loan file is stale at closing and noncompliant.

dependencies.rationale: BR_MORTGAGE_FILE_CONSTRAINT_036_007 requires underwriting documentation (including verifications of income and assets) to be dated no more than 180 days before the note date when in a FEMA-Declared Disaster Area eligible for Individual Assistance. BR_DESKTOP_UNDERWRITER_DOCUMENTATION_025_005 is an asset verification documentation rule (bank statements and timing relative to application). In disaster scenarios, both constraints must be satisfied; the disaster rule acts as an additional prerequisite for acceptability of those documents at closing/delivery.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 180-day dating is exceeded: (1) updated bank statement(s) must be obtained, (2) underwriting conditions must be reopened, (3) closing may be delayed until refreshed docs are in file.

business_rules.rule_name: Blanket authorization must be signed and reused by copy
business_rules.description: The lender must obtain the borrower’s signature on the Blanket Authorization form to obtain the documentation needed to evaluate the borrower’s creditworthiness. A photocopy of the signed form also serves as authorization, and the information obtained is only to be used in processing the mortgage loan application.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to borrower creditworthiness documentation requests during application
business_rules.consequences: Signed authorization supports verifications; missing signature blocks verifications
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The lender obtains the borrower’s signature on the following Blanket Authorization form to obtain the documentation needed to evaluate the borrower’s creditworthiness. This borrower-signed document gives the lender blanket authorization to request the information needed to document the borrower’s creditworthiness... It is understood that a photocopy of this form also will serve as authorization. The information the lender obtains is only to be used in the processing of my application for a mortgage loan.

examples: Borrower signs the Blanket Authorization at application; lender orders a credit report and VOE using that authorization.
Lender uses a photocopy of the signed authorization to request verification of deposit from a bank; bank accepts the copy as authorization.
If the authorization is unsigned, the lender cannot properly order/verify credit and asset information and must obtain a signature before proceeding.
Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

business_rules.rule_name: Omitted credit liability with balance > $0 needs support
business_rules.description: If a liability appears on the borrower’s credit report with a balance greater than zero but is omitted from the loan application, the lender must obtain supporting documentation addressing the omission.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when credit report shows a liability balance > $0 omitted from application.
business_rules.consequences: Meets: omission supported; Violated: underwriting defect and delivery/QC risk.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: Trended Credit Data Lenders are not required to analyze trended credit data in the credit report. For more information, see B3-2-03, Risk Factors Evaluated by DU . Omitted Accounts Supporting documentation is required when a credit report liability with a balance greater than zero is omitted from the loan application.

examples: Credit report shows an auto loan with $8,500 balance omitted from the 1003; lender obtains documentation showing it was paid off and updates/resubmits as needed.
Borrower omitted a $1 balance store card; because balance is > $0, lender documents why it is excluded/paid and retains support.
Credit report shows a closed account with $0 balance omitted; this rule does not trigger because balance is not greater than zero.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand (if material and not cured)
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit requirement with numeric threshold (balance > 0).

examples: Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_APPLICATION_DOCUMENTATION_010_006 requires documentation when a liability appears on the borrower’s credit report but is omitted from the application. BR_LOAN_APPLICATION_VALIDATION_032_006 defines what constitutes an acceptable in-file credit report (multi-repository). The lender must first obtain/confirm an acceptable credit report before it can identify omitted liabilities and apply BR_010_006.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the credit report does not meet BR_032_006, the lender risks missing omitted debts, understating DTI, and creating delivery/QC defects; underwriting may need to be reworked after an updated/tri-merge equivalent report is obtained.

business_rules.rule_name: Seasonal Income Requires Form 1005 or 2 Years W-2s
business_rules.description: When seasonal income is used to qualify, the lender must obtain either (i) a completed Request for Verification of Employment (Form 1005) or (ii) the most recent paystub and two years’ W-2s. In all cases, a verbal verification of employment (VOE) is also required per the verbal VOE policy cross-reference.
business_rules.conditions: Seasonal income is included as qualifying income on the loan file
business_rules.consequences: If met, seasonal income may be evaluated; if not, income cannot be used to qualify
business_rules.exceptions: For seasonal unemployment comp, verify ties to seasonal layoffs and tax returns

source_reference.source_text: Documentation The lender must obtain • a completed Request for Veriﬁcation of Employment (Form 1005); or • the most recent paystub and two years' W-2s. Additionally, a verbal VOE is required. See B3-3.1-04, Verbal Veriﬁcation of Employment, for speciﬁc requirements.

examples: Borrower uses seasonal landscaping income; lender includes Form 1005 and completes verbal VOE—income may be considered.
Borrower provides only one W-2 and no Form 1005; lender cannot support seasonal income—exclude it from qualifying income.
Seasonal unemployment compensation is used; lender documents it on borrower tax returns as tied to seasonal layoffs per referenced policy.
Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_APPLICATION_DOCUMENTATION_015_001 requires a completed VOE (Form 1005) OR paystub+W-2s and also requires a verbal VOE. If the lender uses the Form 1005 option, BR_MORTGAGE_FILE_VALIDATION_008_003 controls whether the 1005 is valid (authorization attached, direct from institution, signed/dated). Thus, compliant seasonal-income documentation via Form 1005 depends on satisfying the verification-form integrity requirements in BR_008_003.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_008_003 conditions are not met (e.g., 1005 not direct source or missing authorization), the seasonal income documentation path relying on Form 1005 becomes defective; lender may have to switch to paystub/W-2 documentation and/or re-verify, delaying underwriting.

dependencies.rationale: BR_015_002 sets the threshold eligibility for seasonal income (minimum two-year history). BR_015_001 provides documentation requirements when seasonal income is used to qualify. Eligibility must be established before documentation is treated as supporting qualifying income.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_015_002 fails (<2-year history), the lender must exclude seasonal income regardless of documents obtained, requiring re-qualification and potentially causing denial or restructuring.

dependencies.rationale: BR_015_001 allows the lender to use "the most recent paystub" as part of the documentation option. BR_029_008 defines what qualifies as an acceptable paystub (dated within 30 days of application and includes YTD). Therefore, if paystubs are used under BR_015_001, they must be validated under BR_029_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_029_008 fails, the paystub component of BR_015_001 becomes unacceptable, requiring updated paystub(s) or alternative documentation, delaying processing.

business_rules.rule_name: Capital gains income requires 2 years tax returns
business_rules.description: When capital gains income is used to qualify, the lender must obtain the borrower’s signed personal federal income tax returns for the most recent two years (including Form 1040 and Schedule D) and evidence of a portfolio of assets that can be sold for future payments. Current receipt of capital gains income is not required for allowable age compliance, but asset ownership documentation must comply with allowable age requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Borrower uses capital gains income to qualify for the loan
business_rules.consequences: If docs complete, capital gains may be used; if not, income cannot qualify
business_rules.exceptions: Current receipt not required; asset ownership docs must meet age rules

source_reference.source_text: However, if the borrower is using income from capital gains to qualify, the income must be veriﬁed in accordance with the following requirements. The lender must obtain: • copies of the borrower’s signed personal federal income tax returns for the most recent two years, including IRS Form 1040, Schedule D, and • evidence that the borrower owns a portfolio of assets that can be sold if additional income is needed to make future mortgage loan payments Note: Due to the nature of this income, the current receipt of the income is not required to comply with the Allowable Age of Credit Documents policy. However, documentation of the asset ownership must be in compliance with the Allowable Age of Credit Documents policy

examples: Borrower claims $3,000/month capital gains: lender gets 2 years 1040 + Schedule D and brokerage statement evidencing asset ownership; income can be considered.
Borrower provides only 1 year tax return: lender cannot use capital gains income for qualifying until 2 years are obtained.
Borrower has acceptable tax returns but outdated asset ownership evidence beyond allowable age: lender must refresh asset documentation before using income.
Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

business_rules.rule_name: Subject Rental Income Requires Form 1007 or 1025
business_rules.description: If the borrower has a history of receiving rental income from the subject property and the transaction is a refinance, the lender must obtain the applicable rent schedule (Form 1007 for one-unit or Form 1025 for two- to four-unit), plus either the borrower’s most recent year of signed federal income tax returns (including Schedule 1 and Schedule E) or current lease agreement(s) only when the borrower documents a qualifying exception.
business_rules.conditions: Subject property rental income used; borrower has rental history; refinance
business_rules.consequences: If docs obtained, rental income may be evaluated; if missing, rental income cannot be used
business_rules.exceptions: Lease may substitute for returns only with documented qualifying exception

source_reference.section_id: B3-3.8-01 (Documenting Rental Income from Subject Property table)
source_reference.source_text: Yes Reﬁnance Form 1007 or Form 1025, as applicable, and either • the borrower’s most recent year of signed federal income tax returns, including Schedule 1 and Schedule E, or • copies of the current lease agreement(s) if the borrower can document a qualifying exception (see Reconciling Partial or No Rental History on Tax Returns  below).

examples: Refinance 1-unit: Lender gets Form 1007 and 2024 signed 1040 with Schedule E; rental income can be considered.
Refinance 3-unit: Lender gets Form 1025 and leases only, and documents qualifying exception (e.g., significant interruption); acceptable per guide.
Refinance: Lender provides neither Schedule E nor exception-supported lease; rental income must not be used to qualify.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for purchase / potential repurchase demand

examples: Loan application submitted for purchase of a primary residence
Loan application submitted for refinance requiring updated appraisal
Loan application underwritten through Desktop Underwriter (DU)

dependencies.rationale: BR_034_002 permits current lease agreement(s) as documentation (only when a qualifying exception is documented). BR_034_001 sets a content restriction on lease agreements used to document rental income: the rental payment must be in U.S. dollars and virtual currency is not permitted. Thus, lease acceptability is a prerequisite to using the lease as documentation under BR_034_002.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the lease payment is denominated in virtual currency, the lease cannot be used to support rental income; lender must obtain tax returns and rent schedule or otherwise exclude rental income, potentially affecting qualification.

business_rules.rule_name: Manual files must retain Form 1008; DU files retain final report
business_rules.description: For manually underwritten mortgage loans, Form 1008 (or a similar document) must be retained in the mortgage file. For loans underwritten with DU, the final DU Underwriting Analysis Report must be retained in the mortgage file. For DU loans, retaining Form 1008 is optional (may, but not required).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when underwriting method is manual or Desktop Underwriter (DU)
business_rules.consequences: Retention supports audit/QC; missing required item causes file deficiency
business_rules.exceptions: For DU loans, Form 1008 retention is optional (not required)

source_reference.section_id: B1-1-02, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary and DU Underwriting Analysis Report
source_reference.source_text: The Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary (Form 1008) summarizes key data from the loan application package... Form 1008 (or a similar document) must be retained in the mortgage file for manually underwritten mortgage loans. Lenders may, but are not required to, retain Form 1008 for loans underwritten with DU. For loans underwritten with DU, the final DU Underwriting Analysis Report must be retained in the mortgage file.

examples: Manual underwrite: lender places a completed Form 1008 in the closed loan file to meet the retention requirement.
DU underwrite: lender retains the final DU Underwriting Analysis Report in the file; Form 1008 is absent and still compliant.
DU underwrite: if the final DU report is missing from the file, QC flags a documentation defect requiring remediation.
business_rules.enforcement_action: QC finding; remediation or repurchase risk if defects are material

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: If tax return not obtained Apr 15 to Jun 30, signed 4506-C required
business_rules.description: For application dates April 15 through June 30 of the current year, the most recent year’s tax return is recommended but previous year(s) are acceptable. If the most recent year’s tax return is not obtained, the loan file must include a completed and signed IRS Form 4506-C for transcripts of tax returns provided by the borrower to the lender.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when tax returns are required and application date is Apr 15–Jun 30
business_rules.consequences: With 4506-C, file meets alternative documentation; without it, file deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B1-1-03, Allowable Age of Federal Income Tax Returns
source_reference.source_text: April 15, current year to June 30, current year The most recent year’s tax return is recommended; however, the previous year(s) is also acceptable. In the event the most recent year's tax return is not obtained, the loan file must include a completed and signed IRS Form 4506-C for transcripts of tax returns provided by the borrower to the lender.

examples: Application 2026-05-20: lender uses 2024 and 2023 returns; since 2025 is not obtained, lender includes a signed 4506-C in the file.
Application 2026-06-15: borrower provides 2025 return; 4506-C is not required by this specific fallback condition.
Application 2026-04-20: lender fails to obtain 2025 return and omits 4506-C; loan file is incomplete and must be corrected.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting condition; potential post-purchase documentation defect

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: Asset verification report must cover 30 or 60 days minimum
business_rules.description: When a third-party asset verification report is used, the mortgage loan file must retain the report covering the minimum time period required for the purpose of the assessment. For cash flow assessment, the file must contain a minimum of 30 days for limited cash-out refinances and 60 days for purchase transactions. For rent payment history, the file must contain a minimum of 60 days for purchase transactions. When income or employment is validated using an asset verification report and the full report containing all data submitted to DU is not retained, the lender must have access to the full report at origination and retain ongoing access (no shorter than the lender’s full loan file documentation retention period) with the ability to reproduce it.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a third-party asset verification report is used for DU/underwriting purposes.
business_rules.consequences: Meets: file is QC-ready; Violated: documentation defect and QC findings risk.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; different minimum periods apply by assessment purpose.

source_reference.section_id: A2-4.1-01, Retention of Third-Party Asset Verification Reports
source_reference.source_text: Cash ﬂow assessmentA minimum of • 30 days (limited cash-out reﬁnances); • 60 days (purchase transactions) Rent payment history A minimum of 60 days (purchase transactions) Income or employment validation using an asset veriﬁcation report• The full asset veriﬁcation report containing the entirety of the data submitted to DU, or • The greater of ⚬ the period of time (30- or 60 -days) required for asset veriﬁcation for the transaction, or ⚬ 30 -days if no asset documentation is required for the transaction When income or employment is validated using an asset veriﬁcation report and the full asset veriﬁcation report containing the entirety of the data submitted to DU is not retained in the loan ﬁle, the lender must • have access to the full asset veriﬁcation report at the time of origination; • retain on-going access to the full asset veriﬁcation report.

examples: Purchase loan uses rent history via asset verification; file retains at least 60 days of report data—meets requirement.
Limited cash-out refinance uses cash-flow assessment; file retains at least 30 days—meets requirement.
Lender keeps only a shortened asset report excerpt; to comply, it must retain ongoing access to the full report and be able to reproduce it for QC.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Compensatory fee, indemnification, or repurchase demand (if material defects)
business_rules.extraction_notes: Strong numeric specificity (30/60 days) and explicit retention/access requirements.

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_010_010 requires retaining third-party asset verification reports for the minimum time period required for the purpose (cash flow, rent history, income/employment validation). BR_017_002 defines when an asset verification report is eligible to be used for DU validation of income/employment. When BR_017_002 is satisfied and the report is used for that purpose, BR_010_010 retention becomes operationally required to evidence DU validation inputs.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_010_010 is not met, the lender may pass underwriting initially but fail post-close QC due to missing third-party report artifacts supporting DU validation reliance.

dependencies.rationale: BR_014_005 requires confirming borrower ownership/name on the asset verification report over the relevant period. BR_010_010 requires retaining the asset verification report that was used. In practice, the workflow is: obtain report → perform ownership/name confirmation → retain report in file. Thus retention is sequentially dependent on (and supports evidencing) the BR_014_005 review.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the report is not retained, the lender cannot evidence the BR_014_005 confirmation in audit/QC, increasing post-close findings risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1084 cash flow analysis must be in permanent loan file
business_rules.description: When qualifying self-employment income under the one-year returns option, the lender must complete Fannie Mae’s Cash Flow Analysis (Form 1084) or another cash flow analysis form applying the same principles, and a copy of the written analysis must be included in the permanent loan file.
business_rules.conditions: When lender documents self-employed income using the one-year returns option
business_rules.consequences: If written cash flow analysis is in file, requirement met; if missing, file is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B3-3.5-01, Verification of Income (cash flow analysis requirement)
source_reference.source_text: when there are multiple self- employment income sources. the lender must complete Fannie Mae’s Cash Flow Analysis (Form 1084 ) or any other type of cash ﬂow analysis form that applies the same principles. A copy of the written analysis must be included in the permanent loan ﬁle. Published

examples: Lender uses one year returns based on 5-year business/ownership: file includes completed Form 1084 and meets the requirement.
Lender uses a third-party cash flow worksheet applying the same principles as Form 1084 and retains it in the permanent file: acceptable if equivalent.
Lender qualifies income but fails to retain any written cash flow analysis in the file: documentation defect that can trigger post-purchase findings.
Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_012_007 requires a written cash flow analysis (Form 1084 or equivalent) to be included in the file for the one-year returns option. BR_012_008 states that if the lender uses Income Calculator, the Findings Report may be used to satisfy the written analysis requirement (including Form 1084). Thus, using Income Calculator overrides the need to separately produce/retain Form 1084 as long as the Findings Report is retained.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_012_008 is not recognized, lender may create duplicative analyses; if it is misapplied (Findings Report not retained), the file fails the written-analysis requirement and becomes deficient.

business_rules.rule_name: Income Calculator findings report must be kept in loan file
business_rules.description: If the lender uses Income Calculator, the Income Calculator Findings Report may be used to satisfy the requirement to prepare a written analysis of qualifying income calculation (e.g., Form 1084, Form 1088, or similar). A copy of the Findings Report must be maintained in the loan file.
business_rules.conditions: When lender uses Income Calculator to calculate qualifying income for the loan
business_rules.consequences: If report is retained, written-analysis requirement may be satisfied; if not, file deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Income Calculator Findings Report can be used to satisfy the requirements for the lender to prepare a written analysis of their evaluation regarding the calculation of qualifying income, such as Fannie Mae's Cash Flow Analysis (Form 1084), Comparative Income Analysis (Form 1088) or a similar form. A copy of the Findings Report must be maintained in the loan ﬁle.

examples: Lender runs Income Calculator and retains the Findings Report in the file: satisfies the written analysis retention requirement.
Lender uses Income Calculator but only retains screenshots, not the Findings Report: fails the “must be maintained” requirement.
Manual underwrite using Income Calculator output: Findings Report retained supports written analysis documentation.
Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: Trust note allonge must identify note with 4 elements and be affixed
business_rules.description: When a lender uses a signature addendum (allonge) for trustee signatures on a mortgage note for an inter vivos revocable trust, (1) all individual borrowers must sign the note itself, (2) the addendum must be permanently affixed to the note, (3) the addendum must clearly identify the note by referencing at least the borrower name, the date of the note, the amount of the note, and the address of the security property, and (4) the note must clearly and accurately reference the attached addendum. The lender must indemnify Fannie Mae for losses resulting from the use of a signature addendum.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a signature addendum/allonge is used for trustee signatures.
business_rules.consequences: If not met: signature may be unenforceable; lender indemnifies Fannie Mae losses.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; lender must modify forms for non-CA states as needed.

source_reference.section_id: E-2-04 (02/27/2018), Use of a Signature Addendum to Note
source_reference.source_text: In these situations, the lender may use a separate attachment or addendum (sometimes referred to as an allonge) for the borrower signatures, so long as the following requirements are met: All individual borrower(s) must sign the note itself; lenders may use a signature addendum for trustee signatures. The addendum must be permanently aﬃxed to the related note and must clearly identify the note by referencing at least the name of the borrower, the date of the note, the amount of the note, and the address of the security property. The note must clearly and accurately reference the attached signature addendum... The lender must indemnify Fannie Mae...

examples: Borrower signs the note; two trustees sign on an allonge permanently affixed that lists borrower name, note date, note amount, and property address; note references the allonge.
Allonge is stapled loosely and does not include the note amount; this fails the requirement and creates enforceability/indemnification risk.
Lender originates in a non-CA state and updates terminology and signature format to meet local law; retains compliant allonge and note references in file.
Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: eNote Must Use Uniform Form and 18-Digit MIN at Execution
business_rules.description: To be eligible for sale to Fannie Mae, eMortgages must be originated using the Uniform Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac form of eNote (modified per the eNote header/footer/clause requirements, including the electronic-note provision referencing the MERS eRegistry). Immediately upon execution by the borrower, all eNotes must be registered on the MERS eRegistry and contain a valid, unique 18-digit Mortgage Identification Number (MIN). Fannie Mae requires creation of eNotes in the MISMO SMART Doc® format.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when the loan is an eMortgage intended for sale to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.consequences: If not Uniform eNote/18-digit MIN/SMART Doc, loan is ineligible for sale.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: To be eligible for sale to Fannie Mae, eMortgages must be originated using the Uniform Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac form of eNote... Such modification includes addition of a specific provision (Section 11, or Section 12 in the case of ARM eNotes) that addresses the electronic nature of the note. This special provision also includes reference to the MERS eRegistry. Immediately upon execution by the borrower, all eNotes must be registered on the MERS eRegistry and contain a valid, unique 18-digit Mortgage Identification Number (MIN). Fannie Mae requires creation of eNotes in the MISMO SMART Doc® format.

examples: Borrower signs an eNote in SMART Doc format; MIN is 18 digits and registration occurs immediately → meets rule.
Lender uses a proprietary eNote template instead of Uniform eNote → ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
eNote is Uniform but MIN is missing or not 18 digits → ineligible until corrected/re-executed as required.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: eMortgage Records Retained for Life of Loan Plus 7 Years
business_rules.description: For eMortgages, the lender must retain documents and system records supporting eSignature attribution and identity for the life of the loan plus seven years. Retained files must include, at minimum: the name of the person (and related entity if not a natural person) who signed each document, the signature date, the signing method, and relevant identifying information to verify the electronic signature and its attribution. Lenders must provide Fannie Mae requested audit trail files in a format compatible with Fannie Mae systems, and upon servicing transfer must provide the new servicer with all attribution evidence and audit trails for transferred loans.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to eMortgages sold or to be sold to Fannie Mae.
business_rules.consequences: If retention/audit trail not available, evidentiary support is deficient and noncompliant.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: To preserve the lender's ability to attribute the signer's signature to their identity... the lender must retain evidence of a borrower's identity in the loan file... the lender must collect and retain systems logs and audit trails... Additionally, the lender must collect evidence of a borrower's agreement to conduct the transaction electronically... All files must be retained for the life of the loan plus seven years. Any files that a lender maintains must include the following: the name of the person... the date of the signature, the method by which the document was signed, and any relevant identifying... information... Lenders must provide Fannie Mae with any audit trail files requested... In the event of a servicing transfer, the lender is required to provide the new servicer with all attribution evidence and audit trails

examples: Loan paid off after 6 years; lender retains eClosing audit logs until 13 years after payoff (life + 7) → compliant.
Fannie Mae requests audit trail showing ID presentation and eSignature event; lender cannot produce logs → noncompliant retention.
Servicing transfers to new servicer; lender provides complete attribution evidence package with audit trails → compliant.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan ineligible for sale; potential repurchase/indemnification exposure.

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: Remote ink-signed notarization needs 2-ID or 2-factor and 10-year recording
business_rules.description: For audio-visual aided remote ink-signed notarization (RIN), the lender must ensure the audio-visual method meets minimum standards including identity verification via either (a) review of two forms of government-issued photo ID with signature via two-way audio-video and capture of the IDs, or (b) two-factor identity authentication using at least two of: remote presentation of a government-issued photo ID with signature, credential analysis, or identity-proofing (knowledge-based authentication). The system must be able to record the audio-video portion and retain that recording for the minimum period required by applicable law, or if none is specified, for 10 years; the lender must retain for the greater of 10 years or the required legal period. Loans closed using RIN must be delivered with SFC 920.
business_rules.conditions: When closing and delivering a loan that used audio-visual aided remote ink-signed notarization
business_rules.consequences: If standards/retention/SFC missing, loan may be ineligible or documentation-defective
business_rules.exceptions: Retention period follows applicable law if longer than 10 years

source_reference.section_id: A2-4.1-04 (RIN requirements; Minimum Standards; Additional Requirements)
source_reference.source_text: All loans delivered to Fannie Mae that have been closed using audio-visual aided ink-signed notarization must be delivered with special feature code (SFC) 920 in addition to any other special feature codes that may apply. ... It must include a review of two forms of government-issued photo ID that have a signature via two-way audio-video technology and capture of such photo ID[s] via a photocopy or other electronic image; or two-factor identity authentication... Recording capability It must have the ability to record... with storage for the minimum period required by applicable law, or if no period is speciﬁed... for 10 years. ... The lender must maintain... the recording... for the greater of 10 years or the minimum period required by applicable law.

examples: RIN closing in a state requiring 5-year retention: lender retains recording 10 years and delivers with SFC 920; compliant.
RIN closing where lender reviewed only one government ID and did not capture an image: noncompliant identity standard.
State law requires 12-year retention: lender retains recording 12 years (greater than 10) and includes SFC 920; compliant.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection / repurchase for legal enforceability defect

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: MERS MIN must be registered before delivery and on note
business_rules.description: For all mortgages delivered to Fannie Mae (when applicable), the original note must be endorsed “in blank” and without recourse with no break in the chain of endorsements. For MERS registered loans, the MIN must be added to the upper right-hand corner of the note and must be part of the electronic data delivery, and the MIN must be registered prior to delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Loans delivered to Fannie Mae requiring custodial documents; MERS loans
business_rules.consequences: If note endorsement chain/MIN requirements fail, delivery may be suspended/cured
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Note The original note endorsed “in blank” and without recourse and there is no break in the chain of endorsements. For MERS registered loans, the MIN should be added to the upper right-hand corner of the note and must be part of the electronic data delivery. The MIN must be registered prior to delivery.

examples: MERS loan delivered with MIN printed on the note, MIN registered, and MIN included in electronic delivery—meets requirement.
MERS loan MIN is on the note but not registered prior to delivery—must cure (register) before delivery acceptance.
Non-MERS loan has a missing endorsement creating a break in chain—custodian rejects package until corrected.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit custodial document requirement; includes a clear pre-delivery registration condition for MIN.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery suspense; potential repurchase demand if not cured

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_030_008 requires the original note be endorsed “in blank” and without recourse with no break in the chain of endorsements. BR_039_007 defines the required ownership/payee/endorsement chain mechanics (original payee, endorsements to subsequent owners, seller endorsement in blank). In practice, BR_039_007 supplies the detailed chain logic needed to satisfy BR_030_008’s ‘no break in chain’ expectation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_039_007 is not satisfied (e.g., originating lender not original payee, missing intermediate endorsements), BR_030_008 cannot be met in substance even if the final endorsement appears “in blank.” Delivery may be suspended pending cure; enforceability risk increases; custodian may reject collateral.

dependencies.rationale: BR_030_008 requires an unbroken endorsement chain and final delivery-ready endorsement status. When endorsements are executed using an allonge, the allonge becomes part of the legal endorsement instrument. A noncompliant allonge breaks enforceability and therefore breaks the endorsement-chain integrity required by BR_030_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_039_008 fails, BR_030_008 fails (delivery suspended/cure required). Potential outcomes include custodian rejection, delayed purchase, or repurchase/indemnification risk if undetected.

business_rules.rule_name: Note chain of endorsements must end with seller blank endorsement without recourse
business_rules.description: The originating lender must be the original payee on the note. The note must be endorsed to each subsequent owner of the mortgage unless one or more owners endorsed the note in blank. The last endorsement on the note should be that of the mortgage seller, and the mortgage seller must endorse the note in blank and without recourse.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to the promissory note included in the closing/delivery file for Fannie Mae loans
business_rules.consequences: If endorsement chain is defective: enforceability/delivery defect and remedy exposure
business_rules.exceptions: Subsequent endorsements not required when note was endorsed in blank earlier

source_reference.source_text: The originating lender must be the original payee on the note, even when MERS is named as nominee for the beneﬁciary in the security instrument. The note must be endorsed to each subsequent owner of the mortgage unless one or more of the owners endorsed the note in blank. The last endorsement on the note should be that of the mortgage seller. The mortgage seller must endorse the note in blank and without recourse.

examples: Originator is payee, then endorsed to aggregator, then seller endorses in blank without recourse: acceptable chain for delivery.
Note endorsed only to aggregator and not further to seller/blank: endorsement chain defect requiring correction before/after delivery.
If an intermediate owner endorsed in blank, later endorsements are not required (but seller still should be last endorsement).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection or repurchase demand

examples: Mortgage file retained with appraisal, DU findings, and closing package
Mortgage file requested for examination during Fannie Mae review
Mortgage file missing required exhibit leading to defect findings

business_rules.rule_name: UAD Appendix D Required for Forms 1004 and 1073
business_rules.description: For conventional loans, Fannie Mae requires specified appraisal report forms to be completed using Appendix D of the Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) Specification when reporting appraisal results. The required forms are: Form 1004, Form 1004 Desktop, Form 1004 Hybrid, Form 1073, and Form 1073 Hybrid.
business_rules.conditions: When reporting appraisal results for a conventional loan using listed forms
business_rules.consequences: If met: appraisal is UAD-compliant; if violated: UCDP/loan delivery defects likely
business_rules.exceptions: Other forms may use UAD to the extent applicable; not mandated for all forms

source_reference.source_text: Fannie Mae requires that the following appraisal report forms are completed utilizing Appendix D of the UAD Speciﬁcation when reporting the results of an appraisal for a conventional loan: Uniform Residential Appraisal Report ( Form 1004 ), Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Desktop) (Form 1004 Desktop ) Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Hybrid) (Form 1004 Hybrid ) Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report ( Form 1073 ), and Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report (Hybrid) (Form 1073 Hybrid ).

examples: Conventional purchase uses Form 1004; lender/appraiser completes UAD Appendix D fields as required.
Conventional condo appraisal uses Form 1073 Hybrid; report must follow UAD Appendix D for applicable fields.
Lender delivers a Form 1004 Desktop without UAD Appendix D formatting; UCDP edits/flags increase delivery risk.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection or post-purchase remedy exposure

examples: Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (URAR) with UAD-compliant fields
Appraisal report reused within permitted age and use requirements
Appraisal report flagged for unacceptable practices or missing exhibits

business_rules.rule_name: Manufactured Home Delivery Requires Special Feature Codes 235 791 859
business_rules.description: For manufactured home deliveries, the lender must deliver specific Special Feature Code(s) based on manufactured home type and width: Single-width Manufactured—codes 235 and 791; Multi-width—code 235; MH Advantage MultiWide—codes 235 and 859; MH Advantage SingleWide—codes 235, 791, and 859. These codes are in addition to any other special feature codes that apply.
business_rules.conditions: Loan secured by a manufactured home is being delivered and requires SFC identification
business_rules.consequences: If codes are delivered correctly, loan data is complete; if missing/incorrect, delivery defects may result
business_rules.exceptions: Codes listed are additive; other applicable SFCs may also be required

source_reference.source_text: Manufactured Home Type... Special Feature Code(s) Single-width Manufactured SingleWide 235 and 791 Multi-width MultiWide 235 MH Advantage MultiWide 235 and 859 MH Advantage SingleWide 235, 791, and 859 Note: These special feature codes are in addition to any other special feature codes that may apply.

examples: Single-wide manufactured home (not MH Advantage): lender delivers SFC 235 and 791 in delivery data.
MH Advantage multi-wide: lender delivers 235 and 859; omitting 859 would be a delivery data defect.
MH Advantage single-wide: lender delivers 235, 791, and 859 plus any additional SFCs applicable to the transaction.
Doublewide manufactured home on a permanent foundation
Manufactured home with HUD label verification
Manufactured housing loan requiring specific eligibility checks

dependencies.rationale: BR_015_008 requires delivering specific Special Feature Codes depending on whether the home is single-width or multi-width (and MH Advantage variants). BR_019_009 sets a minimum width requirement (≥12 feet) and is part of collateral eligibility evaluation. Determining and documenting width/type classification is needed to accurately assign SFCs.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_019_009 assessment (and underlying width determination) is wrong or missing, the lender may deliver incorrect SFCs, creating delivery data defects and potentially mispricing/misclassification.

dependencies.rationale: BR_015_008 applies to "deliveries" of manufactured home loans. BR_019_008 states that without HUD Data Plate/Certification Label evidence (or alternatives), the loan is not eligible for sale to Fannie Mae. Practically, eligibility confirmation (including HUD evidence) precedes or gates delivery data completion tasks like SFC population.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_019_008 fails, resources spent perfecting SFC delivery data are wasted because the loan cannot be delivered; if discovered late, it can cause pipeline fallout and investor delivery failure.

business_rules.rule_name: Gift of equity requires 2 documents: gift letter and settlement statement
business_rules.description: The lender must retain in the loan file (1) a signed gift letter and (2) the settlement statement listing the gift of equity. Both documents must be retained to evidence the gift and its application as a credit in the transaction.
business_rules.conditions: Applies whenever a gift of equity is used in the mortgage transaction
business_rules.consequences: Missing documents create file defects and may trigger repurchase/indemnification
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Documentation Requirements The following documents must be retained in the loan ﬁle: a signed gift letter (see B3-4.3-04, Personal Gifts ), and the settlement statement listing the gift of equity.

examples: Loan file contains signed gift letter but no settlement statement showing the equity credit; lender must obtain and retain the settlement statement to cure.
Settlement statement shows seller credit labeled as gift of equity, but no signed gift letter is retained; lender must obtain a signed gift letter to cure.
Both documents are imaged and indexed to the Fannie Mae loan number in the mortgage file; documentation requirement satisfied.
Seller-parent provides equity gift via below-market sales price
Gift of equity used to satisfy down payment requirement
Equity gift documented in purchase contract and appraisal

business_rules.rule_name: Hybrid appraisal report must enter required assignment fields including YYYY-MM-DD
business_rules.description: For a hybrid appraisal, the appraiser must provide required entries in the appraisal report’s Additional Comments section: Appraisal Assignment Type must be entered as "Hybrid"; Subject Property Data Collection Method must be "Physical"; Subject Property Data Collection Date must be entered in "YYYY-MM-DD" format (the date data collection was performed); and Subject Property Data Workforce must be one of: "Appraiser", "AppraiserTrainee", "RealEstateAgent", "HomeInspector", "InsInspector", or "Other".
business_rules.conditions: Applies when appraisal is completed as a hybrid appraisal on Form 1004 Hybrid or Form 1073 Hybrid
business_rules.consequences: If completed, appraisal meets required reporting fields; if missing/invalid, appraisal may be noncompliant and require correction or resubmission
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: B4-1.2-03, Entry of Appraisal Assignment Type
source_reference.source_text: Entry of Appraisal Assignment Type: The appraiser must provide the Appraisal Assignment Type and supporting details (located in the Additional Comments section of the appraisal report) as shown in the following table. Field Labels Required Entry Appraisal Assignment Type "Hybrid" Subject Property Data Collection Method "Physical" Subject Property Data Collection Date "YYYY-MM-DD" Subject Property Data Workforce "Appraiser" "AppraiserTrainee" "RealEstateAgent" "HomeInspector" "InsInspector" (*Insurance Inspector) "Other"

examples: Hybrid appraisal includes: Assignment Type “Hybrid”, Method “Physical”, Date “2026-01-15”, Workforce “HomeInspector” → compliant.
Hybrid appraisal enters data collection date as “01/15/2026” instead of “YYYY-MM-DD” → noncompliant format; must be corrected.
Hybrid appraisal uses Workforce value “Inspector” (not in allowed list) → invalid; must be replaced with an allowed enumeration (e.g., “HomeInspector”).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery delay; potential repurchase demand

examples: Hybrid appraisal with separate property data collector and appraiser analysis
Hybrid appraisal delivered with required data collection documentation
Hybrid appraisal declined due to insufficient data collection scope

dependencies.rationale: BR_031_004 requires specific appraisal forms (including Form 1004 Hybrid and Form 1073 Hybrid) be completed using Appendix D of the UAD Specification. BR_027_008 applies specifically to hybrid appraisals on those forms and requires specific field entries. In practice, the report must first be in the correct UAD form/spec context for the hybrid-required entries to be valid and accepted by downstream systems (e.g., UCDP).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_031_004 is not met (wrong form/spec), BR_027_008 entries may be rejected by UCDP or be nonstandard, causing appraisal submission failures, delivery defects, and rework delays.

dependencies.rationale: BR_023_009 applies to "all hybrid appraisals" and mandates ANSI Z765-2021 measurement for above-/below-grade reporting. BR_027_008 requires specific hybrid appraisal report fields. They don’t strictly require each other, but together they form the compliance completeness of a hybrid appraisal deliverable (content + measurement methodology).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_023_009 fails, the hybrid appraisal may be defective even if all BR_027_008 fields are populated. This can trigger appraisal revision/replacement, delaying underwriting/closing and risking delivery defects.

business_rules.rule_name: ARM conversion disclosure fee cap $250 or $100
business_rules.description: If an ARM includes a conversion option to a fixed-rate loan, the lender’s conversion disclosures must include: (i) the instances when the conversion option may be exercised, (ii) the time frame within which conversion requests must be received, (iii) the time frame within which the borrower must return executed conversion documents, (iv) any processing fees. Fee cap: Fannie Mae allows a $250 fee for ARM plans that have a monthly conversion option and a $100 fee for other ARM plans.
business_rules.conditions: ARM includes a conversion option to a fixed-rate mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If met, disclosures/fees are compliant; if violated, delivery defect risk
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Requirement: Conversion Disclosures Must Include The instances when the conversion option may be exercised. The time frame within which conversion requests must be received. The time frame within which the borrower must return executed conversion documents. Any fees that will be charged for processing the conversion. Note : Fannie Mae allows a $250 fee for ARM plans that have a monthly conversion option and a $100 fee for other ARM plans.

examples: 7/1 ARM with monthly conversion option discloses a $250 conversion processing fee (allowed).
10/6 ARM with a non-monthly conversion option discloses a $100 processing fee (allowed).
5/1 ARM discloses a $400 conversion processing fee; this exceeds the allowed cap and is a defect.
5/6 ARM with periodic and lifetime caps defined
Convertible ARM with conversion feature per product terms
ARM with temporary interest rate buydown applied at origination

business_rules.rule_name: Post-closing credit changes require updated application kept in file
business_rules.description: When a lender requests a new credit report after closing for a DU resubmission, and the new report contains information different from the information used to prepare the final loan application signed at closing, the loan application must be updated. Borrower signatures are not required due to the post-closing update. The lender must include both the final signed and the updated unsigned loan applications in the loan file.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a new post-closing credit report is obtained for DU resubmission.
business_rules.consequences: If not updated/retained, the file is incomplete and may be subject to delivery defects or remedies.
business_rules.exceptions: If new report contains no differences vs closing application data, update not triggered.

source_reference.source_text:
If the new credit report contains information that is diﬀerent than the information used to prepare the
ﬁnal loan application that was signed by the borrower at closing, the loan application must be updated.
(Borrower signature(s) are not required due to the update occurring post-closing.) The lender must include both
the ﬁnal signed and the updated unsigned loan applications in the loan ﬁle.

examples: Post-closing credit refresh adds a new auto loan not on the closing application: update URLA and retain both signed-at-closing and updated unsigned versions.
Post-closing credit refresh shows same tradelines and balances as closing: no application update required under this trigger.
Post-closing credit refresh shows a paid-off debt but lender fails to update the application and only keeps the signed copy: documentation defect.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Direct documentation retention requirement tied to post-closing credit refresh differences.

examples: DU evaluates an application and generates an Approve/Eligible recommendation.
DU evaluates an application and returns Refer with Caution requiring manual underwriting.

dependencies.rationale: BR_006_007 applies "when a lender requests a new credit report after closing for a DU resubmission." BR_032_010 mandates resubmission when specified DTI triggers occur due to changes. In many cases, resubmission requires updated credit inputs, which triggers BR_006_007’s application update/retention requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_032_010-triggered resubmission occurs with a new credit report but BR_006_007 is not followed, the loan file lacks required documentation (final signed + updated unsigned application), creating delivery defects and post-purchase exposure.

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_003 mandates DU resubmission when discrepancies exceed tolerances. If that resubmission requires pulling a new credit report after closing (explicit trigger in BR_006_007), the lender must update and retain both the signed final and updated unsigned applications.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Failure to follow BR_006_007 after a QC-driven new credit report causes a documentation defect, even if the underwriting reassessment was done. This can lead to delivery defects and post-purchase findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Servicing transfer forms required under 11095.4
business_rules.description: Seller must comply with the Servicing Transfer Instructions (as amended) and must complete all required forms contained in, and required by, the Servicing Transfer Instructions. Seller must return required information and items to the designated recipient specified in those instructions.
business_rules.conditions: When servicing is transferred under Servicing Transfer Instructions referenced by the agreement
business_rules.consequences: If complied, transfer package is complete; if not, Seller is in breach of agreement obligations
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 11095.4 Compliance with Requirements. Seller shall comply with the Servicing Transfer Instructions, as amended from time to time; and Seller shall complete any and all required forms contained in and as required by the Servicing Transfer Instructions and return such information and items to the party designated therein to receive such information and items.

examples: Seller receives updated Servicing Transfer Instructions and uses the updated forms (not prior versions) for the transfer package.
Seller completes all required transfer checklists and submits them to the party designated to receive them; missing forms trigger corrective requests.
Seller returns required items (e.g., specified transfer data files) to the designated recipient rather than an incorrect counterparty.
Contract requires record retention and examination of mortgage files for all delivered loans.
Contract defines remedies available to Fannie Mae for material defects.

business_rules.rule_name: Condo Requires ALTA 4.1 and PUD Requires ALTA 5.1 Endorsements
business_rules.description: When the mortgage loan is secured by a condo or PUD unit, the title insurance policy must describe all components of the unit estate and must include the appropriate ALTA endorsement: for condo units, ALTA 4 or 4.1 (or equivalent); for PUD units, ALTA 5 or 5.1 (or equivalent). The endorsement must be attached to the policy or incorporated into the text of the policy.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when collateral is a condo unit or a PUD unit securing the mortgage loan.
business_rules.consequences: If met: title policy meets condo/PUD unit endorsement requirement. If violated: cure before delivery or delivery risk.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.section_id: B7-2-04, Condo and PUD Unit Mortgages
source_reference.source_text: Condo and PUD Unit Mortgages The title insurance policy for a condo or PUD unit mortgage must describe all components of the unit estate. For condo units, an ALTA 4 or 4.1 endorsement or its equivalent is required. For PUD units, an ALTA 5 or 5.1 endorsement or its equivalent is required. These endorsements must be attached to each policy or incorporated in the text of the policy.

examples: Loan secured by a condo unit: title policy includes ALTA 4.1 endorsement in the policy jacket; compliant.
Loan secured by a PUD unit: policy is issued without ALTA 5/5.1 endorsement; lender must add the endorsement before delivery.
State-equivalent endorsement is used instead of ALTA 4.1; acceptable if it is equivalent and included in/attached to the policy.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan delivery rejection / corrective endorsement required

examples: Purchase loan secured by a one-unit primary residence.
Refinance loan secured by a property in a disaster-affected area requiring additional review.

business_rules.rule_name: Significant defect or warranty breach can trigger immediate repurchase or make-whole payment
business_rules.description: If Fannie Mae determines through review (or otherwise learns) that a mortgage loan did not meet requirements due to violation of the Lender Contract, or if the remedies framework applies and a significant defect is identified, Fannie Mae may require the lender to immediately repurchase the mortgage loan/acquired property (or Fannie Mae participation interest) or remit a make whole payment if the property has been liquidated. Fannie Mae may also require repurchase/make whole if any selling lender warranty is untrue and, if the remedies framework applies, qualifies as a significant defect, whether or not the lender had actual knowledge—except that no request will be made when the warranty specifically requires actual knowledge and the lender has no such knowledge.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when QC/loan review identifies contract violation, warranty breach, or significant defect
business_rules.consequences: If triggered, lender must repurchase immediately or remit make-whole payment
business_rules.exceptions: No request if warranty requires actual knowledge and lender had no knowledge

source_reference.source_text: If Fannie Mae's loan review determines (or Fannie Mae otherwise learns) that a mortgage loan did not meet Fannie Mae requirements due to violation of the Lender Contract or, if the “remedies framework” applies and a “signiﬁcant defect” is identiﬁed, Fannie Mae may require the lender to immediately repurchase the mortgage

examples: Post-purchase review discovers misrepresented occupancy that qualifies as a significant defect under the remedies framework; Fannie Mae issues an immediate repurchase demand.
A life-of-loan warranty is untrue; even if the lender lacked actual knowledge, Fannie Mae may require repurchase if it qualifies as a significant defect.
A warranty clause states a breach exists only with actual knowledge; lender proves it had no actual knowledge, so no repurchase request is made under that clause.
Fannie Mae issues repurchase request due to ineligible loan attribute discovered in QC.
Fannie Mae issues make-whole request based on appraisal-related defect findings.

dependencies.rationale: BR_ISSUES_REPURCHASE_REQUEST_COMPLIANCE_008_012 establishes that Fannie Mae may require the lender to immediately repurchase or make-whole upon significant defects. BR_ISSUES_REPURCHASE_REQUEST_PROCESS_017_010 provides an exception/deferral when the demand relates to compliance-with-laws issues under pending litigation or active agency review, stating the Seller/Servicer is not required to repurchase until 30 days after resolution. Therefore, repurchase timing logic must check the override conditions to avoid enforcing an incorrect immediate repurchase deadline.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the override is not applied, the lender may unnecessarily repurchase early (liquidity and operational burden) or mis-handle the demand response process, potentially undermining legal strategy. Conversely, applying deferral when not allowed can cause breach/escalation for non-performance.

business_rules.rule_name: Comparable Concessions Must Be Reported or Explained with Addendum
